The issue is compounded when non-default settings survive SQL Server version upgrades, causing unexpected behavior regression in newer environments that expect standard default configurations.

This check queries sys.configurations WHERE value_in_use indicates a non-default state for the remote login timeout option. A PASS verdict means the setting is at its documented default. INFO means the setting has been changed and should be reviewed against your organization's approved configuration baseline.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Detect Non-Default Value for remote login timeout' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Detect Non-Default Value for remote login timeout' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Backups happening in the last 7 days
Description: This check identifies backups happening in the last 7 days. This check identifies backups happening in the last 7 days. Checks if any database hasn't had a full backup in the last 7 days
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
       WHEN EXISTS (
               SELECT 1 AS CheckID ,
  d.[name] AS DatabaseName ,
  1 AS Priority ,
  'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Backups Not Performed Recently' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/nobak' AS URL ,
  'Last backed up: '
  + COALESCE(CAST(MAX(b.backup_finish_date) AS VARCHAR(25)),'never') AS Details
FROM master.sys.databases d
  LEFT OUTER JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset b ON d.name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = b.database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
     AND b.type = 'D'
     AND b.server_name = SERVERPROPERTY('ServerName') /*Backupset ran on current server */
WHERE d.database_id <> 2 /* Bonus points if you know what that means */
  AND d.state NOT IN(1, 6, 10) /* Not currently offline or restoring, like log shipping databases */
  AND d.is_in_standby = 0 /* Not a log shipping target database */
  AND d.source_database_id IS NULL /* Excludes database snapshots */
  -- AND d.name NOT IN ( SELECT DISTINCT
     -- DatabaseName
    --FROM #SkipChecks
  --  WHERE CheckID IS NULL OR CheckID = 1)
  /*
  The above NOT IN filters out the databases we're not supposed to check.
  */
GROUP BY d.name
HAVING MAX(b.backup_finish_date) <= DATEADD(dd,
     -7, GETDATE())
  OR MAX(b.backup_finish_date) IS NULL
       ) THEN 1
       ELSE 0
      END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Backups happening in the last 7 days' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Backups happening in the last 7 days' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Transaction Log Backup taking place
Description: Checks if any database in FULL recovery hasn't had a log backup in 2 hours
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
       WHEN EXISTS (
        SELECT DISTINCT
 2 AS CheckID ,
 d.name AS DatabaseName ,
 1 AS Priority ,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Full Recovery Model w/o Log Backups' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/biglogs' AS URL ,
 ( 'The ' + CAST(CAST((SELECT ((SUM([mf].[size]) * 8.) / 1024.) FROM sys.[master_files] AS [mf] WHERE [mf].[database_id] = d.[database_id] AND [mf].[type_desc] = 'LOG') AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS VARCHAR(30)) + 'MB log file has not been backed up in the last week.' ) AS Details
FROM master.sys.databases d
--LEFT JOIN #DBCCs ll On ll.DbName = d.name And ll.Field = 'dbi_LastLogBackupTime'
WHERE d.recovery_model IN ( 1, 2 )
 AND d.database_id NOT IN ( 2, 3 )
 AND d.source_database_id IS NULL
 AND d.state NOT IN(1, 6, 10) /* Not currently offline or restoring, like log shipping databases */
 AND d.is_in_standby = 0 /* Not a log shipping target database */
 AND d.source_database_id IS NULL /* Excludes database snapshots */
 AND (
 (
 /* We couldn't get a value from the DBCC DBINFO data so let's check the msdb backup history information */
 --[ll].[Value] Is Null
 --AND 
 NOT EXISTS ( SELECT *
  FROM msdb.dbo.backupset b
  WHERE d.name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = b.database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
   AND b.type = 'L'
   AND b.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(dd,-7, GETDATE())
 )
 )
 
)
       ) THEN 1
       ELSE 0
      END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Transaction Log Backup taking place' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Transaction Log Backup taking place' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: MSDB Backup History Not Purged
Description: Checks for old backup history in the MSDB database. Not purging this history can lead to a full C drive, slow MSDB access, and inefficient monitoring tools.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT TOP 1
 3 AS CheckID ,
 'msdb' ,
 200 AS Priority ,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'MSDB Backup History Not Purged' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/history' AS URL ,
 ( 'Database backup history retained back to '
  + CAST(bs.backup_start_date AS VARCHAR(20)) ) AS Details
 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs
 LEFT OUTER JOIN msdb.dbo.restorehistory rh ON bs.database_name = rh.destination_database_name
 WHERE rh.destination_database_name IS NULL
 ORDER BY bs.backup_start_date ASC

 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'MSDB Backup History Not Purged' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'MSDB Backup History Not Purged' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Sysadmins on SQL server
Description: Lists every login that holds the sysadmin server role. Sysadmin grants full control of the instance - drop databases, read any data, disable auditing, hide tracks. Every extra sysadmin is one more account whose compromise becomes a total compromise.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT 4 AS CheckID ,
 230 AS Priority ,
 'Security' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Sysadmins' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/sa' AS URL ,
 ( 'Login [' + l.name
 + '] is a sysadmin - meaning they can do absolutely anything in SQL Server, including dropping databases or hiding their tracks.' ) AS Details
FROM master.sys.syslogins l
WHERE l.sysadmin = 1
 AND l.name <> SUSER_SNAME(0x01)
 AND l.denylogin = 0
 AND l.name NOT LIKE 'NT SERVICE\%'
 AND l.name <> 'l_certSignSmDetach' /* Added in SQL 2016 */
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Inventory sysadmin membership. Drop accounts without a named owner using "ALTER SERVER ROLE sysadmin DROP MEMBER". Move application accounts off sysadmin onto custom roles with the minimum permissions they actually use.
ExpectedResult: Returns 0 when only the expected DBA accounts hold sysadmin. Returns 1 when any non-DBA, non-system principal is sysadmin. Review the list of returned logins against your documented sysadmin inventory.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Define and Maintain Role-Based Access Control
Access assigned based on job classification and function

Name: Security Admins on SQL server
Description: Identifies users with security admin privileges in SQL Server, which can pose a security risk if not properly managed.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT 5 AS CheckID ,
 230 AS Priority ,
 'Security' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Security Admins' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/sa' AS URL ,
 ( 'Login [' + l.name
 + '] is a security admin - meaning they can give themselves permission to do absolutely anything in SQL Server, including dropping databases or hiding their tracks.' ) AS Details
FROM master.sys.syslogins l
WHERE l.securityadmin = 1
 AND l.name <> SUSER_SNAME(0x01)
 AND l.denylogin = 0
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Security Admins on SQL server' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Security Admins on SQL server' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Jobs owned by users
Description: Checks for SQL Server Agent jobs owned by user accounts, which can cause jobs to fail if the user account is dropped or disabled.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 6 AS CheckID ,
 230 AS Priority ,
 'Security' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Jobs Owned By Users' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/owners' AS URL ,
 ( 'Job [' + j.name + '] is owned by ['
 + SUSER_SNAME(j.owner_sid)
 + '] - meaning if their login is disabled or not available due to Active Directory problems, the job will stop working.' ) AS Details
FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j
WHERE j.enabled = 1
 AND SUSER_SNAME(j.owner_sid) <> SUSER_SNAME(0x01)
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Jobs owned by users' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Jobs owned by users' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Stored Procedures running at startup
Description: Checks for stored procedures that are configured to run automatically when SQL Server starts. These procedures can be a security risk if their purpose is unclear.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 7 AS CheckID ,
 230 AS Priority ,
 'Security' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Stored Procedure Runs at Startup' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/startup' AS URL ,
 ( 'Stored procedure [master].['
 + r.SPECIFIC_SCHEMA + '].['
 + r.SPECIFIC_NAME
 + '] runs automatically when SQL Server starts up. Make sure you know exactly what this stored procedure is doing, because it could pose a security risk.' ) AS Details
FROM master.INFORMATION_SCHEMA.ROUTINES r
WHERE OBJECTPROPERTY(OBJECT_ID(ROUTINE_NAME),
  'ExecIsStartup') = 1
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Stored Procedures running at startup' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Stored Procedures running at startup' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Server Audits running
Description: Reports any SQL Server Audit specification currently running on the instance. This is informational - audits enabled is generally a good sign. Worth knowing what is being captured, where it writes, and whether the audit target has space to keep writing.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
      SELECT 8 AS CheckID,
  230 AS Priority,
  'Security' AS FindingsGroup,
  'Server Audits Running' AS Finding,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/audits' AS URL,
  ('SQL Server built-in audit functionality is being used by server audit: ' + [name]) AS Details 
  FROM sys.dm_server_audit_status --OPTION (RECOMPILE)
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: For each running audit, record its name, target, filter, and review owner. Confirm the target volume has space and rotation. If an audit is running without a documented review process, either start the review or stop the audit.
ExpectedResult: Returns 1 when at least one server audit is in a running state, 0 otherwise. A returned 1 is informational, not a failure. Review the captured audits against compliance scope and operational expectations.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
Audit logs implemented to support detection of anomalies

Name: Resource Governor Enabled
Description: Reports whether Resource Governor is enabled on the instance. Resource Governor classifies sessions and caps their CPU, memory, and IOPS. Powerful, but a misconfigured classifier function can throttle legitimate workload and look like a hardware problem.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 10 AS CheckID,
  100 AS Priority,
  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
  'Resource Governor Enabled' AS Finding,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/rg' AS URL,
  ('Resource Governor is enabled. Queries may be throttled. Make sure you understand how the Classifier Function is configured.') AS Details FROM sys.resource_governor_configuration WHERE is_enabled = 1 
  
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Capture the current pool and classifier configuration. If Resource Governor is enabled without a documented purpose, disable it. If it is in use, confirm the classifier is fast and the pool limits match the workload profile.
ExpectedResult: Returns 1 when sys.resource_governor_configuration.is_enabled = 1, 0 otherwise. A returned 1 is informational - investigate whether the configuration is intentional and documented.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process

Name: Server triggers enabled
Description: Checks for enabled server-level triggers. These triggers can impact performance and should be reviewed.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 11 AS CheckID,
  100 AS Priority,
  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
  'Server Triggers Enabled' AS Finding,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/logontriggers/' AS URL,
  ('Server Trigger [' + [name] ++ '] is enabled. Make sure you understand what that trigger is doing - the less work it does, the better.') AS Details
  FROM sys.server_triggers
  WHERE is_disabled = 0 AND is_ms_shipped = 0 AND name NOT LIKE 'rds^_%' ESCAPE '^'
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Server triggers enabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Server triggers enabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Database Auto-Close enabled
Description: Auto-Close is enabled on one or more user databases. This causes SQL Server to shut down the database and release resources when all connections close. Re-opening the database incurs significant overhead and can cause performance degradation on subsequent connections.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 12 AS CheckID ,
 [name] AS DatabaseName ,
 10 AS Priority ,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Auto-Close Enabled' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/autoclose' AS URL ,
 ( 'Database [' + [name]
 + '] has auto-close enabled. This setting can dramatically decrease performance.' ) AS Details
FROM sys.databases
WHERE is_auto_close_on = 1
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Database Auto-Close enabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Database Auto-Close enabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Database Auto-Shrink enabled
Description: Auto-Shrink is enabled on one or more databases. This causes SQL Server to periodically shrink data and log files, leading to index fragmentation, I/O overhead, and repeated file growth/shrink cycles that hurt performance.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 13 AS CheckID ,
 [name] AS DatabaseName ,
 10 AS Priority ,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Auto-Shrink Enabled' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/autoshrink' AS URL ,
 ( 'Database [' + [name]
 + '] has auto-shrink enabled. This setting can dramatically decrease performance.' ) AS Details
FROM sys.databases
WHERE is_auto_shrink_on = 1
 AND state <> 6 /* Offline */
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Database Auto-Shrink enabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Database Auto-Shrink enabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Corruption checks not optimal
Description: One or more databases do not have PAGE_VERIFY set to CHECKSUM. CHECKSUM page verification allows SQL Server to detect I/O corruption and data integrity issues. Databases using TORN_PAGE_DETECTION or NONE provide weaker integrity guarantees.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

 SELECT 14 AS CheckID,
 [name] as DatabaseName,
 50 AS Priority,
 'Reliability' AS FindingsGroup,
 'Page Verification Not Optimal' AS Finding,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/torn' AS URL,
 ('Database [' + [name] + '] has ' + [page_verify_option_desc] + ' for page verification. SQL Server may have a harder time recognizing and recovering from storage corruption. Consider using CHECKSUM instead.') COLLATE database_default AS Details
 FROM sys.databases
 WHERE page_verify_option < 2
 AND name <> 'tempdb'
 AND state NOT IN (1, 6) /* Restoring, Offline */
  
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Corruption checks not optimal' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Corruption checks not optimal' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Auto-Create Statistics Disabled
Description: Auto-Create Statistics is disabled on one or more databases. SQL Server relies on statistics to build efficient query plans. When disabled, the query optimizer lacks accurate data distribution information, leading to poor plan choices and degraded query performance.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  15 AS CheckID ,
          [name] AS DatabaseName ,
          110 AS Priority ,
          'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
          'Auto-Create Stats Disabled' AS Finding ,
          'https://www.brentozar.com/go/acs' AS URL ,
          ( 'Database [' + [name]
            + '] has auto-create-stats disabled.  SQL Server uses statistics to build better execution plans, and without the ability to automatically create more, performance may suffer.' ) AS Details
        FROM    sys.databases
        WHERE   is_auto_create_stats_on = 0
  
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Auto-Create Statistics Disabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Auto-Create Statistics Disabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Auto-Update Stats Disabled
Description: Auto-Update Statistics is disabled on one or more databases. When statistics become stale due to data changes, the query optimizer continues using outdated information, which can lead to suboptimal query execution plans and poor performance.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 16 AS CheckID ,
 [name] AS DatabaseName ,
 110 AS Priority ,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Auto-Update Stats Disabled' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/aus' AS URL ,
 ( 'Database [' + [name]
 + '] has auto-update-stats disabled. SQL Server uses statistics to build better execution plans, and without the ability to automatically update them, performance may suffer.' ) AS Details
FROM sys.databases
WHERE is_auto_update_stats_on = 0  
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Auto-Update Stats Disabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Auto-Update Stats Disabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Stats Updated Asynchronously
Description: Auto-Update Statistics Asynchronously is enabled on one or more databases. Queries may use stale statistics for the current execution while an update runs in the background, potentially causing poor plan choices until the update completes.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 17 AS CheckID ,
 [name] AS DatabaseName ,
 150 AS Priority ,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Stats Updated Asynchronously' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/asyncstats' AS URL ,
 ( 'Database [' + [name]
 + '] has auto-update-stats-async enabled. When SQL Server gets a query for a table with out-of-date statistics, it will run the query with the stats it has - while updating stats to make later queries better. The initial run of the query may suffer, though.' ) AS Details
FROM sys.databases
WHERE is_auto_update_stats_async_on = 1 
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Stats Updated Asynchronously' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Stats Updated Asynchronously' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Replication in use
Description: One or more databases are configured for replication (transactional, merge, or snapshot). Replication adds overhead to DML operations through replication triggers and log reader activity. This is informational to note its presence and associated performance implications.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (             
SELECT  19 AS CheckID ,
        [name] AS DatabaseName ,
        200 AS Priority ,
        'Informational' AS FindingsGroup ,
        'Replication In Use' AS Finding ,
        'https://www.brentozar.com/go/repl' AS URL ,
        ( 'Database [' + [name]
          + '] is a replication publisher, subscriber, or distributor.' ) AS Details
FROM    sys.databases
WHERE    (is_published = 1
        OR is_subscribed = 1
        OR is_merge_published = 1
                  OR is_distributor = 1)            
  ) 
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Replication in use' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Replication in use' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Date correlation
Description: Date Correlation Optimization is enabled on one or more databases. This feature is rarely needed and can cause the optimizer to make incorrect assumptions about date-correlated tables, potentially leading to poor query plans.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

 SELECT 20 AS CheckID ,
 [name] AS DatabaseName ,
 200 AS Priority ,
 'Informational' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Date Correlation On' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/corr' AS URL ,
 ( 'Database [' + [name]
  + '] has date correlation enabled. This is not a default setting, and it has some performance overhead. It tells SQL Server that date fields in two tables are related, and SQL Server maintains statistics showing that relation.' ) AS Details
 FROM sys.databases
 WHERE is_date_correlation_on = 1 
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Date correlation' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Date correlation' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Database Encrypted
Description: One or more databases have Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) enabled. TDE encrypts data at rest but introduces CPU overhead for encryption/decryption operations. Encrypted databases also require backup encryption key management.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 21 AS CheckID,
  [name] as DatabaseName,
  200 AS Priority,
  'Informational' AS FindingsGroup,
  'Database Encrypted' AS Finding,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tde' AS URL,
  ('Database [' + [name] + '] has Transparent Data Encryption enabled. Make absolutely sure you have backed up the certificate and private key, or else you will not be able to restore this database.') AS Details
  FROM sys.databases
  WHERE is_encrypted = 1 
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Database Encrypted' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Database Encrypted' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Non-Default Server Configuration Values
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 22' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations
  WHERE value_in_use <> value
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 22' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 22' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: System Database on C Drive
Description: System Database on C Drive - File Configuration (sp_Blitz CheckID 24)
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT DISTINCT
  24 AS CheckID ,
  DB_NAME(database_id) AS DatabaseName ,
  170 AS Priority ,
  'File Configuration' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'System Database on C Drive' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/cdrive' AS URL ,
  ( 'The ' + DB_NAME(database_id)
    + ' database has a file on the C drive.  Putting system databases on the C drive runs the risk of crashing the server when it runs out of space.' ) AS Details
FROM    sys.master_files
WHERE   UPPER(LEFT(physical_name, 1)) = 'C'
  AND DB_NAME(database_id) IN ( 'master',
          'model', 'msdb' )
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'System Database on C Drive' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'System Database on C Drive' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: TempDB on C Drive
Description: Detects when the tempdb database has data or log files located on the C drive. TempDB frequently grows unpredictably, putting your server at risk of running out of C drive space and crashing hard. The C drive is also often much slower than other drives, so performance may be suffering even if space is not immediately a concern.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.master_files
    WHERE UPPER(LEFT(physical_name, 1)) = 'C'
      AND DB_NAME(database_id) = 'tempdb'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Move tempdb data and log files to a separate fast dedicated drive.
Pre-size tempdb files to avoid autogrowth events and use fixed-size growth increments.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when tempdb has no files on the C drive. Returns FAIL when tempdb files reside on C.

Name: User Database on C Drive
Description: One or more user database data or log files are located on the C: (system) drive. Storing user databases on the OS drive risks filling the system partition, which can crash SQL Server and the operating system.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT DISTINCT
  26 AS CheckID ,
  DB_NAME(database_id) AS DatabaseName ,
  20 AS Priority ,
  'Reliability' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'User Databases on C Drive' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/cdrive' AS URL ,
  ( 'The ' + DB_NAME(database_id)
    + ' database has a file on the C drive.  Putting databases on the C drive runs the risk of crashing the server when it runs out of space.' ) AS Details
FROM    sys.master_files
WHERE   UPPER(LEFT(physical_name, 1)) = 'C'
  AND DB_NAME(database_id) NOT IN ( 'master','model', 'msdb', 'tempdb' )
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'User Database on C Drive' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'User Database on C Drive' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Tables in the Master Database
Description: User-created tables were found in the master database. Master should only contain system objects. User tables in master are not backed up by default database backup jobs and can interfere with SQL Server upgrades and maintenance.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  27 AS CheckID ,
  'master' AS DatabaseName ,
  200 AS Priority ,
  'Informational' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Tables in the Master Database' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/mastuser' AS URL ,
  ( 'The ' + name
    + ' table in the master database was created by end users on '
    + CAST(create_date AS VARCHAR(20))
    + '. Tables in the master database may not be restored in the event of a disaster.' ) AS Details
FROM    master.sys.tables
WHERE   is_ms_shipped = 0
  AND   name NOT IN ('CommandLog','SqlServerVersions','$ndo$srvproperty')
  AND name NOT LIKE 'rds^_%' ESCAPE '^'
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Tables in the Master Database' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Tables in the Master Database' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Tables in the MSDB Database
Description: User-created tables were found in the msdb database. MSDB is a system database for SQL Server Agent, backups, and maintenance. User tables in msdb risk data loss during upgrades and may cause maintenance complications.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  28 AS CheckID ,
        'msdb' AS DatabaseName ,
  200 AS Priority ,
  'Informational' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Tables in the MSDB Database' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/msdbuser' AS URL ,
  ( 'The ' + name
    + ' table in the msdb database was created by end users on '
    + CAST(create_date AS VARCHAR(20))
    + '. Tables in the msdb database may not be restored in the event of a disaster.' ) AS Details
FROM    msdb.sys.tables
WHERE   is_ms_shipped = 0 AND name NOT LIKE '%DTA_%'
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Tables in the MSDB Database' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Tables in the MSDB Database' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Tables in the Model Database
Description: User-created tables were found in the model database. Model is the template for all new databases created on the instance. Objects in model are copied to every new database, which is rarely intended and can cause unexpected schema in new databases.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  29 AS CheckID ,
        'model' AS DatabaseName ,
  200 AS Priority ,
  'Informational' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Tables in the Model Database' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/model' AS URL ,
  ( 'The ' + name
    + ' table in the model database was created by end users on '
    + CAST(create_date AS VARCHAR(20))
    + '. Tables in the model database are automatically copied into all new databases.' ) AS Details
FROM    model.sys.tables
WHERE   is_ms_shipped = 0
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Tables in the Model Database' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Tables in the Model Database' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Not All Alerts Configured
Description: SQL Server does not have alerts configured for all critical severity levels (17-25) and error numbers (823, 824, 825). Without complete alert coverage, DBAs may not be notified of serious errors including I/O failures and potential corruption events.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  30 AS CheckID ,
  200 AS Priority ,
  'Monitoring' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Not All Alerts Configured' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/alert' AS URL ,
  ( 'Not all SQL Server Agent alerts have been configured.  This is a free, easy way to get notified of corruption, job failures, or major outages even before monitoring systems pick it up.' ) AS Details
  WHERE (
  SELECT COUNT(1) FROM   msdb.dbo.sysalerts
  WHERE  severity BETWEEN 19 AND 25) < 7
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Not All Alerts Configured' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Not All Alerts Configured' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: No Operators Configured/Enabled
Description: No Operators Configured/Enabled - Monitoring (sp_Blitz CheckID 31)
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  31 AS CheckID ,
 200 AS Priority ,
 'Monitoring' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'No Operators Configured/Enabled' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/op' AS URL ,
 ( 'No SQL Server Agent operators (emails) have been configured.  This is a free, easy way to get notified of corruption, job failures, or major outages even before monitoring systems pick it up.' ) AS Details     WHERE NOT EXISTS ( SELECT  *
FROM    msdb.dbo.sysoperators
 WHERE   enabled = 1 )
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'No Operators Configured/Enabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'No Operators Configured/Enabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Triggers on Tables
Description: Lists user tables that have enabled DML triggers (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE). Triggers fire inside the same transaction as the DML and can silently double write times, hold locks longer, and break inserts when they error. They often hide audit logic, business rules, or replication wiring that the original author has long since left the company.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.triggers WHERE parent_class = 1 AND is_disabled = 0 AND parent_id NOT IN(SELECT object_id FROM sys.objects WHERE type = 'U')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: It looks like one or more user tables carry undocumented DML triggers. Inventory each enabled trigger, capture the definition, and confirm with the application owner whether the logic is still required. Replace audit-only triggers with Change Data Capture or temporal tables where the edition supports them, then disable (reversible) before dropping.
ExpectedResult: PASS when no enabled DML triggers exist on user tables. INFO when one or more enabled DML triggers exist - the check surfaces them for inventory and review rather than failing outright, because triggers are sometimes legitimate.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process

Name: Enterprise Edition Features In Use
Description: Reports any database that uses Enterprise-only features (data compression, partitioning, In-Memory OLTP, Change Data Capture pre-2016 SP1, columnstore segment elimination, online operations, TDE on older versions). The DMV sys.dm_db_persisted_sku_features is what Microsoft uses internally to block backups from restoring onto Standard. A "yes" here means moving the database to a Standard or Web edition instance is blocked until the features are removed.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_db_persisted_sku_features) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: It looks like one or more databases hold persisted Enterprise-only features - moving them to Standard or restoring onto a Standard DR instance is blocked until the features come out. Inventory with SELECT * FROM sys.dm_db_persisted_sku_features in every user database, map each feature against the Microsoft edition matrix for your SQL version, then decide per database - keep the Enterprise license or schedule feature removal.
ExpectedResult: PASS when no user database returns rows from sys.dm_db_persisted_sku_features. INFO when one or more user databases use Enterprise-only features - this is fine if Enterprise is the licensed edition, but the dependency must be documented so a downgrade or DR-to-Standard plan does not fail later. SKIP for SQL Server versions earlier than 2008.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Software Inventory
Risk Identification - Vendor and Business Partners

Name: Database Corruption Detected
Description: Database corruption has been detected via DBCC CHECKDB results stored in msdb or the default trace. Corruption can lead to data loss and application failures. Immediate investigation and restoration from a clean backup may be required.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT DISTINCT
    34 AS CheckID ,
    db.name ,
    1 AS Priority ,
    'Corruption' AS FindingsGroup ,
    'Database Corruption Detected' AS Finding ,
    'https://www.brentozar.com/go/repair' AS URL ,
    ( 'Database mirroring has automatically repaired at least one corrupt page in the last 30 days. For more information, query the DMV sys.dm_db_mirroring_auto_page_repair.' ) AS Details
    FROM (SELECT rp2.database_id, rp2.modification_time
   FROM sys.dm_db_mirroring_auto_page_repair rp2
   WHERE rp2.[database_id] not in (
   SELECT db2.[database_id]
   FROM sys.databases as db2
   WHERE db2.[state] = 1
   ) ) as rp
    INNER JOIN master.sys.databases db ON rp.database_id = db.database_id
    WHERE   rp.modification_time >= DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Database Corruption Detected' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Database Corruption Detected' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Single-Use Plans in Procedure Cache
Description: Checks whether "optimize for ad hoc workloads" is disabled while a large number of single-use ad-hoc plans are sitting in the procedure cache. Each plan compiles once, is cached, and never runs again - wasting memory that SQL Server could use for data pages. It seems like this has never been looked at on most servers. The fix is a single sp_configure change and a cache flush.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS ( 
SELECT  35 AS CheckID ,
   100 AS Priority ,
   'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
   'Single-Use Plans in Procedure Cache' AS Finding ,
   'https://www.brentozar.com/go/single' AS URL ,
   ( CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR(10))
     + ' query plans are taking up memory in the procedure cache. This may be wasted memory if we cache plans for queries that never get called again. This may be a good use case for SQL Server 2008''s Optimize for Ad Hoc or for Forced Parameterization.' ) AS Details
 FROM    sys.dm_exec_cached_plans AS cp
 WHERE   cp.usecounts = 1
   AND cp.objtype = 'Adhoc'
   AND EXISTS ( SELECT
         1
       FROM sys.configurations
       WHERE
         name = 'optimize for ad hoc workloads'
         AND value_in_use = 0 )
 HAVING  COUNT(*) > 1
) 
THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: It looks like "optimize for ad hoc workloads" is off and the plan cache is filling with one-time plans. Run sp_configure ''optimize for ad hoc workloads'', 1 then RECONFIGURE then DBCC FREEPROCCACHE. No restart needed. If the application uses heavy ad-hoc SQL that cannot be parameterised, also evaluate Forced Parameterization at the database level.
ExpectedResult: PASS when "optimize for ad hoc workloads" is already enabled, or no single-use ad-hoc plans exceed the threshold. FAIL when single-use plans are accumulating and the setting is off. SKIP for SQL Server versions earlier than 2008.

Name: Slow Storage Reads on Drive
Description: Detects drives where average read latency exceeds 200ms across databases with significant read activity. At 200ms a read is ten times slower than a healthy SSD and three times slower than a spinning disk performing well. Every query that touches a cold page on that drive stalls. It looks like storage I/O is the bottleneck here - worth investigating before a performance complaint becomes an outage.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL) AS fs
    INNER JOIN sys.master_files AS mf
        ON fs.database_id = mf.database_id
        AND fs.[file_id] = mf.[file_id]
    WHERE (io_stall_read_ms / (1.0 + num_of_reads)) > 200
      AND num_of_reads > 100000
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Storage reads are averaging over 200ms on at least one drive hosting SQL Server data files. Pull sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats to identify which files are slow, then check storage health. If a RAID set is degraded or a controller is queuing I/O, this needs a maintenance window call now - not next sprint.
ExpectedResult: PASS when no database file averages more than 200ms per read over a significant sample (>100000 reads). FAIL when any file exceeds the threshold. SKIP not applicable.

Name: Slow Storage Writes on Drive
Description: Detects drives where average write latency exceeds 100ms. Write I/O is in the critical path for every committed transaction - the log write must complete before the commit returns. At 100ms a server doing 100 commits per second is spending 10 seconds per second waiting on storage. It looks like something in the I/O stack is queuing writes - SAN contention, a degraded RAID set, or a full storage controller queue are all common causes.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

 SELECT DISTINCT
   37 AS CheckID ,
   150 AS Priority ,
   'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
   'Slow Storage Writes on Drive '
   + UPPER(LEFT(mf.physical_name, 1)) AS Finding ,
   'https://www.brentozar.com/go/slow' AS URL ,
   'Writes are averaging longer than 100ms for at least one database on this drive.  For specific database file speeds, run the query from the information link.' AS Details
 FROM    sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(NULL, NULL)
   AS fs
   INNER JOIN sys.master_files AS mf ON fs.database_id = mf.database_id
           AND fs.[file_id] = mf.[file_id]
 WHERE   ( io_stall_write_ms / ( 1.0
         + num_of_writes ) ) > 100
  AND num_of_writes > 100000
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Write latency is averaging over 100ms on at least one drive. Pull sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats to isolate the slow files. Check RAID health and SAN controller write cache status. Move transaction logs to a dedicated fast volume if they are sharing storage with data files. Do not wait on this - slow writes hit every commit on the server.
ExpectedResult: PASS when no database file averages more than 100ms per write over a significant sample (>100000 writes). FAIL when any file exceeds the threshold. SKIP not applicable.

Name: Multiple TempDB Data Files Required
Description: Multiple TempDB Data Files Required - Performance (sp_Blitz CheckID 40)
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

        SELECT   40 ,
            'tempdb' ,
            170 ,
            'File Configuration' ,
            'TempDB Only Has 1 Data File' ,
            'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tempdb' ,
            'TempDB is only configured with one data file.  More data files are usually required to alleviate SGAM contention.'
       WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*)
        FROM   tempdb.sys.database_files
        WHERE  type_desc = 'ROWS'
         ) = 1
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Multiple TempDB Data Files Required' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Multiple TempDB Data Files Required' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Multiple Log Files on One Drive
Description: One or more databases have multiple transaction log files on the same drive. SQL Server only uses one log file at a time (log files are sequential, not parallel), so multiple log files on the same drive provide no benefit and add management complexity.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT physical_name) AS cnt, [file_id], type_desc FROM sys.master_files WHERE [type] = 1 AND database_id > 4 GROUP BY [file_id], type_desc HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT physical_name) > 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Multiple Log Files on One Drive' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Multiple Log Files on One Drive' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Uneven File Growth Settings in One Filegroup
Description: One or more filegroups contain data files with different auto-growth settings. Uneven file growth leads to unbalanced proportional fill, where SQL Server cannot evenly distribute I/O across files, resulting in one file becoming disproportionately larger.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT mf.file_id FROM sys.master_files mf INNER JOIN sys.filegroups fg ON mf.data_space_id = fg.data_space_id WHERE mf.database_id > 4 AND fg.type = 'FG' GROUP BY mf.file_id, mf.growth, fg.data_space_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT mf.growth) > 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Uneven File Growth Settings in One Filegroup' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Uneven File Growth Settings in One Filegroup' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Queries Forcing Order Hints
Description: Queries in the plan cache are using FORCE ORDER hints, instructing the query optimizer to join tables in the order specified rather than choosing the optimal join order. This can lock in suboptimal execution plans as data distributions change.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
   SELECT  44 AS CheckID ,
  150 AS Priority ,
  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Queries Forcing Order Hints' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/hints' AS URL ,
  CAST(occurrence AS VARCHAR(10))
  + ' instances of order hinting have been recorded since restart.  This means queries are bossing the SQL Server optimizer around, and if they don''t know what they''re doing, this can cause more harm than good.  This can also explain why DBA tuning efforts aren''t working.' AS Details
FROM    sys.dm_exec_query_optimizer_info
WHERE   counter = 'order hint'
  AND occurrence > 1000
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Queries Forcing Order Hints' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Queries Forcing Order Hints' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Queries Forcing Join Hints
Description: Queries in the plan cache are using join hints (LOOP, HASH, or MERGE), overriding the query optimizer's join selection. Hard-coded join hints can prevent the optimizer from adapting to data changes and may result in degraded performance over time.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
   SELECT  45 AS CheckID ,
    150 AS Priority ,
    'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
    'Queries Forcing Join Hints' AS Finding ,
    'https://www.brentozar.com/go/hints' AS URL ,
    CAST(occurrence AS VARCHAR(10))
    + ' instances of join hinting have been recorded since restart.  This means queries are bossing the SQL Server optimizer around, and if they don''t know what they''re doing, this can cause more harm than good.  This can also explain why DBA tuning efforts aren''t working.' AS Details
  FROM    sys.dm_exec_query_optimizer_info
  WHERE   counter = 'join hint'
    AND occurrence > 1000
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Queries Forcing Join Hints' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Queries Forcing Join Hints' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Leftover Fake Indexes From Wizards
Description: Finds hypothetical indexes left behind by the Database Engine Tuning Advisor (DTA) or Index Tuning Wizard. Index names matching _dta_ or _hyp_ patterns are statistics-only structures DTA creates while modelling workloads - they hold no data and were meant to be cleaned up at the end of the tuning session. When the session crashes or someone closes the window early, they linger in sys.indexes forever, confusing future DBAs and skewing index analysis tools.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.indexes WHERE name LIKE '%[_]dta[_]%' OR name LIKE '%[_]hyp[_]%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: It looks like a previous DTA tuning session left hypothetical indexes behind. Inventory them by name (_dta_ or _hyp_) and by sys.indexes.is_hypothetical = 1, then DROP INDEX each one. There is no rollback risk - they hold no data. Drop any associated _dta_ statistics at the same time.
ExpectedResult: PASS when no hypothetical indexes (names like _dta_% or _hyp_%) are found. WARN when one or more are present - safe to drop because they hold no data. SKIP for SQL Server versions earlier than 2008.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process

Name: Indexes Disabled
Description: One or more indexes have been disabled. Disabled indexes cannot be used by the query optimizer and their data is not maintained during DML operations. The index definition remains but the optimizer cannot use it until it is rebuilt.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.indexes WHERE is_disabled = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Indexes Disabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Indexes Disabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Foreign Keys Not Trusted
Description: Finds foreign key constraints where is_not_trusted = 1. Untrusted FKs are constraints that were created or re-enabled WITH NOCHECK - SQL Server cannot use them for plan-quality decisions like join elimination or constant folding. The constraint still rejects new bad rows, so it looks healthy, but the optimizer treats it as advisory only.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.foreign_keys WHERE is_not_trusted = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: It looks like one or more foreign keys were re-enabled WITH NOCHECK and are now untrusted - the optimizer cannot use them for join elimination. Inventory them from sys.foreign_keys.is_not_trusted = 1, then run ALTER TABLE [child] WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT [fk_name] on each. Schedule large tables in a maintenance window. Re-trust untrusted CHECK constraints at the same time.
ExpectedResult: PASS when every foreign key has is_not_trusted = 0. WARN when one or more foreign keys are untrusted - safe but suboptimal for query plans. SKIP for SQL Server versions earlier than 2008.

Name: Linked Server Configured
Description: One or more linked servers are configured on this instance. Linked servers enable distributed queries but introduce security risks, performance overhead, and dependency on external servers. Failures on the remote server can impact local queries.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
   SELECT DISTINCT
     49 AS CheckID ,
     200 AS Priority ,
     'Informational' AS FindingsGroup ,
     'Linked Server Configured' AS Finding ,
     'https://www.brentozar.com/go/link' AS URL ,
     +CASE WHEN l.remote_name = 'sa'
        THEN COALESCE(s.data_source, s.name, s.provider)
          + ' is configured as a linked server. Check its security configuration as it is connecting with sa, because any user who queries it will get admin-level permissions.'
        ELSE COALESCE(s.data_source, s.name, s.provider)
          + ' is configured as a linked server. Check its security configuration to make sure it isn''t connecting with SA or some other bone-headed administrative login, because any user who queries it might get admin-level permissions.'
      END AS Details
   FROM    sys.servers s
     INNER JOIN sys.linked_logins l ON s.server_id = l.server_id
   WHERE   s.is_linked = 1
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Linked Server Configured' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Linked Server Configured' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Max Memory Set Too High
Description: Checks whether the max server memory setting exceeds the physical RAM installed on the server. SQL Server will attempt to use the configured ceiling - if that ceiling is larger than what Windows can provide, the OS will begin paging to disk. A server swapping to disk under SQL Server memory pressure runs queries an order of magnitude slower. This is usually a misconfiguration from a server rebuild or a memory upgrade that was never matched with a max memory adjustment.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  50 AS CheckID ,
    100 AS Priority ,
    'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
    'Max Memory Set Too High' AS Finding ,
    'https://www.brentozar.com/go/max' AS URL ,
    'SQL Server max memory is set to '
   + CAST(c.value_in_use AS VARCHAR(20))
   + ' megabytes, but the server only has '
   + CAST(( CAST(m.total_physical_memory_kb AS BIGINT) / 1024 ) AS VARCHAR(20))
   + ' megabytes.  SQL Server may drain the system dry of memory, and under certain conditions, this can cause Windows to swap to disk.' AS Details
    FROM    sys.dm_os_sys_memory m
    INNER JOIN sys.configurations c ON c.name = 'max server memory (MB)'
    WHERE   CAST(m.total_physical_memory_kb AS BIGINT) < ( CAST(c.value_in_use AS BIGINT) * 1024 )
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Max server memory is configured higher than physical RAM. Run sp_configure ''max server memory (MB)'', <value>; RECONFIGURE WITH OVERRIDE, setting the value to total physical RAM in MB minus at least 4096 MB for the OS. No restart needed. Verify with SELECT physical_memory_in_use_kb FROM sys.dm_os_process_memory after the change.
ExpectedResult: PASS when max server memory is at or below the server physical RAM. FAIL when configured memory exceeds physical RAM - Windows may page SQL Server to disk. SKIP for SQL Server versions earlier than 2008.

Name: Max Memory Set Too Low
Description: Detects when available physical memory on the server drops below 256 MB. At that level the OS starts paging - SQL Server buffer pool pages, thread stacks, and Windows kernel structures all compete for the last few hundred megabytes. Query throughput drops sharply and the server can become unresponsive. This is a near-OOM condition, not just a configuration advisory - it needs attention before the next workload spike.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
    SELECT  51 AS CheckID ,
    1 AS Priority ,
    'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
    'Memory Dangerously Low' AS Finding ,
    'https://www.brentozar.com/go/max' AS URL ,
    'The server has ' + CAST(( CAST(m.total_physical_memory_kb AS BIGINT) / 1024 ) AS VARCHAR(20)) + ' megabytes of physical memory, but only ' + CAST(( CAST(m.available_physical_memory_kb AS BIGINT) / 1024 ) AS VARCHAR(20))
   + ' megabytes are available.  As the server runs out of memory, there is danger of swapping to disk, which will kill performance.' AS Details
    FROM    sys.dm_os_sys_memory m
    WHERE   CAST(m.available_physical_memory_kb AS BIGINT) < 262144
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Available physical memory has dropped below 256MB - the server is close to swapping. Check SQL Server max memory with sp_configure ''max server memory (MB)'' - if it is at the default (2147483647) it is likely set too high. Reduce it to leave the OS at least 4GB. If max memory is already correct, look for runaway memory grants or a VM balloon driver taking memory without SQL Server''s knowledge.
ExpectedResult: PASS when available physical memory is above 256MB. FAIL when available RAM is dangerously low - risk of paging and query timeouts. SKIP for SQL Server versions earlier than 2008.

Name: Cluster Node Detected
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 53' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN SERVERPROPERTY('IsClustered') = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 53' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 53' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Interesting Database Owner
Description: Lists user databases whose owner is not the sa account. Non-sa owners get implicit CONTROL DATABASE rights and can become a privilege-escalation path when combined with TRUSTWORTHY ON. It looks like the more common case - the owner is a departed developer's domain account - and when that account is disabled, ownership-chained operations start failing in production.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name, SUSER_SNAME(d.owner_sid) AS owner FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.name NOT IN('master','model','msdb','tempdb') AND SUSER_SNAME(d.owner_sid) <> SUSER_SNAME(0x01) AND SUSER_SNAME(d.owner_sid) IS NOT NULL AND d.state = 0 AND d.is_in_standby = 0 AND d.source_database_id IS NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: It looks like one or more databases are owned by something other than sa. Capture the current owner as rollback, then run ALTER AUTHORIZATION ON DATABASE::YourDatabase TO sa on each. Review TRUSTWORTHY status at the same time - TRUSTWORTHY ON plus a sysadmin owner is the textbook db_owner-to-sysadmin escalation path.
ExpectedResult: PASS when every user database is owned by sa (or a documented service principal). WARN when one or more user databases have a different owner - typically a departed developer's domain account or a non-standard service principal. SKIP for SQL Server versions earlier than 2008.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Define and Maintain Role-Based Access Control
Access to System Components and Data is Appropriately Defined and Assigned

Name: Untrusted Check Constraints
Description: Finds CHECK constraints marked WITH NOCHECK - meaning SQL Server cannot trust that existing rows pass the constraint. Untrusted constraints still block new violations but the optimizer cannot use them to eliminate redundant filter operations. Plans get worse because the engine must re-check every row to defend against existing data that might not satisfy the rule. It seems like this happens most often after bulk imports or replication-switch operations where the NOCHECK flag was used to avoid the scan.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.check_constraints WHERE is_not_trusted = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more CHECK constraints are untrusted. Run ALTER TABLE [schema].[table] WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT [constraint_name] for each one - this validates existing data and re-marks the constraint as trusted. Schedule large tables in a maintenance window. Re-trust any untrusted foreign keys at the same time.
ExpectedResult: PASS when all check constraints have is_not_trusted = 0. WARN when one or more are untrusted - optimizer cannot use them for plan simplification. SKIP for SQL Server versions earlier than 2008.

Name: Alerts Configured without Follow Up
Description: SQL Server alerts are configured but have no operator notification or job response set up. Alerts without follow-up actions provide no value since nobody is notified when the alert condition is met.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  59 AS CheckID ,
  200 AS Priority ,
  'Monitoring' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Alerts Configured without Follow Up' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/alert' AS URL ,
  ( 'SQL Server Agent alerts have been configured but they either do not notify anyone or else they do not take any action.  This is a free, easy way to get notified of corruption, job failures, or major outages even before monitoring systems pick it up.' ) AS Details

  WHERE EXISTS (SELECT  * FROM    msdb.dbo.sysalerts
    WHERE   enabled = 1
    AND COALESCE(has_notification, 0) = 0
    AND (job_id IS NULL OR job_id = 0x)) 
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Alerts Configured without Follow Up' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Alerts Configured without Follow Up' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Fill Factor Changed
Description: Finds indexes where fill_factor is set to a value other than 0 (the default, treated as 100%) or 100. A non-default fill factor leaves intentional free space on every leaf page to reduce page splits during random inserts. Set blindly across every index, it inflates index size, increases IO for every read, and wastes buffer-pool memory. It looks like this setting was applied as a cargo-cult fix without measuring actual page-split rates - a common pattern that degrades the whole workload.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.indexes WHERE fill_factor <> 0 AND fill_factor <> 100) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: It looks like one or more indexes carry a non-default fill factor that may have been applied without measurement. Inventory them from sys.indexes, measure leaf_allocation_count on each from sys.dm_db_index_operational_stats, then ALTER INDEX REBUILD WITH (FILLFACTOR = 100, ONLINE = ON) on the ones where the page-split rate does not justify the setting. Reserve low fill factors for narrow, random-insert indexes only.
ExpectedResult: PASS when every index has fill_factor = 0 (default) or 100. WARN when one or more indexes use a different fill factor - confirm the setting was based on measured page-split rate, not a guess. SKIP for SQL Server versions earlier than 2008.

Name: Old Compatibility Level
Description: One or more databases are running at compatibility level 90 (SQL Server 2005) or below, despite sitting on a newer SQL Server version. Older compatibility levels block the optimizer from using improvements introduced in later releases. It looks like this never got updated after an upgrade - a common oversight that quietly caps query performance.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  62 AS CheckID ,
  [name] AS DatabaseName ,
  200 AS Priority ,
  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Old Compatibility Level' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/compatlevel' AS URL ,
  ( 'Database ' + [name]
    + ' is compatibility level '
    + CAST(compatibility_level AS VARCHAR(20))
    + ', which may cause unwanted results when trying to run queries that have newer T-SQL features.' ) AS Details
FROM    sys.databases
WHERE   compatibility_level <= 90
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: It looks like one or more databases were left at a legacy compatibility level after a SQL Server upgrade. Raise each database to the current server compatibility level one at a time. Use Query Store to capture a plan baseline before and after the change to catch regressions early.
ExpectedResult: PASS when no databases are at compatibility level 90 or below. WARN when one or more databases are running at a legacy level - each one needs a regression test before the ALTER DATABASE change.

Name: SQL Server Agent Jobs Owned By Users
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 63' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs WHERE enabled = 1 AND owner_sid <> 0x01) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 63' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 63' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: SQL Agent Jobs Without Failure Notifications
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 64' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.sysoperators o ON j.notify_email_operator_id = o.id WHERE j.enabled = 1 AND o.id IS NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 64' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 64' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: SQL Agent Jobs Without Schedules
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 65' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobschedules js RIGHT JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobs j ON js.job_id = j.job_id WHERE j.enabled = 1 AND js.schedule_id IS NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 65' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 65' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: SQL Agent Jobs Disabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 66' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT j.name FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h ON j.job_id = h.job_id WHERE j.enabled = 1 AND h.run_date < CONVERT(VARCHAR(8), DATEADD(dd, -7, GETDATE()), 112)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 66' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 66' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: SQL Server Agent Jobs Starting Automatically
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 67' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND owner_sid != 0x01) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 67' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 67' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: DBCC CHECKDB Not Performed Recently
Description: Flags read-write user databases where no DBCC CHECKDB run evidence exists in the last 7 days. CHECKDB is the only built-in way to catch storage-level corruption before a backup containing the corrupt page rolls off retention. By the time torn pages or 824 errors surface in the error log, your backup chain may already be poisoned.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.restorehistory rh ON d.name = rh.destination_database_name AND rh.restore_type = 'D' WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 AND d.name <> 'tempdb' AND d.source_database_id IS NULL AND ISNULL(DATABASEPROPERTYEX(d.name, 'Updateability'), '') = 'READ_WRITE' AND DATEDIFF(dd, ISNULL(rh.restore_date, '1900-01-01'), GETDATE()) > 7) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: It looks like one or more databases have no integrity-check evidence in the last week - so corruption could be silently propagating. Deploy Ola Hallengren's DatabaseIntegrityCheck and schedule weekly CHECKDB on every user database. For VLDBs, restore to a non-production instance and CHECKDB there to offload the IO cost. Add SQL Agent alerts on errors 823, 824, and 825.
ExpectedResult: PASS when every read-write user database has a successful DBCC CHECKDB within the last 7 days. FAIL when one or more read-write user databases have no CHECKDB evidence. SKIP when SQL Server version is earlier than 2008 or no read-write user databases are present.

Name: High VLF Count
Description: Counts Virtual Log Files (VLFs) inside each user database transaction log and flags databases with more than 999. VLF count grows when the log auto-grows in small increments over time. Excessive VLFs slow database startup, log backup, AlwaysOn redo, and transaction-log replay during restore - the last one is the painful surprise during a real recovery.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name, CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR(20)) AS VLFCount FROM sys.databases d CROSS APPLY sys.dm_db_log_info(DB_ID(d.name)) li WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.name NOT IN('master','model','msdb','tempdb') AND d.source_database_id IS NULL GROUP BY d.name HAVING COUNT(*) > 999) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: It looks like one or more transaction logs have grown in tiny increments and now carry thousands of VLFs. In a maintenance window, BACKUP LOG, then DBCC SHRINKFILE the log to a small size, then ALTER DATABASE MODIFY FILE to pre-grow in one large chunk with a fixed (non-percentage) FILEGROWTH. Confirm with COUNT(*) FROM sys.dm_db_log_info afterwards.
ExpectedResult: PASS when every user database has a VLF count at or below 999. WARN when one or more user databases exceed 999 VLFs - the log file fragmentation extends startup, log backup, and failover times. SKIP when SQL Server version is below 2016 SP2 (sys.dm_db_log_info not available).

Name: @@SERVERNAME Not Set
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 70' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS ( 
SELECT  70 AS CheckID ,
200 AS Priority ,
'Informational' AS FindingsGroup ,
'@@Servername Not Set' AS Finding ,
'https://www.brentozar.com/go/servername' AS URL ,
'@@Servername variable is null. You can fix it by executing: "sp_addserver ''<LocalServerName>'', local"' AS Details
) AND @@SERVERNAME IS NULL
THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 70' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 70' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: The partitioned database may have non-aligned indexes
Description: One or more partitioned databases may contain non-aligned indexes where the index partitioning does not match the table partitioning. Non-aligned indexes prevent partition elimination and can significantly reduce the performance benefits of partitioning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.indexes i INNER JOIN sys.partition_schemes ps ON i.data_space_id = ps.data_space_id WHERE i.data_space_id <> 0 AND ps.data_space_id IS NOT NULL AND OBJECTPROPERTY(i.object_id, 'IsUserTable') = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'The partitioned database may have non-aligned indexes' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'The partitioned database may have non-aligned indexes' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Default Trace Not Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 73' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE name = 'sa' AND is_disabled = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 73' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 73' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Auto-Growth Not Using Fixed Size
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 74' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.master_files WHERE is_percent_growth = 1 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 74' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 74' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Fill Factor Changed From Default
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 75' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.indexes WHERE fill_factor <> 0 AND fill_factor <> 100) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 75' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 75' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: MaxDOP Not Configured
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 76' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'max degree of parallelism' AND value_in_use = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 76' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 76' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Cost Threshold for Parallelism Too Low
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 77' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'cost threshold for parallelism' AND value_in_use <= 5) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 77' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 77' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Stored Procedure WITH RECOMPILE
Description: One or more stored procedures are created or called WITH RECOMPILE, forcing the procedure to be recompiled on every execution. While sometimes necessary, excessive recompilation consumes CPU and can be a significant performance drain under high concurrency.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.procedures WHERE create_date IS NOT NULL AND name LIKE '%RECOMPILE%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Stored Procedure WITH RECOMPILE' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Stored Procedure WITH RECOMPILE' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Forced Parameterization Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 79' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_parameterization_forced = 1 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 79' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.

Name: Max File Size Set
Description: One or more database files have a maximum file size configured. If a database file reaches its maximum size and cannot grow, the database will go into an error state, causing application failures and potential data loss.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.master_files WHERE max_size > 0 AND max_size <= growth AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Max File Size Set' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Max File Size Set' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
