Name: File growth set to percent
Description: One or more database files are configured with percentage-based auto-growth. Percentage growth causes increasingly large growth events as the file gets bigger (e.g., 10% of a 1TB database = 100GB growth), causing prolonged I/O stalls.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.master_files WHERE growth > 0 AND is_percent_growth = 1 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'File growth set to percent' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'File growth set to percent' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Remote Admin Connections Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 83' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_auto_create_stats_on = 0 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 83' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 83' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Database Mail Not Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 84' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.

Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 85' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.

RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 85' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.

Name: Elevated Permissions on a Database
Description: One or more users have elevated database roles such as db_owner or db_securityadmin in user databases. These broad permissions violate the principle of least privilege and can create security vulnerabilities.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT dbp.name FROM sys.database_principals dbp INNER JOIN sys.database_permissions p ON dbp.principal_id = p.grantee_principal_id WHERE p.class = 0 AND p.state_desc = 'GRANT' AND dbp.name NOT IN ('dbo','guest','INFORMATION_SCHEMA','sys') AND dbp.type NOT IN ('A','R') AND dbp.is_fixed_role = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Elevated Permissions on a Database' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Elevated Permissions on a Database' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Dedicated Admin Connection Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 88' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'remote admin connections' AND value_in_use = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 88' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 88' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Availability Groups has automatically repaired corruption
Description: This check identifies availability groups has automatically repaired corruption. Availability Groups has automatically repaired at least one corrupt page in the last 30 days. For more information, query the DMV sys.dm_hadr_auto_page_repair
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT DISTINCT
    89 AS CheckID ,
    db.name ,
    1 AS Priority ,
    'Corruption' AS FindingsGroup ,
    'Database Corruption Detected' AS Finding ,
    'https://www.brentozar.com/go/repair' AS URL ,
    ( 'Availability Groups has automatically repaired at least one corrupt page in the last 30 days. For more information, query the DMV sys.dm_hadr_auto_page_repair.' ) AS Details
    FROM    sys.dm_hadr_auto_page_repair rp
    INNER JOIN master.sys.databases db ON rp.database_id = db.database_id
    WHERE   rp.modification_time >= DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Availability Groups has automatically repaired corruption' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Availability Groups has automatically repaired corruption' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Database Corruption Detected
Description: Additional database corruption evidence has been detected, typically from DBCC output stored in the default trace or error log. This check complements BLITZ_034 and may indicate corruption in different databases or objects.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT DISTINCT
    90 AS CheckID ,
    db.name ,
    1 AS Priority ,
    'Corruption' AS FindingsGroup ,
    'Database Corruption Detected' AS Finding ,
    'https://www.brentozar.com/go/repair' AS URL ,
    ( 'SQL Server has detected at least one corrupt page in the last 30 days. For more information, query the system table msdb.dbo.suspect_pages.' ) AS Details
    FROM    msdb.dbo.suspect_pages sp
    INNER JOIN master.sys.databases db ON sp.database_id = db.database_id
    WHERE   sp.last_update_date >= DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Database Corruption Detected' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Database Corruption Detected' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Query Store Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 91' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE actual_state = 0 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 91' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 91' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Query Store Size Exceeds Threshold
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 92' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE max_storage_size_mb < 1024) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 92' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 92' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Backing Up to Same Drive Where Databases Reside
Description: Database backups are being written to the same physical drive where the database files reside. If that drive fails, both the database and its backups will be lost simultaneously, making recovery impossible.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT
 93 AS CheckID ,
 1 AS Priority ,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Backing Up to Same Drive Where Databases Reside' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/backup' AS URL ,
 CAST(COUNT(1) AS VARCHAR(50)) + ' backups done on drive '
 + UPPER(LEFT(bmf.physical_device_name, 3))
 + ' in the last two weeks, where database files also live. This represents a serious risk if that array fails.' Details
FROM msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily AS bmf
 INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset AS bs ON bmf.media_set_id = bs.media_set_id
   AND bs.backup_start_date >= ( DATEADD(dd,
   -14, GETDATE()) )
 /* Filter out databases that were recently restored: */
 LEFT OUTER JOIN msdb.dbo.restorehistory rh ON bs.database_name = rh.destination_database_name AND rh.restore_date > DATEADD(dd, -14, GETDATE())
WHERE UPPER(LEFT(bmf.physical_device_name, 3)) <> 'HTT' AND
  bmf.physical_device_name NOT LIKE '\\%' AND -- GitHub Issue #2141
  -- @IsWindowsOperatingSystem = 1 AND -- GitHub Issue #1995
  UPPER(LEFT(bmf.physical_device_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS, 3)) IN (
 SELECT DISTINCT
 UPPER(LEFT(mf.physical_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS, 3))
 FROM sys.master_files AS mf
  WHERE mf.database_id <> 2 )
 AND rh.destination_database_name IS NULL
GROUP BY UPPER(LEFT(bmf.physical_device_name, 3))
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Backing Up to Same Drive Where Databases Reside' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Backing Up to Same Drive Where Databases Reside' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Agent Jobs Without Failure Emails
Description: Finds enabled SQL Server Agent jobs that have no failure notification operator attached (no email, no net send, no pager). When these jobs fail, nobody finds out until a downstream report is wrong or a backup is missing. Most teams discover the gap during a real incident, by which point the data loss window has already opened.
SqlQuery:
;WITH las_job_run AS (
           SELECT  MAX(instance_id) AS instance_id, 
             job_id, COUNT_BIG(*) AS job_executions,
             SUM(CASE WHEN run_status = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed_executions
           FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory
           WHERE step_id = 0
           GROUP BY job_id
           )
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  94 AS CheckID ,
  200 AS [Priority] ,
  'Monitoring' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Agent Jobs Without Failure Emails' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/alerts' AS URL ,
  'The job ' + [name]
  + ' has not been set up to notify an operator if it fails.' 
  + CASE 
   WHEN jh.run_date IS NULL OR jh.run_time IS NULL OR jh.run_status IS NULL THEN ''
   ELSE N' Executions: '+ CAST(ljr.job_executions AS VARCHAR(30)) 
    + CASE ljr.failed_executions
     WHEN 0 THEN N''
     ELSE N' ('+CAST(ljr.failed_executions AS NVARCHAR(10)) + N' failed)'
     END
    + N' - last execution started on '
    + CAST(CONVERT(DATE,CAST(jh.run_date AS NVARCHAR(8)),113) AS NVARCHAR(10)) 
    + N', at ' 
    + STUFF(STUFF(RIGHT(N'000000' + CAST(jh.run_time AS varchar(6)),6),3,0,N':'),6,0,N':')
    + N', with status "'
    + CASE jh.run_status 
      WHEN 0 THEN N'Failed'
      WHEN 1 THEN N'Succeeded'
      WHEN 2 THEN N'Retry'
      WHEN 3 THEN N'Canceled'
      WHEN 4 THEN N'In Progress'
     END +N'".'
   END AS Details
FROM    msdb.[dbo].[sysjobs] j
  LEFT JOIN las_job_run ljr 
   ON ljr.job_id = j.job_id
  LEFT JOIN msdb.[dbo].[sysjobhistory] jh
   ON ljr.job_id = jh.job_id AND ljr.instance_id = jh.instance_id
WHERE   j.enabled = 1
  AND j.notify_email_operator_id = 0
  AND j.notify_netsend_operator_id = 0
  AND j.notify_page_operator_id = 0
          AND j.category_id <> 100
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: It looks like one or more enabled Agent jobs have no failure notification path. Create a Database Mail profile and an Agent operator, then attach the operator to every enabled job with notify_level_email = 2 (on failure). Add severity 16-25 alerts so corruption and severe errors page out even when a job did not run.
ExpectedResult: PASS when every enabled non-system Agent job has a failure notification operator (email, net send, or pager) attached. WARN when one or more enabled jobs have no notification path. SKIP when SQL Server Agent is not installed (Express Edition) or version is below 2008.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures

Name: Plan Guides Enabled
Description: One or more plan guides exist on this instance. Plan guides force specific query execution plans for targeted queries. While useful for fixing specific plan regressions, they add complexity and can mask underlying query or index issues.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.plan_guides) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Plan Guides Enabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Plan Guides Enabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: No Alerts for Corruption
Description: SQL Server alerts for corruption-related errors (823, 824, 825) are not configured. Without these alerts, corruption events will not trigger notifications to DBAs, potentially allowing undetected data integrity issues to go unresolved.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  96 AS CheckID ,
200 AS Priority ,
'Monitoring' AS FindingsGroup ,
'No Alerts for Corruption' AS Finding ,
'https://www.brentozar.com/go/alert' AS URL ,
( 'SQL Server Agent alerts do not exist for errors 823, 824, and 825.  These three errors can give you notification about early hardware failure. Enabling them can prevent you a lot of heartbreak.' ) AS Details
  WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT  *
  FROM    msdb.dbo.sysalerts
  WHERE   message_id IN ( 823, 824, 825 )) 
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'No Alerts for Corruption' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'No Alerts for Corruption' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Unusual SQL Server Edition
Description: SQL Server is running an unusual or unexpected edition (such as Express, Web, or Developer). These editions have feature and resource limitations that may not be appropriate for production workloads, including memory caps and missing enterprise features.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  97 AS CheckID ,
  100 AS Priority ,
  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Unusual SQL Server Edition' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/workgroup' AS URL ,
  ( 'This server is using '
    + CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100))
    + ', which is capped at low amounts of CPU and memory.' ) AS Details
WHERE   CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) NOT LIKE '%Standard%'
  AND CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) NOT LIKE '%Enterprise%'
  AND CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) NOT LIKE '%Data Center%'
  AND CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) NOT LIKE '%Developer%'
  AND CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) NOT LIKE '%Business Intelligence%'
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Unusual SQL Server Edition' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Unusual SQL Server Edition' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Alerts Disabled
Description: One or more SQL Server Agent alerts are disabled. Disabled alerts will not fire when their trigger conditions are met, potentially leaving critical errors or performance events unnoticed by the DBA team.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  98 AS CheckID ,
  200 AS Priority ,
  'Monitoring' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Alerts Disabled' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/alert' AS URL ,
  ( 'The following Alert is disabled, please review and enable if desired: '
    + name ) AS Details
FROM    msdb.dbo.sysalerts
WHERE   enabled = 0
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Alerts Disabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Alerts Disabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Infinite merge replication metadata retention period
Description: Merge replication is configured with an infinite metadata retention period. This causes the MSmerge_genhistory table in the publication database to grow without bounds, potentially consuming large amounts of disk space over time.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_published = 1 OR is_subscribed = 1 OR is_merge_published = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Infinite merge replication metadata retention period' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Infinite merge replication metadata retention period' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Remote DAC Disabled
Description: The Remote Dedicated Administrator Connection (DAC) is disabled. The DAC allows administrators to connect to SQL Server when it is unresponsive to normal connections. Without remote DAC, a DBA must have physical or RDP access to resolve certain emergencies.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  100 AS CheckID ,
 170 AS Priority ,
 'Reliability' AS FindingGroup ,
 'Remote DAC Disabled' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/dac' AS URL ,
 'Remote access to the Dedicated Admin Connection (DAC) is not enabled. The DAC can make remote troubleshooting much easier when SQL Server is unresponsive.'
 WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT  1
 FROM    sys.configurations
 WHERE   name = 'remote admin connections'
 AND value_in_use = 0 )
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Remote DAC Disabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Remote DAC Disabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: CPU Schedulers Offline
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 101' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  101 AS CheckID ,
   50 AS Priority ,
   'Performance' AS FindingGroup ,
   'CPU Schedulers Offline' AS Finding ,
   'https://www.brentozar.com/go/schedulers' AS URL ,
   'Some CPU cores are not accessible to SQL Server due to affinity masking or licensing problems.'
   WHERE EXISTS ( SELECT  *
FROM    sys.dm_os_schedulers
WHERE   is_online = 0 )
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 101' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 101' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Unusual Database State
Description: One or more databases are in an unusual state (not ONLINE), such as SUSPECT, RECOVERY_PENDING, RESTORING, or OFFLINE. Non-online databases are inaccessible to applications and indicate a potential problem requiring immediate investigation.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  102 AS CheckID ,
  [name] ,
  20 AS Priority ,
  'Reliability' AS FindingGroup ,
  'Unusual Database State: ' + [state_desc] AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/repair' AS URL ,
  'This database may not be online.'
FROM    sys.databases
WHERE   state > 1
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Unusual Database State' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Unusual Database State' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Login Can Control Server
Description: One or more SQL Server logins have been granted the CONTROL SERVER permission. CONTROL SERVER is a highly privileged permission equivalent to sysadmin for most purposes and should only be granted when absolutely necessary.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT 104 AS [CheckID] ,
 230 AS [Priority] ,
 'Security' AS [FindingsGroup] ,
 'Login Can Control Server' AS [Finding] ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/sa' AS [URL] ,
 'Login [' + pri.[name]
 + '] has the CONTROL SERVER permission - meaning they can do absolutely anything in SQL Server, including dropping databases or hiding their tracks.' AS [Details]
FROM sys.server_principals AS pri
WHERE pri.[principal_id] IN (
 SELECT p.[grantee_principal_id]
 FROM sys.server_permissions AS p
 WHERE p.[state] IN ( 'G', 'W' )
 AND p.[class] = 100
 AND p.[type] = 'CL' )
 AND pri.[name] NOT LIKE '##%##'
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Login Can Control Server' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Login Can Control Server' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Extended Stored Procedures in Master
Description: Checks whether extended stored procedures exist in the master database. Extended procedures in master indicate CLR is in use or legacy xp_-style procedures are present — both require master to be part of your backup and recovery plan. Most shops do not back up master on the same schedule as user databases, so this is a gap waiting to surface at the worst time.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  105 AS CheckID ,
  'master' ,
  200 AS Priority ,
  'Reliability' AS FindingGroup ,
  'Extended Stored Procedures in Master' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/clr' AS URL ,
  'The [' + name
  + '] extended stored procedure is in the master database. CLR may be in use, and the master database now needs to be part of your backup/recovery planning.'
FROM    master.sys.extended_procedures
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Extended stored procedures are in master. Make sure master is in your backup job and test it in DR rehearsals. Inventory all extended procedures with SELECT name FROM master.sys.extended_procedures and evaluate moving CLR objects to a user database with a proper backup schedule.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no extended stored procedures exist in master. Returns FAIL when one or more extended stored procedures are detected.

Name: Replication In Use
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 106' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_published = 1 OR is_subscribed = 1 OR is_merge_published = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 106' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 106' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Poison Wait Detected
Description: SQL Server is experiencing poison waits — wait types associated with severe performance problems such as THREADPOOL (thread exhaustion), RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE (memory pressure), or other critical wait conditions that indicate systemic performance issues.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  107 AS CheckID ,
  50 AS Priority ,
  'Performance' AS FindingGroup ,
  'Poison Wait Detected: ' + wait_type  AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/poison/#' + wait_type AS URL ,
  CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), (SUM([wait_time_ms]) / 1000) / 86400) + ':' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(20), DATEADD(s, (SUM([wait_time_ms]) / 1000), 0), 108) + ' of this wait have been recorded. This wait often indicates killer performance problems.'
FROM sys.[dm_os_wait_stats]
WHERE wait_type IN('IO_QUEUE_LIMIT', 'IO_RETRY', 'LOG_RATE_GOVERNOR', 'POOL_LOG_RATE_GOVERNOR', 'PREEMPTIVE_DEBUG', 'RESMGR_THROTTLED', 'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE', 'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE_QUERY_COMPILE','SE_REPL_CATCHUP_THROTTLE','SE_REPL_COMMIT_ACK','SE_REPL_COMMIT_TURN','SE_REPL_ROLLBACK_ACK','SE_REPL_SLOW_SECONDARY_THROTTLE','THREADPOOL')
GROUP BY wait_type
HAVING SUM([wait_time_ms]) > (SELECT 5000 * datediff(HH,create_date,CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) AS hours_since_startup FROM sys.databases WHERE name='tempdb')
AND SUM([wait_time_ms]) > 60000
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Poison Wait Detected' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Poison Wait Detected' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Memory Nodes Offline
Description: One or more NUMA memory nodes are offline, meaning SQL Server cannot access all physical memory regions. Offline memory nodes reduce the total memory available to SQL Server and can cause uneven memory distribution across NUMA nodes.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (


  SELECT  110 AS CheckID ,
  50 AS Priority ,
  'Performance' AS FindingGroup ,
  'Memory Nodes Offline' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/schedulers' AS URL ,
  'Due to affinity masking or licensing problems, some of the memory may not be available.' 
  WHERE EXISTS (SELECT  *
FROM sys.dm_os_nodes n
INNER JOIN sys.dm_os_memory_nodes m ON n.memory_node_id = m.memory_node_id
WHERE n.node_state_desc = 'OFFLINE')
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Memory Nodes Offline' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Memory Nodes Offline' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Possibly Broken Log Shipping
Description: Log shipping appears to be configured but may not be functioning correctly. The log shipping secondary may be falling behind or restore jobs may be failing, putting the secondary database out of sync with the primary.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

  SELECT  111 AS CheckID ,
    50 AS Priority ,
    'Reliability' AS FindingGroup ,
    'Possibly Broken Log Shipping'  AS Finding ,
    d.[name] ,
    'https://www.brentozar.com/go/shipping' AS URL ,
    d.[name] + ' is in a restoring state, but has not had a backup applied in the last two days. This is a possible indication of a broken transaction log shipping setup.'
    FROM [master].sys.databases d
    INNER JOIN [master].sys.database_mirroring dm ON d.database_id = dm.database_id
     AND dm.mirroring_role IS NULL
    WHERE ( d.[state] = 1
    OR (d.[state] = 0 AND d.[is_in_standby] = 1) )
    AND NOT EXISTS(SELECT * FROM msdb.dbo.restorehistory rh
    INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset bs ON rh.backup_set_id = bs.backup_set_id
    WHERE d.[name] COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = rh.destination_database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
    AND rh.restore_date >= DATEADD(dd, -2, GETDATE()))
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Possibly Broken Log Shipping' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Possibly Broken Log Shipping' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Change Tracking Enabled
Description: Change Tracking is enabled on one or more databases. Change Tracking records row-level changes for synchronization scenarios but adds overhead to DML operations and increases storage usage in the change tracking internal tables.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 112 AS CheckID,
  100 AS Priority,
  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
  'Change Tracking Enabled' AS Finding,
  d.[name],
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tracking' AS URL,
  ( d.[name] + ' has change tracking enabled. This is not a default setting, and it has some performance overhead. It keeps track of changes to rows in tables that have change tracking turned on.' ) AS Details 
  FROM sys.change_tracking_databases AS ctd INNER JOIN sys.databases AS d ON ctd.database_id = d.database_id
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Change Tracking Enabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Change Tracking Enabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Full Text Indexes Not Updating
Description: One or more full-text indexes are not being updated. Stale full-text indexes return outdated search results. This may indicate that full-text change tracking is disabled or full-text crawls are failing.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.fulltext_catalogs ftc INNER JOIN sys.fulltext_indexes fti ON ftc.fulltext_catalog_id = fti.fulltext_catalog_id INNER JOIN sys.objects o ON fti.object_id = o.object_id WHERE o.is_ms_shipped = 0 AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_fts_index_population WHERE database_id = DB_ID() AND status <> 0 AND catalog_id = ftc.fulltext_catalog_id)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Full Text Indexes Not Updating' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Full Text Indexes Not Updating' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Database Compatibility Level Mismatch
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 114' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE compatibility_level < 130 AND state = 0 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 114' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 114' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Parallelism Rocket Surger
Description: SQL Server has unusual parallelism configuration — either MAXDOP is set too high relative to the number of processors or Cost Threshold for Parallelism is set unusually low (default 5). These settings can cause excessive parallel query execution under load.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_query_plan(qs.plan_handle) qp WHERE qp.query_plan.exist('//RelOp[@Parallel="1"]') = 1 AND qs.last_execution_time > DATEADD(hh, -4, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Parallelism Rocket Surger' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Parallelism Rocket Surger' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Backup Compression Default Off
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Backup Compression Default Off' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  116 AS CheckID ,
 200 AS Priority ,
 'Informational' AS FindingGroup ,
 'Backup Compression Default Off'  AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/backup' AS URL ,
 'Uncompressed full backups have happened recently, and backup compression is not turned on at the server level. Backup compression is included with Standard Edition. We recommend turning backup compression on by default so that ad-hoc backups will get compressed.'
 FROM sys.configurations
 WHERE configuration_id = 1579 AND CAST(value_in_use AS INT) = 0
    AND EXISTS (SELECT * FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE backup_size = compressed_backup_size AND type = 'D' AND backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(DD, -14, GETDATE()))
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Backup Compression Default Off' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Backup Compression Default Off' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Memory Pressure Affecting Queries
Description: SQL Server has recorded forced memory grants in sys.dm_exec_query_resource_semaphores, meaning queries had to run with less memory than they requested because the server was under pressure. When this happens, queries spill to TempDB and slow down. It looks like the server's max server memory is misconfigured or workload has grown past available RAM.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

 SELECT 117 AS CheckID,
 100 AS Priority,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
 'Memory Pressure Affecting Queries' AS Finding,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/grants' AS URL,
 CAST(SUM(forced_grant_count) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + ' forced grants reported in the DMV sys.dm_exec_query_resource_semaphores, indicating memory pressure has affected query runtimes.'
 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_resource_semaphores WHERE [forced_grant_count] IS NOT NULL 
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: It looks like memory pressure has forced queries to run with reduced memory grants. Check max server memory configuration first - it should leave 10-20% for the OS. Then identify the queries requesting the largest grants and investigate their execution plans for missing indexes causing bad cardinality estimates.
ExpectedResult: PASS when forced_grant_count = 0. WARN when any resource semaphore shows forced grants - queries have been running slower than they should. Investigate max server memory and top memory-consuming queries.

Name: SQL Agent Job History Not Purged
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 118' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory WHERE run_date < CONVERT(VARCHAR(8), DATEADD(dd, -90, GETDATE()), 112)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 118' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 118' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: TDE Certificate Not Backed Up Recently
Description: A TDE (Transparent Data Encryption) certificate has not been backed up within the past 30 days. Without a backup of the TDE certificate and its private key, encrypted database backups cannot be restored to another server.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 119 AS CheckID,
  1 AS Priority,
  'Backup' AS FindingsGroup,
  'TDE Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' AS Finding,
  db_name(dek.database_id) AS DatabaseName,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tde' AS URL,
  'The certificate ' + c.name + ' is used to encrypt database ' + db_name(dek.database_id) + '. Last backup date: ' + COALESCE(CAST(c.pvt_key_last_backup_date AS VARCHAR(100)), 'Never') AS Details
  FROM master.sys.certificates c INNER JOIN sys.dm_database_encryption_keys dek ON c.thumbprint = dek.encryptor_thumbprint
  WHERE pvt_key_last_backup_date IS NULL OR pvt_key_last_backup_date <= DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE()) 

 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'TDE Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'TDE Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Poison Wait Detected: Serializable Sleeping
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 121' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  121 AS CheckID ,
  50 AS Priority ,
  'Performance' AS FindingGroup ,
  'Poison Wait Detected: Serializable Locking'  AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/serializable' AS URL ,
  CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), (SUM([wait_time_ms]) / 1000) / 86400) + ':' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(20), DATEADD(s, (SUM([wait_time_ms]) / 1000), 0), 108) + ' of LCK_M_R% waits have been recorded. This wait often indicates killer performance problems.'
FROM sys.[dm_os_wait_stats]
WHERE wait_type IN ('LCK_M_RS_S', 'LCK_M_RS_U', 'LCK_M_RIn_NL','LCK_M_RIn_S', 'LCK_M_RIn_U','LCK_M_RIn_X', 'LCK_M_RX_S', 'LCK_M_RX_U','LCK_M_RX_X')
HAVING SUM([wait_time_ms]) > (SELECT 5000 * datediff(HH,create_date,CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) AS hours_since_startup FROM sys.databases WHERE name='tempdb')
AND SUM([wait_time_ms]) > 60000
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 121' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 121' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: User-Created Statistics In Place
Description: Reports whether any statistics objects exist that were created manually (user_created = 1, auto_created = 0). These are often filtered or multi-column stats added by a DBA to fix a specific cardinality estimate. Worth knowing they are there - they rarely update themselves and they outlive the DBA who created them.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.stats WHERE user_created = 1 AND auto_created = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: List the user-created statistics. For each, decide keep or drop based on whether the originating query still matters. For the keepers, add them to the UPDATE STATISTICS maintenance schedule and document why they exist.
ExpectedResult: Returns 1 when at least one row in sys.stats has user_created = 1 and auto_created = 0, 0 otherwise. A returned 1 is informational - the stats may be legitimate, but each one needs an owner and a maintenance plan.

Name: TempDB Unevenly Sized Data Files
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 123' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.master_files mf INNER JOIN sys.databases d ON mf.database_id = d.database_id WHERE d.name = 'tempdb' AND mf.type = 0 GROUP BY mf.size HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT mf.size) > 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 123' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 123' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Deadlocks Happening Daily
Description: The deadlock counter from sys.dm_os_performance_counters, divided by days since the last server restart, shows more than 10 deadlocks per day. Each deadlock kills at least one transaction and forces the application to retry or surface an error. It looks like there is a concurrency design problem in the application that has never been looked at.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(p.cntr_value AS BIGINT) / NULLIF(CAST(DATEDIFF(DD, d.create_date, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) AS DECIMAL(23,2)), 0)) > 10 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters p INNER JOIN sys.databases d ON d.name = 'tempdb' WHERE RTRIM(p.counter_name) = 'Number of Deadlocks/sec' AND RTRIM(p.instance_name) = '_Total' AND p.cntr_value > 0
RecommendedAction: Capture the deadlock graphs from system_health, identify the two transactions involved in the most frequent pattern, and fix the table access order or add indexes to reduce lock duration. Consider enabling READ COMMITTED SNAPSHOT ISOLATION to eliminate reader-writer conflicts at the source.
ExpectedResult: PASS when deadlock rate is below 10 per day. WARN when the counter shows more than 10 daily deadlocks since the last restart - application transaction patterns need review.

Name: Plan Cache Erased Recently
Description: Looks for evidence that DBCC FREEPROCCACHE was executed recently, which clears all cached execution plans. After a plan cache clear, every query compiles a fresh plan on next execution - causing a CPU spike and temporary slowdown. This check is informational; a cleared cache is not always a problem, but it is worth knowing when it happened.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(qs.plan_handle) st WHERE st.text LIKE '%DBCC FREEPROCCACHE%' AND qs.last_execution_time > DATEADD(hh, -24, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Identify who cleared the plan cache and whether it was intentional. If a maintenance script is running DBCC FREEPROCCACHE regularly, replace it with a targeted alternative. Add an audit on DBCC commands to catch future accidental runs.
ExpectedResult: PASS when no recent FREEPROCCACHE is detected. INFO when the command appears in recent query stats - correlate with any CPU spikes in the same window and confirm the action was deliberate.

Name: Priority Boost Enabled
Description: SQL Server's Priority Boost option is enabled, which raises the SQL Server process priority in the Windows scheduler. This can cause the SQL Server process to starve the OS and other processes of CPU time, potentially causing system instability or crashes.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 126, 5, 'Reliability', 'Priority Boost Enabled', 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/priorityboost/',
        'Priority Boost sounds awesome, but it can actually cause your SQL Server to crash.'
FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'priority boost' AND (value = 1 OR value_in_use = 1)
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Priority Boost Enabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Priority Boost Enabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Old Compatibility Level Detected
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 128' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE compatibility_level < 130 AND state = 0 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 128' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 128' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Query Store Cleanup Disabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 129' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE desired_state = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 129' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 129' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Query Store in Read-Only Mode
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 130' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE actual_state = 2 AND desired_state = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 130' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 130' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Max Server Memory Not Configured
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 142' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'max server memory (MB)' AND value_in_use = 2147483647) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 142' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 142' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: High Memory Use for In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton)
Description: In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton) tables are consuming a high percentage of the SQL Server memory grant. When In-Memory OLTP usage approaches the configured memory limit, the instance can begin to reject transactions with out-of-memory errors.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_db_xtp_memory_consumers WHERE memory_consumer_type_desc = 'VARHEAP' AND allocated_bytes > 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'High Memory Use for In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton)' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'High Memory Use for In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton)' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton) In Use
Description: In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton) tables or natively compiled stored procedures are in use on this instance. This is informational to flag the presence of this feature. In-Memory OLTP has specific backup, high-availability, and monitoring requirements that differ from standard disk-based tables - worth knowing before you run a routine health check.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_db_xtp_memory_consumers) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Confirm backup jobs include the memory-optimized filegroup and test a restore. Review HA configuration to verify replicas support In-Memory OLTP. Document which tables and procedures are memory-optimized in the runbook.
ExpectedResult: PASS when sys.dm_db_xtp_memory_consumers returns no rows. INFO when memory-optimized consumers are found - this is informational, not a failure, but it triggers a backup and HA configuration review.

Name: In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton) Transaction Errors
Description: In-Memory OLTP is generating transaction errors, such as write conflicts or out-of-memory conditions. These errors indicate that In-Memory OLTP workloads are exceeding configured capacity or encountering concurrency issues.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_db_xtp_transactions) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton) Transaction Errors' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'In-Memory OLTP (Hekaton) Transaction Errors' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Database Files on Network File Shares
Description: Detects database files stored on UNC network file shares. Network shares introduce single points of failure — a dropped network connection or share permission change takes the database offline immediately. SQL Server also requires specific service account permissions for UNC paths that are easy to break during OS or AD maintenance.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
SELECT DISTINCT 148 AS CheckID ,
         d.[name] AS DatabaseName ,
         170 AS Priority ,
         'Reliability' AS FindingsGroup ,
         'Database Files on Network File Shares' AS Finding ,
         'https://www.brentozar.com/go/nas' AS URL ,
         ( 'Files for this database are on: ' + LEFT(mf.physical_name, 30)) AS Details
 FROM    sys.databases d
   INNER JOIN sys.master_files mf ON d.database_id = mf.database_id
 WHERE mf.physical_name LIKE '\\%'  
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Database files are on network shares. A dropped network connection takes the database suspect immediately. Plan migration to locally attached or SAN storage. If UNC is unavoidable, make sure it is SMB 3.0+, on a dedicated network path, with SQL service account permissions documented and change-controlled.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no database files are on UNC network paths. Returns FAIL when one or more database files are detected on network file shares.

Name: Database Files Stored in Azure
Description: Detects database files stored directly on Azure Blob Storage via HTTP URL paths. While SQL Server supports this configuration, Azure blob latency is higher than locally attached storage and connectivity gaps can take a database offline. Most shops using this pattern do not have the network redundancy or failover plan the setup requires.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.databases d
    INNER JOIN sys.master_files mf ON d.database_id = mf.database_id
    WHERE mf.physical_name LIKE 'http://%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Database files are stored on Azure blob. A connectivity gap takes them offline immediately with no retry. Verify your network redundancy plan covers this path. If the configuration is unintentional, plan migration to locally attached storage.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no database files are stored on Azure blob via URL. Returns FAIL when URL-based files exist.

Name: Errors Logged Recently in the Default Trace
Description: SQL Server's default trace has recorded errors or significant events recently. The default trace captures events such as login failures, configuration changes, and database growth events that may indicate underlying issues.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.fn_trace_gettable((SELECT TOP 1 path FROM sys.traces WHERE is_default = 1), DEFAULT) WHERE EventClass IN(55, 56, 57, 58, 59, 60) AND StartTime > DATEADD(dd, -7, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Errors Logged Recently in the Default Trace' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Errors Logged Recently in the Default Trace' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: File Growths Slow
Description: Detects database file autogrowth events that took longer than 15 seconds in the last 30 days. SQL Server pauses all write activity to a file while it grows - so a 15-second growth means 15 seconds of application timeouts. More than one slow growth in a month means the files are undersized. Have you ever had a performance specialist look at your file sizing? No - it seems like this has never been set deliberately.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.traces t
    CROSS APPLY sys.fn_trace_gettable(t.[path], t.is_rollover) ft
    WHERE t.is_default = 1
      AND ft.EventClass IN (92, 93)
      AND ft.Duration > 15000000
      AND ft.StartTime > DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: File growths over 15 seconds are happening. Each one froze application writes for that duration. Pre-size the affected files to avoid growth events. Enable Instant File Initialization on the SQL Server service account to make future growths near-instant. Change autogrowth from percentage to a fixed MB increment.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no slow file growth events are detected. Returns FAIL when growths exceeding 15 seconds are found in the last 30 days.

Name: Trace Flags Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 152' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_server_registry WHERE registry_key LIKE '%Parameters%' AND value_name LIKE 'TraceFlags%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 152' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 152' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: 32-bit SQL Server Installed
Description: SQL Server is running as a 32-bit process. 32-bit SQL Server has a maximum addressable memory limit of approximately 4GB (with PAE extensions), which is severely limiting for any production workload and prevents use of modern hardware capabilities.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  154 AS CheckID ,
  10 AS Priority ,
  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
  '32-bit SQL Server Installed' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/32bit' AS URL ,
  ( 'This server uses the 32-bit x86 binaries for SQL Server instead of the 64-bit x64 binaries. The amount of memory available for query workspace and execution plans is heavily limited.' ) AS Details
WHERE   CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) NOT LIKE '%64%'
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding '32-bit SQL Server Installed' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of '32-bit SQL Server Installed' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Buffer Pool Extension Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 155' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_buffer_pool_extension_configuration) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 155' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 155' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Dangerous Build of SQL Server (Security)
Description: SQL Server is running on a build that has known security vulnerabilities. Microsoft has released patches or cumulative updates that address security issues in this build. Applying the latest CU is recommended to protect against known exploits.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE compatibility_level < 130 AND state = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Dangerous Build of SQL Server (Security)' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Dangerous Build of SQL Server (Security)' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: File growth set to 1MB
Description: One or more database files are configured to grow in 1MB increments. For any database larger than a few gigabytes, 1MB growth events are extremely small, causing frequent growth events that each stall I/O until the file system allocates the new space.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.master_files WHERE growth = 1 AND is_percent_growth = 0 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'File growth set to 1MB' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'File growth set to 1MB' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Memory Dangerously Low in NUMA Nodes
Description: This check identifies memory dangerously low in numa nodes'. This check identifies memory dangerously low in numa nodes'. Check 159 (sp_Blitz CheckID 159, Priority 100)
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
    SELECT DISTINCT 159 AS CheckID ,
    1 AS Priority ,
    'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
    'Memory Dangerously Low in NUMA Nodes' AS Finding ,
    'https://www.brentozar.com/go/max' AS URL ,
    'At least one NUMA node is reporting THREAD_RESOURCES_LOW in sys.dm_os_nodes and can no longer create threads.' AS Details
    FROM    sys.dm_os_nodes m
    WHERE   node_state_desc LIKE '%THREAD_RESOURCES_LOW%'
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Memory Dangerously Low in NUMA Nodes'' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Memory Dangerously Low in NUMA Nodes'' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Many Plans for One Query
Description: One or more queries have an unusually high number of execution plans cached. This indicates query plan instability, often caused by parameter sniffing issues or varying SET options between connections leading to excessive plan compilation overhead.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT * FROM sys.all_columns WHERE name = 'query_hash')
     AND EXISTS (
SELECT TOP 1 160 AS CheckID,
 100 AS Priority,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
 'Many Plans for One Query' AS Finding,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/parameterization' AS URL,
 CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT plan_handle) AS NVARCHAR(50)) + ' plans are present for a single query in the plan cache - meaning we probably have parameterization issues.' AS Details
 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs
    CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_plan_attributes(qs.plan_handle) pa
    WHERE pa.attribute = 'dbid'
    GROUP BY qs.query_hash, pa.value
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT plan_handle) > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 2 FROM sys.databases)
 ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT plan_handle) DESC
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Many Plans for One Query' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Many Plans for One Query' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: High Number of Cached Plans
Description: The procedure cache contains an unusually high number of cached plans. A bloated plan cache consumes significant memory that could otherwise be used for data pages, and can slow plan lookups during query compilation.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT TOP 1 161 AS CheckID,
 100 AS Priority,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
 'High Number of Cached Plans' AS Finding,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/planlimits' AS URL,
 'Your server configuration is limited to ' + CAST(ht.buckets_count * 4 AS VARCHAR(20)) + ' ' + ht.name + ', and you are currently caching ' + CAST(cc.entries_count AS VARCHAR(20)) + '.' AS Details
 FROM sys.dm_os_memory_cache_hash_tables ht
 INNER JOIN sys.dm_os_memory_cache_counters cc ON ht.name = cc.name AND ht.type = cc.type
 where ht.name IN ( 'SQL Plans' , 'Object Plans' , 'Bound Trees' )
 AND cc.entries_count >= (3 * ht.buckets_count)
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'High Number of Cached Plans' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'High Number of Cached Plans' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: DBCC CHECKDB Not Executed Recently
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 162' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 AND d.name <> 'tempdb' AND (SELECT MAX(checkdb_time) FROM msdb.dbo.sysdbmaintplan_history WHERE database_name = d.name AND activity = 'CHECKDB') < DATEADD(dd, -7, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 162' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 162' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Query Store Disabled
Description: Query Store is disabled on one or more user databases. Query Store records query execution plans and runtime statistics, making it the first tool you reach for when a query suddenly gets slower after a deployment or statistics update. Without it, plan regressions are diagnosed by guesswork. It should be on for every production database.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE desired_state = 0 AND actual_state = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Query Store is off. Enable it on each affected database - ALTER DATABASE [name] SET QUERY_STORE = ON. Without it, the next plan regression after a deployment will take hours to diagnose instead of minutes.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when Query Store is enabled on all user databases. Returns FAIL when one or more databases have Query Store disabled.

Name: Plan Guides Failing
Description: One or more plan guides are failing to apply. When a plan guide fails, SQL Server silently falls back to the optimizer's default plan selection. Failing plan guides indicate that the query signature has changed and the guide no longer matches.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.plan_guides WHERE is_disabled = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Plan Guides Failing' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Plan Guides Failing' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Too Much Free Memory
Description: SQL Server has an unusually large amount of free (uncommitted) memory relative to the server's configured maximum. This may indicate that the max server memory setting is too high relative to what workloads actually need, or that workloads have recently decreased.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT 165, 50, 'Performance', 'Too Much Free Memory', 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/freememory',
  CAST((CAST(cFree.cntr_value AS BIGINT) / 1024 / 1024 ) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + N'GB of free memory inside SQL Server''s buffer pool, which is ' + CAST((CAST(cTotal.cntr_value AS BIGINT) / 1024 / 1024) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + N'GB. You would think lots of free memory would be good, but check out the URL for more information.' AS Details
 FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters cFree
 INNER JOIN sys.dm_os_performance_counters cTotal ON cTotal.object_name LIKE N'%Memory Manager%'
  AND cTotal.counter_name = N'Total Server Memory (KB)                                                                                                        '
 WHERE cFree.object_name LIKE N'%Memory Manager%'
  AND cFree.counter_name = N'Free Memory (KB)                                                                                                                '
  AND CAST(cTotal.cntr_value AS BIGINT) > 20480000000
  AND CAST(cTotal.cntr_value AS BIGINT) * .3 <= CAST(cFree.cntr_value AS BIGINT)
        AND CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) NOT LIKE '%Standard%'
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Too Much Free Memory' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Too Much Free Memory' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Backup Encryption Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 166' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE is_copy_only = 0 AND type = 'D' AND encrypted = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 166' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 166' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Agent is Currently Offline
Description: SQL Server Agent is currently stopped or offline. Agent being offline means no scheduled jobs will run, including backups, maintenance plans, monitoring jobs, and any ETL or automated processes depending on the Agent.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs WHERE enabled = 1 AND name LIKE '%Agent%' AND name NOT LIKE '%SQL Server Agent%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Agent is Currently Offline' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Agent is Currently Offline' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Full-Text Filter Daemon Launcher Offline
Description: Detects when the SQL Full-text Filter Daemon Launcher service is not running. This service is responsible for managing full-text index population and querying. When it is offline, full-text indexes will not be populated or updated, and full-text queries may return incomplete or no results, silently breaking full-text search functionality.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_services
    WHERE [status_desc] <> 'Running'
      AND [servicename] LIKE 'SQL Full-text Filter Daemon Launcher%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Start the SQL Full-text Filter Daemon Launcher service and set its startup type to Automatic.
Check the Windows event log for service startup failures that may indicate underlying issues.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when the Full-text Filter Daemon Launcher is running. Returns FAIL when it is stopped.

Name: SQL Server Running as NT Service Account
Description: Reports when the SQL Server database engine service is running under a virtual NT Service account (e.g., NT Service\MSSQLSERVER). This is the modern default for SQL Server installations and is generally a secure, low-privilege configuration. This check is purely informational and helps administrators understand the service account configuration at a glance.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_services
    WHERE [service_account] LIKE 'NT Service%'
      AND [servicename] LIKE 'SQL Server%'
      AND [servicename] NOT LIKE 'SQL Server Agent%'
      AND [servicename] NOT LIKE 'SQL Server Launchpad%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
No action required; this is the expected and secure service account configuration.
If a domain account is required for specific needs, ensure it follows the principle of least privilege.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when SQL Server uses an NT Service account (informational). Returns FAIL otherwise.

Name: SQL Agent Running as NT Service Account
Description: Reports when the SQL Server Agent service is running under a virtual NT Service account (e.g., NT Service\SQLSERVERAGENT). Virtual accounts provide automatic password management and reduced attack surface. This check is informational and helps you quickly verify the service account configuration for SQL Agent during audits or troubleshooting.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_services
    WHERE [service_account] LIKE 'NT Service%'
      AND [servicename] LIKE 'SQL Server Agent%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
No action required; virtual accounts are the secure default for SQL Agent.
If a domain account is used for cross-server job steps, ensure it has only necessary permissions.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when SQL Agent uses an NT Service account. Returns FAIL otherwise.

Name: Resumable Index Operations Paused
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 172' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.index_resumable_operations WHERE state_desc = 'PAUSED') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 172' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 172' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Non-Production License
Description: Detects when SQL Server is running under a Developer or Evaluation edition license. Developer Edition is free for development and testing but is not licensed for production use. Evaluation Edition has a 180-day time limit after which SQL Server will stop working. Running either in production violates Microsoft licensing terms and puts your application at risk of unexpected shutdown.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    WHERE CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) LIKE '%Developer%'
       OR CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) LIKE '%Evaluation%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Upgrade to a licensed edition (Standard or Enterprise) before deploying to production.
If this is a development or test server, no action is required.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when a production-licensed edition is in use. Returns FAIL when Developer or Evaluation edition is detected.

Name: Buffer Pool Extensions Enabled
Description: Buffer Pool Extension (BPE) is enabled. BPE uses a fast SSD as a second-tier cache for the buffer pool, extending its effective size. This feature can help on systems with insufficient RAM but adds complexity and may not provide expected benefits.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_buffer_pool_extension_configuration) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Buffer Pool Extensions Enabled' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Buffer Pool Extensions Enabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: TempDB Has More Than 16 Data Files
Description: Detects when tempdb has more than 16 data files. SQL Server recommends one tempdb data file per logical processor core, up to a maximum of 8, unless you observe allocation page latch contention (PAGELATCH waits on tempdb files). Having too many tempdb files can actually increase management overhead without improving performance and may indicate a misconfiguration or a runaway script that created excessive files.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.master_files AS mf
    WHERE mf.database_id = 2
      AND mf.[type] = 0
    HAVING COUNT_BIG(*) > 16
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Reduce tempdb data file count to match the number of logical CPU cores, up to a maximum of 8.
Ensure all tempdb data files have identical sizing and autogrowth settings.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when tempdb has 16 or fewer data files. Returns FAIL when more than 16 exist.

Name: Extended Events Hyperextension
Description: An unusually high number of Extended Events sessions are active. Too many active Extended Events sessions can add overhead to SQL Server operations, as each session must evaluate and capture events matching its configured filters.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_xe_sessions WHERE name NOT LIKE '%system_health%' AND name NOT LIKE '%sp_blocker%' AND name NOT LIKE '%telemetry%' AND create_time > DATEADD(dd, -1, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Extended Events Hyperextension' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Extended Events Hyperextension' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Disabled Internal Monitoring Features
Description: Detects when SQL Server has been started with the -x startup parameter, which disables internal monitoring features including CPU and I/O diagnostic events, system health extended event sessions, and other lightweight monitoring built into the engine. The -x flag should only be used temporarily under Microsoft support guidance and is extremely dangerous for production workloads because it blinds you to performance and health telemetry.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_registry AS dsr
    WHERE dsr.registry_key LIKE N'%MSSQLServer\Parameters'
      AND dsr.value_data = '-x'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Remove the -x startup parameter immediately from SQL Server Configuration Manager.
Restart the SQL Server service after removing the parameter to restore internal monitoring.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when -x startup parameter is not present. Returns FAIL when -x is detected.

Name: Snapshot Backups Occurring
Description: SQL Server is performing volume snapshot backups (using VSS or third-party snapshot technology). While valid, snapshot backups require proper configuration and may affect SQL Server's log truncation behavior if not properly acknowledged.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 178 AS CheckID ,
 200 AS Priority ,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Snapshot Backups Occurring' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/snaps' AS URL ,
 ( CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR(20)) + ' snapshot-looking backups have occurred in the last two weeks, indicating that IO may be freezing up.') AS Details
 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs
 WHERE bs.type = 'D'
 AND bs.backup_size >= 50000000000 /* At least 50GB */
 AND DATEDIFF(SECOND, bs.backup_start_date, bs.backup_finish_date) <= 60 /* Backup took less than 60 seconds */
 AND bs.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -14, GETDATE()) /* In the last 2 weeks */
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Snapshot Backups Occurring' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Snapshot Backups Occurring' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Dangerous Third Party Modules
Description: Detects non-Microsoft DLLs loaded into the SQL Server process space. Antivirus agents, monitoring hooks, and backup software loaded in-process have caused SQL Server crashes, access violations, and memory corruption. When a third-party DLL corrupts the SQL process, the only fix is a restart — and a core dump with no clear root cause.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT name FROM sys.dm_os_loaded_modules WHERE name NOT LIKE '%Microsoft%' AND name NOT LIKE '%SQL%' AND name NOT LIKE '%.dll' AND name NOT LIKE '%sqlservr%' AND company NOT LIKE '%Microsoft%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Non-Microsoft modules are loaded in the SQL Server process. Review with SELECT company, name FROM sys.dm_os_loaded_modules WHERE company NOT LIKE '%Microsoft%' AND company IS NOT NULL. Work with each vendor to move their agent out-of-process. Exclude SQL data and log files from antivirus scanning per KB309422.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no non-Microsoft modules are loaded in the SQL Server process. Returns FAIL when one or more non-Microsoft modules are detected in-process.

Name: Shrink Database Step In Maintenance Plan
Description: A SQL Server Maintenance Plan contains a Shrink Database step. Shrinking a database fragments every index it touches, and the freed space is typically re-consumed within days, so the I/O was wasted. This is the most common self-inflicted performance wound in SQL Server maintenance - remove it from the plan.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysssispackages WHERE CAST(CAST(packagedata AS VARBINARY(MAX)) AS VARCHAR(MAX)) LIKE '%Shrink Database Task%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
