RecommendedAction: A Shrink Database task is in a Maintenance Plan. Remove it. Every time it runs, it fragments indexes and wastes I/O for space that grows back. Open the Maintenance Plan in SSMS and delete the Shrink Database step.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no Shrink Database task is found in any Maintenance Plan. Returns FAIL when the task exists.

Name: Repetitive Steps In Maintenance Plans
Description: SQL Server Maintenance Plans contain redundant steps - such as both rebuilding and reorganizing indexes on the same database, or running DBCC CHECKDB multiple times. Redundant steps waste the maintenance window and compound I/O load. It looks like the plans were configured without a clear strategy and may be doing more harm than good.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysssispackages WHERE CAST(CAST(packagedata AS VARBINARY(MAX)) AS VARCHAR(MAX)) LIKE '%Duplicate Steps%' OR CAST(CAST(packagedata AS VARBINARY(MAX)) AS VARCHAR(MAX)) LIKE '%Repetitive Steps%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Redundant maintenance steps are configured. Open the Maintenance Plan in SSMS, remove the duplicate operation type (keep rebuild OR reorganize, not both), and consolidate any duplicate CHECKDB steps. This reduces I/O load and shortens the maintenance window.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no redundant steps are found in Maintenance Plans. Returns FAIL when duplicate step types exist.

Name: Query Store Cleanup Disabled
Description: Detects databases where Query Store is enabled but automatic size-based cleanup is turned off. Without cleanup, Query Store data grows until it hits the configured max size, at which point Query Store switches to read-only mode and stops capturing new query plans. You lose the plan history you need exactly when a regression happens.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE actual_state = 2 AND size_based_cleanup_mode = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Query Store cleanup is disabled on one or more databases. Enable size-based cleanup with ALTER DATABASE [dbname] SET QUERY_STORE (SIZE_BASED_CLEANUP_MODE = AUTO). Check current fill level with SELECT actual_state_desc, current_storage_size_mb, max_storage_size_mb FROM sys.database_query_store_options.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all databases with Query Store enabled have size-based cleanup active. Returns FAIL when one or more databases have Query Store enabled with cleanup disabled.

Name: TempDB Unevenly Sized Data Files
Description: TempDB data files are not all the same size. For proportional fill to distribute I/O evenly across TempDB files, all data files should be equal in size. Unevenly sized files cause SQL Server to disproportionately fill larger files first.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

 SELECT    183 ,
     'tempdb' ,
     170 ,
     'File Configuration' ,
     'TempDB Unevenly Sized Data Files' ,
     'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tempdb' ,
     'TempDB data files are not configured with the same size.  Unevenly sized tempdb data files will result in unevenly sized workloads.'
WHERE ( SELECT COUNT (distinct [size])
FROM   tempdb.sys.database_files
WHERE  type_desc = 'ROWS'
HAVING MAX((size * 8) / (1024. * 1024)) - MIN((size * 8) / (1024. * 1024)) > 1.
       ) <> 1
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'TempDB Unevenly Sized Data Files' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'TempDB Unevenly Sized Data Files' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: No Failover Cluster Nodes Available
Description: Checks whether a Windows Server Failover Cluster has any nodes in a state other than Up. If all cluster nodes are active and none are standing by, a node failure means there is nowhere to fail over to. The cluster exists on paper but provides no protection.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN SERVERPROPERTY('IsClustered') = 1 AND NOT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_cluster_nodes WHERE is_current_owner = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more cluster nodes are not in an Up state. Check SELECT node_name, status, status_description FROM sys.dm_os_cluster_nodes. A cluster with no available passive node is not providing failover protection. Investigate the node condition and test failover in a maintenance window.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all cluster nodes are Up. Returns FAIL when one or more cluster nodes are in a non-Up state. Returns SKIP on standalone instances.

Name: MSDB Backup History Purged Too Frequently
Description: MSDB backup history is being purged very frequently (possibly daily or more). Overly aggressive history purging removes the ability to verify backup chains, audit backup history, or identify gaps in backup coverage over time.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT TOP 1
 186 AS CheckID ,
 'msdb' ,
 200 AS Priority ,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'MSDB Backup History Purged Too Frequently' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/history' AS URL ,
 ( 'Database backup history only retained back to '
 + CAST(bs.backup_start_date AS VARCHAR(20)) ) AS Details
FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs
ORDER BY backup_start_date ASC
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'MSDB Backup History Purged Too Frequently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'MSDB Backup History Purged Too Frequently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Endpoints Owned by Users
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Endpoints Owned by Users' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS ( 
SELECT
     187 AS [CheckID] ,
     230 AS [Priority] ,
     'Security' AS [FindingsGroup] ,
     'Endpoints Owned by Users' AS [Finding] ,
     'https://www.brentozar.com/go/owners' AS [URL] ,
     ( 'Endpoint ' + ep.[name] + ' is owned by ' + SUSER_NAME(ep.principal_id) + '. If the endpoint owner login is disabled or not available due to Active Directory problems, the high availability will stop working.'
     ) AS [Details]
FROM sys.database_mirroring_endpoints ep
LEFT OUTER JOIN sys.dm_server_services s ON SUSER_NAME(ep.principal_id) = s.service_account
WHERE s.service_account IS NULL AND ep.principal_id <> 1
) AND SERVERPROPERTY('IsHadrEnabled') = 1
THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Endpoints Owned by Users' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Endpoints Owned by Users' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Cost threshold for parallelism
Description: Checks the server-level "cost threshold for parallelism" sp_configure setting and flags values at or below the SQL Server install default of 5. The default was set in the SQL Server 2000 era when CPUs were single-core - on a modern server it forces tiny queries into parallel plans and inflates CXPACKET / CXCONSUMER waits. Most OLTP workloads run better with this value between 25 and 50.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'cost threshold for parallelism' AND value_in_use <= 5) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: It looks like the cost threshold for parallelism sits at the SQL Server 2000-era install default. Raise it to 50 for mixed OLTP / reporting (or 25 for pure OLTP) during a maintenance window with EXEC sp_configure 'cost threshold for parallelism', 50; RECONFIGURE. Capture wait stats before and after to validate the change.
ExpectedResult: PASS when cost threshold for parallelism is greater than 5. WARN when the value is at or below 5 - the SQL Server install default and almost always inappropriate for modern hardware. SKIP for SQL Server versions earlier than 2008.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process

Name: Missing Features
Description: SQL Server is missing recommended features or configurations for its version. This may indicate that version-specific features that improve performance, security, or reliability have not been enabled or configured after installation.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_windows_info WHERE windows_release <> '10' AND windows_sku < 7) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Missing Features' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Missing Features' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: TempDB File Error
Description: Detects a mismatch between TempDB file counts in sys.master_files and tempdb.sys.database_files. A count mismatch means SQL Server tried to add or remove a TempDB file but the change did not take effect — typically because the server was not restarted after the configuration change. TempDB errors affect every database on the instance.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
        
SELECT
191 AS [CheckID] ,
50 AS [Priority] ,
'Reliability' AS [FindingsGroup] ,
'TempDB File Error' AS [Finding] ,
'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tempdboops' AS [URL] ,
'Mismatch between the number of TempDB files in sys.master_files versus tempdb.sys.database_files' AS [Details]
WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sys.master_files WHERE database_id = 2) <> (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tempdb.sys.database_files)
   /* User may have no permissions to see tempdb files in sys.master_files. In that case count returned will be 0 and we want to skip the check */
   AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sys.master_files WHERE database_id = 2) <> 0
  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: TempDB file count is mismatched. The configured layout is not what SQL Server is actually using. Confirm all TempDB file paths exist on disk, then restart SQL Server during a maintenance window to apply the pending configuration.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when TempDB file count in sys.master_files matches tempdb.sys.database_files. Returns FAIL when a count mismatch is detected.

Name: Memory-Optimized TempDB Metadata Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 192' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_memory_optimized_enabled = 1 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 192' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 192' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Accelerated Database Recovery Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 193' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_accelerated_database_recovery_on = 1 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 193' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 193' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: CPU w/Odd Number of Cores
Description: SQL Server sees an odd or uneven number of CPU cores per NUMA node. Parallel query execution splits work across threads in even pairs - an unbalanced NUMA configuration means some parallel plans cannot distribute threads evenly, adding scheduler overhead on every parallel query. This is most commonly caused by a hypervisor vCPU assignment that was never reviewed by a DBA.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
        
SELECT 198 AS CheckID,
                  NULL AS DatabaseName,
                  10 AS Priority,
                  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
                  'CPU w/Odd Number of Cores' AS Finding,
                  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/oddity' AS URL,
                  'Node ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), parent_node_id) + ' has ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), COUNT(cpu_id))
                  + CASE WHEN COUNT(cpu_id) = 1 THEN ' core assigned to it. This is a really bad NUMA configuration.'
                         ELSE ' cores assigned to it. This is a really bad NUMA configuration.'
                    END AS Details
           FROM   sys.dm_os_schedulers
           WHERE  is_online = 1
                  AND scheduler_id < 255
                  AND parent_node_id < 64
      AND EXISTS (
         SELECT 1
         FROM ( SELECT    memory_node_id, SUM(online_scheduler_count) AS schedulers
                FROM      sys.dm_os_nodes
                WHERE     memory_node_id < 64
                GROUP  BY memory_node_id ) AS nodes
             HAVING MIN(nodes.schedulers) <> MAX(nodes.schedulers)
         )
           GROUP BY parent_node_id,
                  is_online
           HAVING ( COUNT(cpu_id) + 2 ) % 2 = 1


  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: It looks like NUMA nodes have uneven core counts. Raise this with the infrastructure team - the vCPU assignment needs correcting at the hypervisor so each NUMA node has an equal, even number of cores visible to SQL Server. This is a configuration change outside SQL Server itself.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all NUMA nodes have equal core counts. Returns FAIL when NUMA nodes have unequal or odd core counts.

Name: There Is An Error With The Default Trace
Description: Detects when the SQL Server default trace has stopped running. The default trace captures server-level events including logins, schema changes, security errors, and error messages. When it stops, those events are not recorded. Any post-incident investigation that relies on default trace history will find a gap.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.traces WHERE is_default = 1 AND status = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: The default trace has stopped. Server-level events including schema changes and security errors are not being recorded. Restart it with EXEC sp_trace_setstatus @traceid = 1, @status = 1. Investigate why it stopped and check the trace file path is accessible.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when the default trace is running. Returns FAIL when the default trace exists but is stopped.

Name: Encryption Certificates with Private Keys Not Backed Up
Description: Queries sys.certificates for certificates that have a private key and have not been backed up to an external location within the last 30 days. Loss of a TDE or backup-encryption certificate private key means permanent data loss - the encrypted data cannot be recovered by anyone. Database files are not recoverable. Take immediate action.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT DISTINCT 202 AS CheckID,
 1 AS Priority,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup,
 'Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' AS Finding,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tde' AS URL,
 'The certificate ' + c.name + ' is used to encrypt database backups. Last backup date: ' + COALESCE(CAST(c.pvt_key_last_backup_date AS VARCHAR(100)), 'Never') AS Details
 FROM sys.certificates c
 INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset bs ON c.thumbprint = bs.encryptor_thumbprint
 WHERE pvt_key_last_backup_date IS NULL OR pvt_key_last_backup_date <= DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())

 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Database files are not recoverable without this certificate. Run BACKUP CERTIFICATE [name] TO FILE = 'path' WITH PRIVATE KEY (FILE = 'key_path', ENCRYPTION BY PASSWORD = 'password') for each flagged certificate. Store the backup on a separate secured system and document the password in your credential manager.
ExpectedResult: PASS when all encryption certificates with private keys have been backed up within the last 30 days. FAIL when any certificate has pvt_key_last_backup_date IS NULL or older than 30 days.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Primary Account Number is Secured Where Stored

Name: Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently
Description: A database encryption certificate (used for TDE or column encryption) has not been backed up in the past 30 days. Without current certificate backups, encrypted databases or data cannot be decrypted or restored after a key loss.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT DISTINCT 202 AS CheckID,
 1 AS Priority,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup,
 'Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' AS Finding,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tde' AS URL,
 'The certificate ' + c.name + ' is used to encrypt database backups. Last backup date: ' + COALESCE(CAST(c.pvt_key_last_backup_date AS VARCHAR(100)), 'Never') AS Details
 FROM sys.certificates c
 INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset bs ON c.thumbprint = bs.encryptor_thumbprint
 WHERE pvt_key_last_backup_date IS NULL OR pvt_key_last_backup_date <= DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())

 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Encryption Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: DBCC Events
Description: DBCC commands with significant impact (such as DBCC CHECKDB, DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS, or DBCC SHRINKFILE) have been detected in the default trace. Frequent DBCC operations can cause performance issues and may indicate improper maintenance practices.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.fn_trace_getinfo(NULL) WHERE property = 5 AND value > 0 AND traceid <> 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'DBCC Events' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'DBCC Events' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Wait Stats Cleared Recently
Description: SQL Server wait statistics have been cleared recently - via DBCC SQLPERF or a server restart. Wait stats are the primary tool for diagnosing performance bottlenecks. A recent clear means the accumulated history is gone and trending is unreliable until the stats rebuild over time. It seems like someone cleared these intentionally, which is worth investigating.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats WHERE last_execution_time IS NULL AND creation_time < DATEADD(hh, -4, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Wait stats were cleared recently. Identify who cleared them and why - check the default trace. Rebuild a new baseline now. Avoid clearing wait stats on production; use a diff approach instead.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when wait stats appear to have accumulated normally. Returns FAIL when a recent clear is detected.

Name: Auto-Shrink Ran Recently
Description: The database auto-shrink feature has run recently, detected via the default trace. Auto-shrink is the most reliably harmful SQL Server database setting - it fragments every index it touches, runs unpredictably competing with production workloads, and immediately gives back the space it freed as the database grows again. It should be disabled on all production databases.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats(DB_ID(), NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL) AS ips INNER JOIN sys.indexes i ON ips.object_id = i.object_id AND ips.index_id = i.index_id WHERE ips.avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 30 AND DATEDIFF(dd, ISNULL(STATS_DATE(i.object_id, i.index_id), '19000101'), GETDATE()) < 7) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Auto-shrink is on and has run recently. Disable it now on all affected databases with ALTER DATABASE [name] SET AUTO_SHRINK OFF. Then run index maintenance to recover from the fragmentation it caused.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when auto-shrink has not run recently. Returns FAIL when a recent auto-shrink event is detected.

Name: DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Ran Recently
Description: DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS has been run recently, flushing all clean (unmodified) pages from the buffer pool. Every subsequent query reads its data from disk rather than cache until the buffer pool refills - this causes a significant but temporary performance hit on a production server. It looks like someone ran this for testing purposes and forgot the server was carrying production load.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats WHERE creation_time IS NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS ran recently on this server. Identify who ran it and why. If it was for testing, redirect that testing to a non-production instance. The buffer pool performance hit fades as the cache refills, but the root cause needs addressing.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no recent DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS is detected. Returns FAIL when the command has been run recently.

Name: DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Usage Advisory
Description:
This is an advisory check only. It cannot programmatically detect DROPCLEANBUFFERS execution because SQL Server does not expose this through DMVs. The check directs the DBA to review the SQL Server error log for DROPCLEANBUFFERS events.

DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS flushes the buffer cache, removing all clean data pages from memory. While it cannot be directly detected via T-SQL, it leaves evidence in the error log. Performance testing teams sometimes run this command to simulate cold cache scenarios, but in production it causes immediate performance degradation. The check performs automated detection through SQL Server Error Log, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets SQL Server Error Log and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
SqlQuery:
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
  CREATE TABLE #fnTraceGettable
   (
     TextData NVARCHAR(4000) ,
     DatabaseName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     EventClass INT ,
     Severity INT ,
     StartTime DATETIME ,
     EndTime DATETIME ,
     Duration BIGINT ,
     NTUserName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     NTDomainName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     HostName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     ApplicationName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     LoginName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     DBUserName NVARCHAR(256)
    );
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;

SELECT UPPER(
     REPLACE(
      SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), 0,
        ISNULL(
         NULLIF(
          CHARINDEX('(', CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)),
           0),
           LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )) --This replaces everything up to an open paren, if one exists.
          , SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )
        , '') --This replaces any optional WITH clause to a DBCC command, like tableresults.
     ) AS [dbcc_event_trunc_upper],
   UPPER(
    REPLACE(
     CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 ), '')) AS [dbcc_event_full_upper],
   MIN(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS min_start_time,
   MAX(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS max_start_time,
   t.NTUserName AS [nt_user_name],
   t.NTDomainName AS [nt_domain_name],
   t.HostName AS [host_name],
      t.ApplicationName AS [application_name],
   t.LoginName [login_name],
   t.DBUserName AS [db_user_name]
    INTO #dbcc_events_from_trace
      FROM #fnTraceGettable AS t
   WHERE t.EventClass = 116
 
BEGIN
     SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
SELECT 207 AS CheckID ,
             10 AS Priority ,
             'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
             'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Ran Recently' AS Finding ,
             'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/dbcc' AS URL ,
             'The user ' + COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name) + ' has run DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + ' times between ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30), MIN(d.min_start_time)) + ' and ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30),  MAX(d.max_start_time)) +
       '. If this is a production box, know that you''re clearing all data out of memory when this happens. What kind of monster would do that?'
       AS Details
     FROM    #dbcc_events_from_trace d
     WHERE d.dbcc_event_full_upper = N'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS'
     GROUP BY COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name)
     HAVING COUNT(*) > 0

  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
END
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Usage Advisory' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Usage Advisory' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: DBCC FREEPROCCACHE Ran Recently
Description: DBCC FREEPROCCACHE has been executed recently, clearing all cached execution plans from the procedure cache. This forces every query to recompile on its next execution, temporarily causing high CPU usage from mass recompilation.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats WHERE last_execution_time IS NOT NULL AND creation_time < DATEADD(dd, -7, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'DBCC FREEPROCCACHE Ran Recently' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'DBCC FREEPROCCACHE Ran Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: DBCC WRITEPAGE Used Recently
Description: Detects whether DBCC WRITEPAGE has been run recently on this instance. DBCC WRITEPAGE is an undocumented command that writes directly to database pages, bypassing SQL Server's normal I/O integrity stack. Any use of it in production is a red flag. It can silently corrupt data in ways that survive checkpoint and backup.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats AS qs CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(plan_handle) st WHERE st.text LIKE '%DBCC WRITEPAGE%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run DBCC CHECKDB on all databases immediately. Identify the session that ran DBCC WRITEPAGE via SQL Server Audit or trace. If no authorized use case exists, escalate as a potential data integrity incident.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no DBCC WRITEPAGE usage is found in cached query plans. Returns FAIL when detected - treat as a potential data integrity incident requiring immediate investigation.

Name: DBCC SHRINK% Ran Recently
Description: A DBCC SHRINKFILE or DBCC SHRINKDATABASE operation has been run recently. Database shrink operations cause severe index fragmentation, requiring index maintenance afterwards to restore performance. Running shrink manually on a production database signals that disk space is being managed reactively rather than planned - the fragmentation cost almost always exceeds the space recovered.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats AS qs CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(plan_handle) st WHERE st.text LIKE '%DBCC SHRINK%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: A DBCC SHRINK operation ran recently. Run index maintenance now on the affected database to repair fragmentation. Investigate what drove the space pressure and address that rather than shrinking again.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no recent DBCC SHRINK operations are detected. Returns FAIL when a recent shrink operation is found.

Name: Transparent Data Encryption Enabled
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 211' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_encrypted = 1 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 211' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 211' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Database Owned by SA Account
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 212' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d WHERE SUSER_SNAME(d.owner_sid) = 'sa') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 212' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 212' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Database Owner is Unknown
Description: One or more databases have an owner SID that resolves to NULL - meaning the original owner login no longer exists. An orphaned database owner breaks ownership-chained queries and fails silently during disaster recovery. It looks like this is a departed employee or deleted service account that nobody noticed.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT  213 AS CheckID ,
   [name] AS DatabaseName ,
   230 AS Priority ,
   'Security' AS FindingsGroup ,
   'Database Owner is Unknown' AS Finding ,
   '' AS URL ,
   ( 'Database name: ' + [name] + '   '
     + 'Owner name: ' + ISNULL(SUSER_SNAME(owner_sid),'~~ UNKNOWN ~~') ) AS Details
 FROM    sys.databases
 WHERE   SUSER_SNAME(owner_sid) is NULL
   
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Run SELECT name, owner_sid FROM sys.databases WHERE SUSER_SNAME(owner_sid) IS NULL to list affected databases. Reassign each with ALTER AUTHORIZATION ON DATABASE::DatabaseName TO sa. Audit how the owner account was deleted.
ExpectedResult: PASS when all databases have a resolvable owner SID. WARN when one or more databases have an orphaned owner where SUSER_SNAME(owner_sid) returns NULL. SKIP for SQL Server versions earlier than 2008.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Define and Maintain Role-Based Access Control

Name: Auto-Growth Events Detected
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 214' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.master_files WHERE growth > 128 AND is_percent_growth = 0 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 214' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 214' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Implicit Transactions
Description: Checks for active sessions running with IMPLICIT_TRANSACTIONS enabled. When implicit transactions are on, every DML statement opens a transaction that must be explicitly committed or rolled back. It seems like this setting gets inherited from application connection strings and never revisited. Left unchecked, it causes long-running open transactions and blocking chains that are hard to trace back to the root cause.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions WHERE transaction_isolation_level = 1 AND is_user_process = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Identify sessions using implicit transactions via sys.dm_exec_sessions. Set IMPLICIT_TRANSACTIONS OFF at the connection or application level. Verify no open transactions remain after making the change.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no user sessions are running with implicit transactions active. Returns INFO when sessions with implicit transactions are detected.

Name: Missing Features
Description: SQL Server is missing recommended features or settings for the installed version. Version-specific optimizations and features that improve performance, security, or manageability may not have been configured after installation or upgrade.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE compatibility_level < 100 AND state = 0 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Missing Features' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Missing Features' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Cumulative Update Available
Description:
Reports the current SQL Server product version using SERVERPROPERTY and advises checking for the latest cumulative update at the SQL Server builds blog. This is an informational check — it does not automatically determine if an update is available.

Running an unpatched SQL Server exposes the instance to known vulnerabilities and bugs that have been fixed in cumulative updates. The gap between current version and latest CU represents accumulated security patches, performance fixes, and stability improvements. The check performs automated detection through SERVERPROPERTY, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets SERVERPROPERTY and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
SqlQuery:
DECLARE @ProductVersionMajor DECIMAL(10,2) ,@ProductVersion NVARCHAR(128),@ProductVersionMinor DECIMAL(10,2)

SET @ProductVersion = CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('ProductVersion') AS NVARCHAR(128));
  SELECT @ProductVersionMajor = SUBSTRING(@ProductVersion, 1,CHARINDEX('.', @ProductVersion) + 1 )
,@ProductVersionMinor = PARSENAME(CONVERT(varchar(32), @ProductVersion), 2);

IF EXISTS (SELECT * FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES WHERE TABLE_NAME = 'SqlServerVersions' AND TABLE_TYPE = 'BASE TABLE')
BEGIN
     SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
        SELECT TOP 1 217, 100, 'Reliability', 'Cumulative Update Available', COALESCE(v.Url, 'https://SQLServerUpdates.com/'),
      v.MinorVersionName + ' was released on ' + CAST(CONVERT(DATETIME, v.ReleaseDate, 112) AS VARCHAR(100))
         FROM dbo.SqlServerVersions v
         WHERE v.MajorVersionNumber = @ProductVersionMajor
           AND v.MinorVersionNumber > @ProductVersionMinor
           AND  SERVERPROPERTY('EngineEdition') <> 8 
         ORDER BY v.MinorVersionNumber DESC     
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check SQL Server Cumulative Update Status' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check SQL Server Cumulative Update Status' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Cumulative Update Available
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Cumulative Update Available' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_server_services WHERE servicename LIKE 'SQL Server%' AND status = 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Cumulative Update Available' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Cumulative Update Available' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Objects created with dangerous SET Options
Description: Database objects (stored procedures, views, or functions) were created with dangerous SET options (such as ANSI_NULLS OFF or QUOTED_IDENTIFIER OFF). These settings can cause incorrect query results and prevent the use of indexed views and filtered indexes.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.objects WHERE OBJECTPROPERTY(object_id, 'ExecIsQuotedIdentOn') = 0 AND type IN('P','FN','IF','TF','V','TR')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Objects created with dangerous SET Options' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Objects created with dangerous SET Options' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Alerts Without Event Descriptions
Description: SQL Server Agent alerts are defined without an event description. Alerts without descriptions are harder to understand and respond to, and may indicate incomplete alert configuration left over from initial setup.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

   SELECT 219 AS CheckID
    ,200 AS [Priority]
    ,'Monitoring' AS FindingsGroup
    ,'Alerts Without Event Descriptions' AS Finding
    ,'https://www.brentozar.com/go/alert' AS [URL]
    ,('The following Alert is not including detailed event descriptions in its output messages: ' + QUOTENAME([name])
    + '. You can fix it by ticking the relevant boxes in its Properties --> Options page.') AS Details
   FROM msdb.dbo.sysalerts
   WHERE [enabled] = 1
     AND include_event_description = 0
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Alerts Without Event Descriptions' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Alerts Without Event Descriptions' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Last Good DBCC CHECKDB Overdue
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 220' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 AND (SELECT DATEDIFF(dd, d.create_date, GETDATE()) > 30)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 220' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 220' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Server restarted in last 24 hours
Description: Detects whether SQL Server was restarted within the last 24 hours by checking tempdb's creation date. A very recent restart means DMV data, wait statistics, and cached plan history are incomplete. Other checks run in the same session may undercount problems because the counters have not had time to accumulate.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases d CROSS APPLY (SELECT create_date FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id = 2) t WHERE DATEDIFF(HH, t.create_date, GETDATE()) < 24) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Confirm the restart was planned. If unplanned, investigate the SQL Server error log and Windows Event Log before acting on any DMV-based findings from this session. Re-run the health check after 24-48 hours of uptime.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when the server has been running for more than 24 hours. Returns WARN when restarted within 24 hours, flagging that DMV data may be incomplete for this session.

Name: SQL Server Agent Alerts Not Configured
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 222' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.sysalerts a ON j.job_id = a.job_id WHERE j.enabled = 1 AND a.id IS NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 222' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 222' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Urgent Maintenance Required
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 223' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory WHERE run_status = 0 AND run_date > CONVERT(VARCHAR(8), DATEADD(dd, -1, GETDATE()), 112)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 223' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 223' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Maintenance Plan Configuration Issues
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 224' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs WHERE qs.last_execution_time < DATEADD(mi, -720, GETDATE()) AND qs.execution_count = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 224' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 224' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Maintenance Configuration Status
Description:
Returns a generic informational message. No DMV query or automated evaluation is performed. This check serves as a placeholder that can be customized with organization-specific maintenance validation logic.

This is a placeholder advisory check that provides no automated analysis. It exists as a hook point for organizations to insert their own maintenance validation logic. The check performs automated detection through None (advisory), providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets None (advisory) and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Maintenance Configuration Status' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Maintenance Configuration Status' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Resumable Index Operation Paused
Description: One or more resumable index build or rebuild operations are currently paused. Paused resumable index operations hold schema modification locks and consume space for temporary data structures. They should be resumed or aborted to release resources.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.index_resumable_operations) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Resumable Index Operation Paused' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Resumable Index Operation Paused' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Large Plan Cache Detected
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 226' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_cached_plans WHERE size_in_bytes > 1048576 AND cacheobjtype = 'Compiled Plan') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 226' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 226' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Query Plan Cache Health Check
Description: Counts all cached execution plans in sys.dm_exec_cached_plans and reports the total. Defers to sp_BlitzCache for detailed analysis of single-use plans, high-CPU plans, and parameter-sensitive queries. A large plan cache is normal, but high single-use ad-hoc plan accumulation can indicate memory pressure or application-level parameterization problems.
RecommendedAction: Run sp_BlitzCache to identify high-impact cached plans. Enable Optimize for Ad Hoc Workloads if single-use plan counts are elevated. Monitor plan cache memory consumption against total buffer pool size.
ExpectedResult: Returns INFO with total cached plan count. No FAIL branch — this check is informational only.

Name: SQL Server Evaluation Edition Detection
Description:
Checks SERVERPROPERTY(Edition) to determine if the instance is running Evaluation Edition. Fails immediately if so. Passes for all licensed editions (Enterprise, Standard, Developer, Express).

SQL Server Evaluation Edition has a 180-day time-limited license. When it expires, the SQL Server service stops functioning. Running Evaluation Edition for production workloads risks unexpected service termination, which is a business continuity incident. The check performs automated detection through SERVERPROPERTY, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets SERVERPROPERTY and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
 EXISTS (             
      SELECT 229 AS CheckID,
             1 AS Priority,
             'Reliability' AS FindingsGroup,
             'Evaluation Edition' AS Finding,
             'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/workgroup' AS URL,
             'This server will stop working on: ' + CAST(CONVERT(DATETIME, DATEADD(DD, 180, create_date), 102) AS VARCHAR(100)) AS details
      FROM   sys.server_principals
      WHERE sid = 0x010100000000000512000000 
 ) AND CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('Edition') AS NVARCHAR(4000)) LIKE '%Evaluation%'
    THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'SQL Server Evaluation Edition Detection' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'SQL Server Evaluation Edition Detection' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Evaluation Edition
Description: SQL Server is running the Evaluation edition, which expires 180 days after installation. When it expires, SQL Server will not start. This instance needs a licensed edition before the expiry date or production will go down completely - no failover, no restore, no DR until it is relicensed.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
 EXISTS (             
      SELECT 229 AS CheckID,
             1 AS Priority,
             'Reliability' AS FindingsGroup,
             'Evaluation Edition' AS Finding,
             'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/workgroup' AS URL,
             'This server will stop working on: ' + CAST(CONVERT(DATETIME, DATEADD(DD, 180, create_date), 102) AS VARCHAR(100)) AS details
      FROM   sys.server_principals
      WHERE sid = 0x010100000000000512000000 
 ) AND CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('Edition') AS NVARCHAR(4000)) LIKE '%Evaluation%'
    THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Check the expiry date from check output. Procure the appropriate SQL Server license. Apply via SQL Server Installation Center > Maintenance > Edition Upgrade. Requires a planned service restart.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when SQL Server is not running Evaluation edition. Returns FAIL when Evaluation is detected, including the expiry date. Requires immediate licensing action to prevent a complete service outage.

Name: Invalid login defined with Windows Authentication
Description: One or more SQL Server logins mapped to Windows users or groups that no longer exist in Active Directory (invalid or deleted accounts). These orphaned logins pose a security risk and should be removed to prevent unauthorized access attempts.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals WHERE type = 'S' AND principal_id > 4 AND name NOT LIKE '##%' AND name NOT LIKE 'NT %') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Invalid login defined with Windows Authentication' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Invalid login defined with Windows Authentication' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Service Broker Enabled on a Database
Description: Detects databases where Service Broker is enabled, which deviates from the SQL Server default (disabled). Service Broker provides asynchronous message queuing and reliable messaging between databases. While it is a powerful feature for building distributed applications, enabling it creates additional background processing overhead and requires careful management of message queues, transmission queues, and endpoints. This check surfaces databases with non-default broker configuration for review.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.databases d
    WHERE d.database_id > 4
      AND DB_NAME(d.database_id) != 'rdsadmin'
      AND d.[state] = 0
      AND (d.is_broker_enabled <> 0 OR d.is_broker_enabled IS NULL)
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Disable Service Broker if not actively used for messaging or asynchronous processing.
If Service Broker is required, ensure transmission queues are monitored and maintained.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no user databases have Service Broker enabled. Returns FAIL otherwise.

Name: Unused Indexes Detected
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 232' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT i.name, OBJECT_NAME(i.object_id) AS tbl, s.user_seeks, s.user_scans, s.user_lookups FROM sys.indexes i LEFT JOIN sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats s ON i.object_id = s.object_id AND i.index_id = s.index_id AND s.database_id = DB_ID() WHERE i.type > 1 AND i.is_primary_key = 0 AND i.is_unique_constraint = 0 AND i.is_unique = 0 AND (s.user_seeks + s.user_scans + s.user_lookups) = 0 AND s.user_updates > 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 232' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 232' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Detect Unused or Rarely-Used Indexes
Description:
Joins sys.indexes with sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats for the current database. Identifies user indexes that have never been used for user reads (no seeks, scans, or lookups recorded since last restart) or have been used fewer than 10 times total.

Unused indexes consume disk space, slow down INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations (every index must be maintained on data changes), and waste buffer pool memory caching index pages that are never read. Rarely-used indexes (fewer than 10 user operations) are maintenance overhead with minimal benefit. The check performs automated detection through sys.indexes + sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets sys.indexes + sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Detect Unused or Rarely-Used Indexes' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Detect Unused or Rarely-Used Indexes' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Memory Leak in USERSTORE_TOKENPERM Cache
Description: Checks whether the USERSTORE_TOKENPERM memory clerk is consuming 10% or more of total SQL Server memory. This clerk stores permission tokens and tends to grow unbounded in environments with frequent permission changes, heavy ad-hoc query patterns, or cross-database ownership chains. It seems like this one never gets caught until the server starts throwing out-of-memory errors. When it hits 10% of the buffer pool, query performance degrades visibly.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (             
SELECT 233 AS CheckID,
              50 AS Priority,
              'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
              'Memory Leak in USERSTORE_TOKENPERM Cache' AS Finding,
              'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/userstore' AS URL,
              N'UserStore_TokenPerm clerk is using ' + CAST(CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'USERSTORE_TOKENPERM' AND name = 'TokenAndPermUserStore' THEN pages_kb * 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS INT) AS NVARCHAR(100)) 
             + N'GB RAM, total buffer pool is ' + CAST(CAST(SUM(pages_kb) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 AS INT) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + N'GB.'
           AS details
       FROM sys.dm_os_memory_clerks
       HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'USERSTORE_TOKENPERM' AND name = 'TokenAndPermUserStore' THEN pages_kb * 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) / SUM(pages_kb) >= 0.1     
         AND SUM(pages_kb) / 1024.0 / 1024.0 >= 1 /* At least 1GB RAM overall */
  ) AND EXISTS (SELECT * FROM sys.all_columns WHERE object_id = OBJECT_ID('sys.dm_os_memory_clerks') AND name = 'pages_kb')
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Run DBCC FREESYSTEMCACHE ('TokenAndPermUserStore') to flush the cache immediately. Investigate ad-hoc query patterns and cross-database ownership chains that drive unbounded growth.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when USERSTORE_TOKENPERM is below 10% of total SQL Server memory. Returns INFO when the threshold is exceeded on a server with at least 1 GB RAM.

Name: SQL Server Update May Fail
Description: A user database has a logical file name of 'master', which conflicts with the system database file name the SQL Server installer expects. This causes CU and SP installations to fail mid-way, leaving the instance in a partially patched state. The fix takes seconds in SSMS and requires no downtime.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
  EXISTS (             
    SELECT 234 AS CheckID,
           100 AS Priority,
        db_name(f.database_id) AS DatabaseName,
           'Reliability' AS FindingsGroup,
           'SQL Server Update May Fail' AS Finding,
           'https://desertdba.com/failovers-cant-serve-two-masters/' AS URL,
           'This database has a file with a logical name of ''master'', which can break SQL Server updates. Rename it in SSMS by right-clicking on the database, go into Properties, and rename the file. Takes effect instantly.' AS details
    FROM master.sys.master_files f
    WHERE (f.name = N'master')
      AND f.database_id > 4
      AND db_name(f.database_id) <> 'master'
  ) 
     THEN 1
  ELSE 0
 END
RecommendedAction: In SSMS, right-click the affected database > Properties > Files. Rename the logical file from 'master' to something unique (e.g. '<dbname>_data'). Takes effect instantly with no restart required.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no user databases have a logical file named 'master'. Returns FAIL with the database name when the conflict is detected - rename before the next patch window.

Name: Inconsistent Query Store metadata
Description: Checks whether any database has a Query Store actual_state that does not match its desired_state. This mismatch means Query Store tried to switch into a mode (typically Read/Write) but something blocked it — often disk space pressure or internal corruption. When Query Store is stuck in the wrong state, execution plan forcing stops working and the Query Store data collected is unreliable.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE actual_state <> desired_state) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Identify databases with state mismatch via sys.database_query_store_options. Check disk space first. Clear and re-enable Query Store on affected databases if state is ERROR.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all databases have Query Store actual_state matching desired_state. Returns INFO when a mismatch is detected.

Name: Snapshotting Too Many Databases
Description: SQL Server is creating database snapshots on a large number of databases simultaneously. Mass snapshotting can cause excessive I/O due to copy-on-write operations for all databases being snapshotted, impacting performance of production workloads.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT TOP 1 236 AS CheckID ,
 50 AS Priority ,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Snapshotting Too Many Databases' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/toomanysnaps' AS URL ,
 ( CAST(SUM(1) AS VARCHAR(20)) + ' databases snapshotted at once in the last two weeks, indicating that IO may be freezing up. Microsoft does not recommend VSS snaps for 35 or more databases.') AS Details
FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs
WHERE bs.type = 'D'
AND bs.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -14, GETDATE()) /* In the last 2 weeks */
GROUP BY bs.backup_finish_date
HAVING SUM(1) >= 35
ORDER BY SUM(1) DESC
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Snapshotting Too Many Databases' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Snapshotting Too Many Databases' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: BATCH_MODE_ADAPTIVE_JOINS Non-Default Value
Description: Detects when the database-scoped configuration BATCH_MODE_ADAPTIVE_JOINS is not set to its default value of 1 (enabled). This feature allows the query optimizer to defer the choice of hash join or nested loop join algorithm until the first input has been scanned, improving plan quality for parameter-sensitive queries. Disabling it may degrade query performance for workloads that benefit from adaptive join selection.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.database_scoped_configurations dsc
    WHERE dsc.configuration_id = 9
      AND (CAST(dsc.value AS NVARCHAR(100)) <> '1' OR dsc.value IS NULL)
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Set BATCH_MODE_ADAPTIVE_JOINS to the default value of 1 unless directed otherwise by Microsoft support.
Run ALTER DATABASE SCOPED CONFIGURATION SET BATCH_MODE_ADAPTIVE_JOINS = ON in affected databases.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when BATCH_MODE_ADAPTIVE_JOINS is set to the default value of 1. Returns FAIL otherwise.

Name: DEFERRED_COMPILATION_TV Non-Default Value
Description: Detects when the database-scoped configuration DEFERRED_COMPILATION_TV is not set to its default value of 1 (enabled). This feature defers compilation of table-valued functions (TVFs) and table-valued parameters until the first execution time, allowing the optimizer to use actual cardinality estimates instead of default guesses. Disabling it reverts to the legacy behavior of using fixed cardinality estimates, which can cause poor query plan choices when TVFs return variable row counts.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.database_scoped_configurations dsc
    WHERE dsc.configuration_id = 19
      AND (CAST(dsc.value AS NVARCHAR(100)) <> '1' OR dsc.value IS NULL)
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Set DEFERRED_COMPILATION_TV to the default value of 1 using ALTER DATABASE SCOPED CONFIGURATION.
If disabled due to a plan regression, test with the latest SQL Server CU before keeping it off.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when DEFERRED_COMPILATION_TV is set to the default value of 1. Returns FAIL otherwise.

Name: LIGHTWEIGHT_QUERY_PROFILING Non-Default Value
Description: Detects when the database-scoped configuration LIGHTWEIGHT_QUERY_PROFILING is not set to its default value of 1 (enabled). This feature provides per-operator query execution statistics with minimal overhead and is the foundation for features like the Query Store wait statistics, last query plan stats, and the sys.dm_exec_query_stats infrastructure. Disabling it removes critical performance diagnostic capabilities from the database.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.database_scoped_configurations dsc
    WHERE dsc.configuration_id = 22
      AND (CAST(dsc.value AS NVARCHAR(100)) <> '1' OR dsc.value IS NULL)
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Set LIGHTWEIGHT_QUERY_PROFILING to the default value of 1 using ALTER DATABASE SCOPED CONFIGURATION.
If disabled, verify that no performance monitoring tool dependencies require this feature.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when LIGHTWEIGHT_QUERY_PROFILING is set to the default value of 1. Returns FAIL otherwise.

Name: Log Backups to NUL
Description: Transaction log backups are being taken but written to the NUL device, effectively discarding the backup. While this truncates the transaction log, the log backup is not recoverable, eliminating the ability to perform point-in-time recovery.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
       WHEN EXISTS (
       SELECT DISTINCT
 256 AS CheckID ,
 d.name AS DatabaseName,
 1 AS Priority ,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Log Backups to NUL' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/nul' AS URL ,
 N'The transaction log file has been backed up ' + CAST((SELECT count(*)
  FROM msdb.dbo.backupset AS b INNER JOIN
  msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily AS bmf
   ON b.media_set_id = bmf.media_set_id
  WHERE b.database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = d.name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
  AND bmf.physical_device_name = 'NUL'
  AND b.type = 'L'
  AND b.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(dd,
   -7, GETDATE())) AS NVARCHAR(8)) + ' time(s) to ''NUL'' in the last week, which means the backup does not exist. This breaks point-in-time recovery.' AS Details
FROM master.sys.databases AS d
WHERE d.recovery_model IN ( 1, 2 )
 AND d.database_id NOT IN ( 2, 3 )
 AND d.source_database_id IS NULL
 AND d.state NOT IN(1, 6, 10) /* Not currently offline or restoring, like log shipping databases */
 AND d.is_in_standby = 0 /* Not a log shipping target database */
 AND d.source_database_id IS NULL /* Excludes database snapshots */
 --AND d.name NOT IN ( SELECT DISTINCT
 --  DatabaseName
 --  FROM #SkipChecks
 --  WHERE CheckID IS NULL OR CheckID = 2)
 AND EXISTS ( SELECT *
  FROM msdb.dbo.backupset AS b INNER JOIN
  msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily AS bmf
   ON b.media_set_id = bmf.media_set_id
  WHERE d.name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = b.database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
  AND bmf.physical_device_name = 'NUL'
  AND b.type = 'L'
  AND b.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(dd,
     -7, GETDATE()) )
       ) THEN 1
       ELSE 0
      END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Log Backups to NUL' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Log Backups to NUL' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Recovery Interval Not Optimal
Description: The Recovery Interval server configuration option is set to a non-default value. An overly high value means longer recovery time after a crash; too low causes excessive I/O from frequent checkpoints impacting normal workload performance.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN  EXISTS(SELECT
               1/0
           FROM sys.all_columns AS ac
           WHERE ac.name = 'target_recovery_time_in_seconds'
           AND   ac.object_id = OBJECT_ID('sys.databases'))
     AND EXISTS ( 
     SELECT
  DatabaseName = 
                       d.name,
                   CheckId = 
                       257,
                   Priority = 
                       50,
                   FindingsGroup = 
                       N'Performance',
                   Finding =
                       N'Recovery Interval Not Optimal',
                   URL = 
                       N'https://sqlperformance.com/2020/05/system-configuration/0-to-60-switching-to-indirect-checkpoints',
                   Details = 
                       N'The database ' +
                       QUOTENAME(d.name) +
                       N' has a target recovery interval of ' +
                       RTRIM(d.target_recovery_time_in_seconds) + 
                       CASE
                           WHEN d.target_recovery_time_in_seconds = 0
                           THEN N', which is a legacy default, and should be changed to 60.'
                           WHEN d.target_recovery_time_in_seconds <> 0 
                           THEN N', which is probably a mistake, and should be changed to 60.'
                       END
               FROM sys.databases AS d
               WHERE d.database_id > 4
               AND   d.is_read_only = 0
               AND   d.is_in_standby = 0
               AND   d.target_recovery_time_in_seconds <> 60
    
  )
  THEN 1
  ELSE 0
 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Recovery Interval Not Optimal' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Recovery Interval Not Optimal' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Dangerous Service Account - SQL Server Running as LocalSystem
Description: Detects when the SQL Server database engine service runs under LocalSystem or NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM. These accounts have unrestricted access to every file, registry key, and network resource on the host. Anyone who can run xp_cmdshell - or inject SQL as a sysadmin - can do literally anything on the server.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_services
    WHERE ([service_account] = 'LocalSystem'
       OR LOWER([service_account]) = 'nt authority\system')
      AND [servicename] LIKE 'SQL Server%'
      AND [servicename] NOT LIKE 'SQL Server Agent%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: SQL Server running as LocalSystem is a full server compromise waiting to happen. Change the service account to a low-privilege domain account or gMSA via SQL Server Configuration Manager. Do not use Windows Services directly - permissions will not be correctly updated.
ExpectedResult: PASS when SQL Server database engine service is not running as LocalSystem or NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM. FAIL when running under either high-privilege account.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts
Ensure the SQL Server Service Account is Not an Administrator
Access to System Components and Data is Appropriately Defined and Assigned

Name: Dangerous Service Account - SQL Agent Running as LocalSystem
Description: Detects when the SQL Server Agent service runs under LocalSystem or NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM. SQL Agent executes scheduled jobs including PowerShell and CmdExec steps. Running as LocalSystem means any job step - including one created by a malicious insider or attacker - runs with full administrative privileges on the host.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
    SELECT 1
    FROM sys.dm_server_services
    WHERE ([service_account] = 'LocalSystem'
       OR LOWER([service_account]) = 'nt authority\system')
      AND [servicename] LIKE 'SQL Server Agent%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: SQL Agent running as LocalSystem means anyone who can create or modify a job can run arbitrary commands on the host. Change the Agent service account to a low-privilege domain account via SQL Server Configuration Manager. Use Proxy accounts for job steps that need elevated access to specific resources.
ExpectedResult: PASS when SQL Agent service is not running as LocalSystem or NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM. FAIL when running under either high-privilege account.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts
Ensure the SQL Server Agent Service Account is Not an Administrator
Access to System Components and Data is Appropriately Defined and Assigned

Name: SQL Server Error Log Contains Critical Entries
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Check 260' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Check 260' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 260' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: SQL Server Error Log Health Advisory
Description:
Advisory check only. Directs the DBA to review the SQL Server error log via sp_readerrorlog for critical events. No automated analysis is performed because error log parsing requires pattern matching that is version-specific.

The SQL Server error log contains critical events: hardware errors, I/O failures, access violations, and stack dumps. Without periodic review, these events accumulate undetected until a failure occurs. This advisory directs the DBA to manually inspect the error log. The check performs automated detection through SQL Server Error Log, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets SQL Server Error Log and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'SQL Server Error Log Health Advisory' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'SQL Server Error Log Health Advisory' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: SQL Server Agent Job Failures in Last 24 Hours
Description:
Joins msdb.dbo.sysjobsteps with msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory to count job step failures (run_status = 0) in the last 24 hours. Fails if any job step failure is detected.

SQL Server Agent jobs automate critical maintenance: backups, index rebuilds, statistics updates, integrity checks, and data warehouse ETL. A job failure that goes undetected for 24 hours means any of these tasks may have silently stopped. Backup job failure is the most serious — it creates a growing data-loss window. The check performs automated detection through msdb.dbo.sysjobsteps + sysjobhistory, providing a binary or tiered verdict with specific messaging that identifies the deviation. This enables integration into monitoring pipelines for automated alerting within 24 hours of any change.

The query targets msdb.dbo.sysjobsteps + sysjobhistory and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO/WARN verdict.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs j INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory h ON j.job_id = h.job_id WHERE j.enabled = 1 AND h.run_status = 0 AND h.run_date >= CONVERT(VARCHAR(8), DATEADD(dd, -1, GETDATE()), 112)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'SQL Server Agent Job Failures in Last 24 Hours' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'SQL Server Agent Job Failures in Last 24 Hours' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE wait_stats_capture_mode = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END

ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Query Store Wait Stats Disabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
