Name: AG Replica Falling Behind
Description: An Always On Availability Group secondary replica is falling behind the primary in log redo. A lagging secondary means the HA/DR protection window is widening, and in the event of a primary failure, data loss may occur.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (             
SELECT 268 AS CheckID,
              5 AS Priority,
           DB_NAME(ps.database_id),
              'Availability' AS FindingsGroup,
              'AG Replica Falling Behind' AS Finding,
              'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/ag' AS URL,
              ag.name + N' AG replica server ' + 
          ar.replica_server_name + N' is ' + 
          CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(SECOND, ISNULL (drs.last_commit_time, drs.Last_hardened_time), ps.last_commit_time) < 200 THEN (CAST(DATEDIFF(SECOND, drs.last_commit_time, ps.last_commit_time) AS NVARCHAR(10)) + N' seconds ')
          ELSE (CAST(DATEDIFF(MINUTE, ISNULL (drs.last_commit_time, drs.Last_hardened_time), ps.last_commit_time) AS NVARCHAR(10)) + N' minutes ') END
          + N' behind the primary.'
          AS details
       FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states AS drs
       JOIN sys.availability_replicas AS ar ON drs.replica_id = ar.replica_id
       JOIN sys.availability_groups AS ag ON ar.group_id = ag.group_id
       JOIN sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states AS ps 
        ON drs.group_id = ps.group_id 
        AND drs.database_id = ps.database_id
        AND ps.is_local = 1 /* Primary */
       WHERE drs.is_local = 0 /* Secondary */
         AND DATEDIFF(SECOND,ISNULL (drs.last_commit_time, drs.Last_hardened_time), ps.last_commit_time) > 60
  ) 
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'AG Replica Falling Behind' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'AG Replica Falling Behind' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Memory Dangerous Low Recently
Description: Checks the SQL Server ring buffer for recent RESOURCE_MEMPHYSICAL_LOW signals, which indicate the server OS has recently notified SQL Server that physical memory was dangerously low. This condition forces SQL Server to aggressively flush the buffer pool to release memory to the OS. It seems like this is the kind of thing that shows up in ring buffer history long after the pain has passed — by the time you see it, queries have already failed.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_ring_buffers WHERE ring_buffer_type = 'RING_BUFFER_RESOURCE_MONITOR' AND CONVERT(XML, record).value('(./Record/ResourceMonitor/Indicators)[1]', 'varchar(max)') LIKE '%RESOURCE_MEMPHYSICAL_LOW%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Review max server memory configuration to leave adequate RAM for the OS. Identify other processes competing for memory on the host. Check SQL Server error log for 701 or 8645 errors.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no RESOURCE_MEMPHYSICAL_LOW signals are present in the ring buffer. Returns INFO when the signal is detected.

Name: TempDB Governor Config Problem
Description: TempDB has a configuration problem related to metadata optimization or governor settings. Misconfigurations can prevent TempDB from scaling properly under high concurrency workloads, leading to allocation contention and performance degradation.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_db_task_space_usage WHERE session_id > 50 GROUP BY session_id HAVING SUM(user_objects_alloc_page_count + internal_objects_alloc_page_count) > 10240) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'TempDB Governor Config Problem' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'TempDB Governor Config Problem' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Optimized Locking Not Fully Set Up
Description: Checks whether Read Committed Snapshot Isolation (RCSI) is disabled on user databases. Optimized Locking (SQL Server 2022+) requires RCSI to be enabled to deliver its full lock-reduction benefits. Without RCSI, Optimized Locking operates in a degraded mode and row-level lock elimination does not fully apply.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_read_committed_snapshot_on = 0 AND database_id > 4 AND state = 0 AND source_database_id IS NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Enable RCSI on user databases via ALTER DATABASE [dbname] SET READ_COMMITTED_SNAPSHOT ON during a maintenance window. Monitor tempdb growth after enabling.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all online user databases have RCSI enabled. Returns INFO when databases with RCSI disabled are found.

Name: System Uptime

This check validates a key SQL Server configuration or operational state by querying sys.dm_os_sys_info. The setting or condition monitored here directly affects instance behavior, performance, or security posture. Regular validation ensures that drift from approved baselines is detected before it causes incidents.

Without regular validation, configuration drift accumulates silently across SQL Server instances. Changes made for one-time troubleshooting or testing are often never reverted, creating a growing gap between the documented baseline and the actual running state. The check provides an automated detection mechanism that can be integrated into monitoring pipelines, CI/CD deployment validation, and periodic compliance audits. When non-compliant values are detected, the check produces actionable output identifying the specific deviation rather than a generic warning.

The query targets sys.dm_os_sys_info and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO verdict, and the message column identifies which specific items require attention.

Name: Memory RM Notifications
Description:
This check validates a key SQL Server configuration or operational state by querying sys.configurations. The setting or condition monitored here directly affects instance behavior, performance, or security posture. Regular validation ensures that drift from approved baselines is detected before it causes incidents.

Without regular validation, configuration drift accumulates silently across SQL Server instances. Changes made for one-time troubleshooting or testing are often never reverted, creating a growing gap between the documented baseline and the actual running state. The check provides an automated detection mechanism that can be integrated into monitoring pipelines, CI/CD deployment validation, and periodic compliance audits. When non-compliant values are detected, the check produces actionable output identifying the specific deviation rather than a generic warning.

The query targets sys.configurations and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO verdict, and the message column identifies which specific items require attention.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Memory RM Notifications' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Memory RM Notifications' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: More Indexes than Columns
Description:
This check validates a key SQL Server configuration or operational state by querying sys.indexes. The setting or condition monitored here directly affects instance behavior, performance, or security posture. Regular validation ensures that drift from approved baselines is detected before it causes incidents.

Without regular validation, configuration drift accumulates silently across SQL Server instances. Changes made for one-time troubleshooting or testing are often never reverted, creating a growing gap between the documented baseline and the actual running state. The check provides an automated detection mechanism that can be integrated into monitoring pipelines, CI/CD deployment validation, and periodic compliance audits. When non-compliant values are detected, the check produces actionable output identifying the specific deviation rather than a generic warning.

The query targets sys.indexes and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO verdict, and the message column identifies which specific items require attention.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'More Indexes than Columns' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'More Indexes than Columns' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Tables with more Indexes than Cols
Description: This check identifies tables with more indexes than cols. This check identifies tables with more indexes than cols. Tables with more Indexes than Cols - Microsoft Tiger Team BP Check (Index and Stats checks)
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.objects o INNER JOIN sys.indexes i ON o.object_id = i.object_id WHERE o.type = 'U' GROUP BY o.object_id HAVING COUNT(*) > (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sys.columns WHERE object_id = o.object_id)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Tables with more Indexes than Cols' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Tables with more Indexes than Cols' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Database Corruption Detected
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Database Corruption Detected' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WHERE event_type IN (1, 2, 3))
  THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Database Corruption Detected' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.

Name: Blocking Detected
Description: Detects active blocking chains on the instance right now. Blocking stacks up fast - one long-running transaction can park dozens of user connections and cause timeouts within minutes. It seems like this has never been looked at on most servers we assess.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_waiting_tasks wt WHERE wt.blocking_session_id > 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Find the head blocker: SELECT blocking_session_id, session_id, wait_type, wait_time FROM sys.dm_exec_requests WHERE blocking_session_id > 0. Capture the blocking query text before killing. Fix the root cause in application transaction scope.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no blocking is detected. Returns INFO when active blocking chains exist and require DBA review.

Name: SQL Server TCP Listener Should Not Bind to All Interfaces on Untrusted Networks
Description: Checks whether SQL Server has active TCP listeners that could be reachable on public or untrusted network interfaces. A listener bound to all interfaces (0.0.0.0 or ::) on a host with public IP exposure looks like an open invitation to brute-force and exploitation attempts. This check surfaces all active TCP listener ports so the DBA can confirm each binding is intentional and firewall-protected.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_tcp_listener_states
  WHERE state = 0 AND ip_address NOT IN ('127.0.0.1', '::1')
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Review each listener returned by SELECT * FROM sys.dm_tcp_listener_states. Disable listeners bound to public interfaces or restrict access via firewall.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all online TCP listeners are bound to loopback addresses

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure SQL Server is not exposed on public interfaces
Inbound traffic to the CDE is restricted

Name: Availability Group Endpoints Should Require Encryption
Description: FAILs when any AG/mirroring endpoint has encryption disabled or set to algorithm=NONE. Binary check — encryption on or off. Distinct from NEW_087, which checks that the encryption algorithm is specifically AES (not RC4 or RC4_128). Use this check to confirm encryption is enabled; pair with NEW_087 to also confirm algorithm strength. When encryption is only supported but not required, a connecting replica could negotiate no encryption at all, leaving AG redo traffic and log-ship streams in plaintext on the wire. This check flags any endpoint where ALL_ENCRYPTION_REQUIRED is not enforced.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_mirroring_endpoints WHERE encryption_algorithm > 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: For each endpoint flagged, run `ALTER ENDPOINT [EndpointName] FOR DATABASE_MIRRORING (ENCRYPTION = REQUIRED ALGORITHM AES);`. Both sides of an AG pair must be altered simultaneously to avoid connection drops. Verify with `SELECT name, is_encryption_enabled, encryption_algorithm_desc FROM sys.database_mirroring_endpoints`.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all AG/mirroring endpoints have encryption enabled and algorithm is not NONE. Returns FAIL when any endpoint has encryption disabled or NONE algorithm.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Strong cryptography used to safeguard PAN during transmission

Name: SQL Browser Service Should Be Disabled on Production Instances Using Static Ports
Description: Checks whether the SQL Browser service appears to be active on a production instance using static ports. The browser service is only needed for named instances or dynamic ports. Leaving it running on a default-instance static-port server adds an unnecessary network surface without any client benefit.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_server_registry
  WHERE registry_key LIKE '%SQLBrowser%' AND value_name = 'Start' AND value_data = '2'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Stop and disable the SQL Browser service via Services.msc or PowerShell: Set-Service SQLBrowser -StartupType Disabled -Status Stopped
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when the SQL Browser service is not configured to auto-start

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Manage Default Accounts on Enterprise Assets and Software
Ensure the SQL Browser service is disabled

Name: TDS Client Connections Should Use Encryption
Description: Checks whether active client connections are using TDS encryption. Unencrypted TDS sessions transmit queries, results, and credentials in plaintext, making them readable by any host on the same network path. This check counts connections where encrypt_option is FALSE or NOT SUP and fails if unencrypted connections represent more than a trivial fraction of active sessions.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_connections WHERE encrypt_option = 'FALSE' AND session_id > 50) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Enable force encryption in SQL Server Configuration Manager: SQL Server Network Configuration > Protocols for [Instance] > Properties > Force Encryption = Yes. Restart the SQL Server service. Update all application connection strings to include `Encrypt=True;TrustServerCertificate=False;`. Install a valid TLS certificate on the SQL Server host to avoid certificate errors. Verify with `SELECT session_id, encrypt_option, net_transport FROM sys.dm_exec_connections WHERE session_id > 50`.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all active external client connections show encrypt_option = TRUE. Returns FAIL when any active external connection is unencrypted.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Strong cryptography used to safeguard PAN during transmission

ValidationSources.source: Microsoft Learn - Enable Encrypted Connections to the Database Engine

Name: Linked Servers Should Use Encrypted Connections
Description: Checks whether any linked server is configured with connection security settings that could allow unencrypted data transfer. Linked-server connections between SQL instances typically carry result sets and write operations in plaintext unless the provider and connection string explicitly request encryption. This check flags linked servers that use OLEDB or SQL Server provider without a confirmed encrypted connection string.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.servers WHERE is_linked = 1 AND is_remote_login_enabled = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: For each flagged linked server, drop and recreate it using sp_addlinkedserver with the MSOLEDBSQL provider and set provider_string to include `Encrypt=yes;TrustServerCertificate=no`. Alternatively, add the Encrypt property via sp_addlinkedsrvlogin or SSMS linked-server properties dialog. Verify with `SELECT name, provider, provider_string FROM sys.servers WHERE is_linked = 1`.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no linked servers are defined or all SQL-provider linked servers have Encrypt=True in provider_string. Returns FAIL when one or more SQL-provider linked servers lack explicit encryption in provider_string.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Strong cryptography used to safeguard PAN during transmission

ValidationSources.source: Brent Ozar - Linked Servers Are Evil

Name: Sysadmin Sessions Should Not Connect From External IP Addresses
Description: Checks whether any currently active session with sysadmin privileges is connecting from an IP address that is not part of the internal corporate subnet. Remote sysadmin sessions from unknown networks are a strong indicator of compromised credentials.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_connections c
  INNER JOIN sys.dm_exec_sessions s ON c.session_id = s.session_id
  WHERE s.is_user_process = 1
    AND s.original_login_name NOT LIKE 'NT %'
    AND s.original_login_name NOT LIKE 'NT SERVICE\%'
    AND IS_SRVROLEMEMBER('sysadmin', s.original_login_name) = 1
    AND c.client_net_address NOT LIKE '192.168.%'
    AND c.client_net_address NOT LIKE '10.%'
    AND c.client_net_address NOT LIKE '172.16.%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Review sysadmin sessions from external networks. Disable remote sysadmin access or require VPN for privileged connections.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no sysadmin sessions connect from external IP addresses

Name: SA Login Should Not Be Used for Remote Connections
Description: Checks whether the built-in sa login has any active sessions connecting from remote hosts. The sa account should be disabled entirely; if enabled, its use from any non-local connection is a critical security finding.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_connections c
  INNER JOIN sys.dm_exec_sessions s ON c.session_id = s.session_id
  WHERE s.login_name = 'sa'
    AND c.client_net_address NOT IN ('127.0.0.1', '::1')
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Disable the sa account with ALTER LOGIN sa DISABLE. Create named sysadmin accounts for administrative tasks.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no active sa sessions connect from remote hosts

Name: Linked Servers Should Not Target Public IP Addresses
Description: Checks whether any linked server has a data_source that resolves to a non-RFC1918 (public) IP address or an obvious public hostname. Linked servers that target public IPs could represent data flowing across untrusted networks or to systems outside the organisation's controlled environment, which is a concern for information flow enforcement (NIST AC-4) and PCI-DSS outbound CDE traffic controls.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.servers WHERE is_linked = 1 AND data_source LIKE '%[0-9]%.[0-9]%.[0-9]%.[0-9]%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run `SELECT name, provider, data_source, product FROM sys.servers WHERE is_linked = 1` and review each entry against the organisation's authorised data-flow register. For any unauthorised or unexpected public endpoint, drop the linked server with `EXEC sp_dropserver @server = 'LinkedServerName', @droplogins = 'droplogins'`. For authorised cloud endpoints, confirm encryption is enforced and document the business justification.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all linked servers target private or local data sources. Returns FAIL when one or more linked servers target apparent public IPs or known cloud database endpoints.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Outbound traffic from the CDE is restricted

ValidationSources.source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 AC-4 Information Flow Enforcement

Name: Service Broker Cross-Database Queues Should Be Reviewed for Information Flow
Description: Checks for Service Broker queues that are active across multiple databases on the same instance, which could represent unauthorised information flow between databases of different sensitivity tiers. Service Broker enables asynchronous message passing and is sometimes used to bridge data between databases without the visibility of a linked server, making it a less-obvious information-flow channel that NIST AC-4 controls should cover.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_broker_enabled = 1 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: For each database with Service Broker enabled, review its services and routes: `USE [DatabaseName]; SELECT name, activation_procedure, is_activation_enabled FROM sys.service_queues WHERE is_ms_shipped = 0;` and `SELECT name, broker_instance, address FROM sys.routes`. Disable Service Broker on any database that does not use it: `ALTER DATABASE [DatabaseName] SET DISABLE_BROKER`. Document authorised cross-database routes with business justification.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when zero or one user database has Service Broker enabled. Returns FAIL when two or more user databases have Service Broker enabled, flagging potential cross-database information flow for review.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Outbound traffic from the CDE is restricted

ValidationSources.source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 AC-4 Information Flow Enforcement

Name: PCI CDE Instances Should Not Use Default SQL Server Port 1433
Description: Checks whether the SQL Server instance is listening on the default port 1433. PCI-DSS environments should use non-standard ports to reduce the attack surface visible to port scanners and automated exploitation tools.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_connections
  WHERE local_net_address IS NOT NULL
    AND local_tcp_port = 1433
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Change SQL Server TCP port via SQL Server Configuration Manager. Update connection strings and firewall rules for the new port.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when SQL Server is not listening on port 1433

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Inbound traffic to the CDE is restricted
Ensure SQL Server uses non-default ports

Name: Database Mail SMTP Profile Should Not Send to External Internet Destinations
Description: Checks whether Database Mail is configured with an SMTP server that appears to be an external internet relay. On PCI-DSS CDE instances, outbound traffic should be restricted; Database Mail routing through a public SMTP relay could exfiltrate data embedded in alert emails or job notifications outside the controlled environment. This check flags mail profiles where the mailserver_name looks like a public mail provider or contains a non-RFC1918 IP.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysmail_account WHERE email_address NOT LIKE '%@yourdomain%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: In SSMS > Management > Database Mail > Database Mail Configuration Wizard, edit each flagged account to use an internal SMTP relay server that is within the organisation's controlled network. Alternatively, disable Database Mail entirely on CDE instances where operational alerts can be routed through a monitoring agent instead. Run `EXEC msdb.dbo.sysmail_stop_sp` to stop the mail queue immediately if an external relay is in use on a regulated instance.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no Database Mail accounts are configured or all configured accounts use internal SMTP servers. Returns FAIL when one or more accounts point to apparent public internet SMTP relays.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Outbound traffic from the CDE is restricted

ValidationSources.source: PCI Security Standards Council - PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 1.3.2

Name: SA Account is_disabled Flag Must Be Set (Mixed-Mode Instances)
Description: Checks the is_disabled flag on the sa login specifically. Distinct from NEW_043 (which tests disabled-or-renamed) and NEW_056 (which tracks sa by SID regardless of rename). This check targets mixed-mode instances where sa exists with its original name and tests only whether the is_disabled bit is set to 1. The sa login is a fixed, well-known account name that is targeted by every SQL Server brute-force tool. When mixed-mode authentication is enabled, an enabled sa account with a weak or default password represents the most common initial-access vector for SQL Server compromise. SOC 2 CC6.1 and CIS Benchmark both require sa to be disabled and access restricted to named accounts.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE name = 'sa' AND is_disabled = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run `ALTER LOGIN sa DISABLE;` on any instance where sa is not required. If a legacy application requires the sa account, rotate the password to a minimum 30-character random value, store it in a privileged access vault, and rename the account: `ALTER LOGIN sa WITH NAME = [sa_legacy_app];`. Remove the sa account (or renamed equivalent) from sysadmin role if possible, and re-grant only the specific permissions the application needs.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when server is Windows-only auth or sa is disabled. Returns FAIL when mixed-mode authentication is active and sa is enabled.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure the sa Account is Disabled
All user IDs are unique and shared accounts are not used

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Disable SA Account

Name: BUILTIN\Administrators Should Not Be a Member of the Sysadmin Role
Description: Checks whether BUILTIN\Administrators or the local Administrators Windows group is a member of the SQL Server sysadmin role. When this group membership exists, any local Windows administrator can connect to SQL Server with full sysadmin privileges without any SQL-level access control, bypassing all database-level security and audit controls. SOC 2 CC6.1 requires that logical access is restricted to authorised principals with documented justification.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals p INNER JOIN sys.server_role_members rm ON p.principal_id = rm.member_principal_id INNER JOIN sys.server_principals r ON rm.role_principal_id = r.principal_id WHERE p.name = 'BUILTIN\Administrators' AND r.name = 'sysadmin') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run `EXEC sp_dropsrvrolemember 'BUILTIN\Administrators', 'sysadmin';` (or the equivalent for any other Administrators group found). Before removal, confirm that at least one other named Windows account or AD group remains in sysadmin to avoid a lockout. Create a dedicated AD security group (e.g., SQL_DBA_Admins) and add it with `EXEC sp_addsrvrolemember 'DOMAIN\SQL_DBA_Admins', 'sysadmin';`.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when BUILTIN\Administrators and NT AUTHORITY groups are absent from the sysadmin role. Returns FAIL when any Windows Administrators group is a member of sysadmin.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure BUILTIN\Administrators is not in sysadmin role
Access to system components is restricted to only those with a legitimate business need

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Remove BUILTIN\Administrators from Sysadmin

Name: SQL Login Passwords Older Than 90 Days OR Expiration Checking Disabled
Description: FAILs when any enabled SQL login has PasswordLastSetTime >90 days OR has is_expiration_checked=0. This is a compound check — it flags both stale passwords AND logins that bypass the expiry policy entirely. Distinct from NEW_041 (dormancy, skips logins without activity) and NEW_054 (365-day threshold only, no expiry-flag test). Stale passwords on SQL logins are a HIPAA and SOC 2 risk indicator; credentials that have not rotated in 90+ days could belong to dormant service accounts or former employees whose access was not fully revoked. This check also flags logins with password expiration checking disabled, which bypasses the OS password policy.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE DATEDIFF(dd, createdate, GETDATE()) > 90 AND is_policy_checked = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run `SELECT name, is_expiration_checked, LOGINPROPERTY(name, 'PasswordLastSetTime') AS pwd_set, LOGINPROPERTY(name, 'IsExpired') AS is_expired FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE is_disabled = 0` to identify flagged logins. For each: confirm the login is still needed; rotate the password; enable expiration with `ALTER LOGIN [name] WITH PASSWORD = '...' MUST_CHANGE, CHECK_EXPIRATION = ON, CHECK_POLICY = ON`. Disable logins no longer required.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all enabled SQL logins have passwords set within 90 days and expiration checking enabled. Returns FAIL when one or more logins have stale passwords or expiration checking disabled.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure SQL Server Login Password Expiration is Enabled
Passwords are changed at least once every 90 days

Name: Orphaned Database Users Should Be Removed
Description: Checks for database users whose SID does not match any server-level principal. Orphaned users cannot authenticate and represent stale security principals that should be cleaned up or re-mapped to valid logins.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT dp.name, dp.type_desc FROM sys.database_principals dp
  WHERE dp.type IN ('S','U')
    AND dp.sid IS NOT NULL
    AND dp.sid <> 0x0
    AND LEN(dp.sid) > 16
    AND dp.name NOT IN ('dbo','guest','INFORMATION_SCHEMA','sys')
    AND dp.sid NOT IN (SELECT sid FROM sys.server_principals WHERE type IN ('S','U'))
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Drop orphaned users with DROP USER. For users that need to remain, map them to a valid login with ALTER USER [name] WITH LOGIN = [login].
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no orphaned database users are found

Name: Sysadmin Role Membership Count Exceeds 5 (SOC 2 Liberal Threshold)
Description: FAILs when sysadmin has more than 5 non-system members. Uses the liberal (SOC 2) threshold of 5. Distinct from NEW_061, which uses the stricter threshold of 3 and targets ISO/NIST environments. Use this check where mixed-size teams make 3 unrealistic but 5 is still a meaningful constraint. A sysadmin role with many members looks like access was granted liberally without least-privilege review. SOC 2 CC6.1 requires that logical access be restricted to the minimum necessary; more than 5 sysadmin members on a production instance is a common indicator that the principle of least privilege has not been applied. This check uses a threshold of 5 members as a conservative flag point.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sys.server_principals p INNER JOIN sys.server_role_members rm ON p.principal_id = rm.member_principal_id WHERE rm.role_principal_id = SUSER_ID('sysadmin') AND p.type IN ('S','U')) > 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run `SELECT member_sp.name, member_sp.type_desc FROM sys.server_role_members srm JOIN sys.server_principals member_sp ON srm.member_principal_id = member_sp.principal_id JOIN sys.server_principals role_sp ON srm.role_principal_id = role_sp.principal_id WHERE role_sp.name = 'sysadmin'` to list all members. For each member beyond the documented approved set, run `EXEC sp_dropsrvrolemember 'account_name', 'sysadmin';` and grant only the specific server roles or permissions actually required (e.g., db_owner on the relevant databases, or serveradmin for restart-only scenarios).
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when sysadmin role has 5 or fewer non-system members. Returns FAIL when the count exceeds 5.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure Sysadmin Role Membership is Minimal
Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts
Access to system components is restricted to the minimum necessary

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Sysadmin Membership Review

Name: AG Replica Endpoints Should Not Use Public DNS Names
Description: Checks whether any Availability Group replica endpoint URL uses a public DNS name instead of a private/internal hostname. Using public DNS for AG endpoints exposes internal replication traffic to DNS poisoning and interception risks.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.availability_replicas ar
  INNER JOIN sys.availability_groups ag ON ar.group_id = ag.group_id
  WHERE ar.endpoint_url NOT LIKE '%.local%'
    AND ar.endpoint_url NOT LIKE '%.internal%'
    AND ar.endpoint_url NOT LIKE '%.corp%'
    AND ar.endpoint_url NOT LIKE '%.lan%'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Reconfigure AG replicas with private DNS names or IP addresses. Update endpoint URLs via ALTER AVAILABILITY GROUP.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all AG endpoints use private/internal hostnames

Name: SQL Logins Should Have Password Policy and Expiration Checks Enabled
Description: Checks whether any enabled SQL login has is_policy_checked or is_expiration_checked set to false. When these flags are off, SQL Server does not enforce the Windows password complexity and expiration policy for that login, allowing weak or perpetual passwords regardless of the domain policy. HIPAA and PCI-DSS both require that password controls be applied consistently to all accounts with access to regulated data.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE is_policy_checked = 0 OR is_expiration_checked = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: For each flagged login, run: `ALTER LOGIN [login_name] WITH CHECK_POLICY = ON, CHECK_EXPIRATION = ON;`. Note that enabling CHECK_EXPIRATION when CHECK_POLICY was previously off may immediately mark the login as expired if the password does not meet policy; have a replacement password ready. Verify with `SELECT name, is_policy_checked, is_expiration_checked FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE is_disabled = 0`.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all enabled non-system SQL logins have both is_policy_checked and is_expiration_checked set to true. Returns FAIL when any enabled login has either flag disabled.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Passwords meet minimum length and complexity requirements
Ensure Password Policy Enforcement is Enabled
All passwords/passphrases for user accounts meet minimum length and complexity requirements

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Password Policy Enforcement

Name: Log Shipping Should Not Send Data to Instances With Lower Security Tier
Description: Checks whether log shipping is configured on this instance and whether secondary destination paths suggest data is flowing to a different (potentially less-secured) server. Log shipping copies full transaction logs to secondary servers; if a regulated or CDE primary sends logs to a server outside the controlled environment, it creates an uncontrolled copy of all data. This check surfaces log shipping configurations that may represent an AC-4 information-flow control gap.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.log_shipping_primary_databases WHERE backup_threshold > 60) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run `SELECT lsp.primary_database, lpss.secondary_server, lpss.secondary_database FROM msdb.dbo.log_shipping_primary_databases lsp JOIN msdb.dbo.log_shipping_primary_secondaries lpss ON lsp.primary_id = lpss.primary_id` to list all log shipping targets. For each, confirm the secondary server is within the same security tier, log file share permissions are restricted to the SQL Server service account only, and the data flow is documented in the organisation's information-flow map. Disable any log shipping job that sends data to an unauthorised destination.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no log shipping primary configurations exist. Returns WARN when log shipping is configured but no cross-server secondaries are detected. Returns FAIL when log shipping targets are on different servers, requiring manual tier verification.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Outbound traffic from the CDE is restricted

ValidationSources.source: Microsoft Learn - Log Shipping Tables and Stored Procedures
NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 AC-4 Information Flow Enforcement

Name: Dedicated Admin Connection Should Not Be Accessible From Remote Hosts
Description: Checks whether the Dedicated Admin Connection (DAC) is configured to accept remote connections. The DAC is a reserved single-connection channel for emergency administrative access that bypasses normal connection limits. Allowing remote DAC connections means any sysadmin can connect directly from a remote host during an emergency, bypassing the jump-server pattern required by NIST AC-17. The remote admin connections option should be 0 (disabled) on all instances where remote DAC is not operationally required.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'remote admin connections' AND value_in_use = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Disable remote admin connections: `EXEC sp_configure 'remote admin connections', 0; RECONFIGURE;`. Verify with `SELECT name, value_in_use FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'remote admin connections'`. For clustered instances where local console is unavailable, this setting may need to remain enabled but must be documented and its network access restricted by firewall rules to the jump server subnet.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when remote admin connections is set to 0 (disabled). Returns FAIL when remote admin connections is set to 1 (enabled).

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure Remote Admin Connections is Disabled
Inbound traffic to the CDE is restricted

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Disable Remote Admin Connections
Microsoft Learn - Diagnostic Connection for Database Administrators

Name: Server Role Nesting Should Not Obscure Sysadmin Membership
Description: Checks whether any user-defined server role is itself a member of the sysadmin role, which would grant all members of that user role sysadmin privileges through role nesting. Deeply nested role membership makes access reviews harder and can result in principals having sysadmin access without it being visible in a simple membership check. HIPAA access establishment controls and SOC 2 CC6.1 require that access paths be transparent and auditable.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_role_members rm1 INNER JOIN sys.server_role_members rm2 ON rm1.member_principal_id = rm2.role_principal_id) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run `SELECT member_sp.name AS nested_role FROM sys.server_role_members srm JOIN sys.server_principals member_sp ON srm.member_principal_id = member_sp.principal_id JOIN sys.server_principals role_sp ON srm.role_principal_id = role_sp.principal_id WHERE role_sp.name = 'sysadmin' AND member_sp.type = 'R' AND member_sp.is_fixed_role = 0` to list nested roles. Remove each with `EXEC sp_dropsrvrolemember 'nested_role_name', 'sysadmin';`. Grant only the specific server-level permissions the role actually needs.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no user-defined server roles are nested within sysadmin. Returns FAIL when one or more user-defined roles are members of sysadmin, creating an indirect privilege escalation path.

Name: PCI CDE Connections Should Originate From Expected Private Subnets
Description: Checks whether active SQL Server connections originate from the expected private subnet ranges defined in PCI-DSS scoping documentation. Connections from unexpected subnets may indicate unauthorized access or misconfigured network segmentation.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_connections
  WHERE client_net_address NOT LIKE '192.168.%'
    AND client_net_address NOT LIKE '10.%'
    AND client_net_address NOT LIKE '172.16.%'
    AND client_net_address NOT IN ('127.0.0.1','::1')
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Review non-private subnet connections. Configure firewall rules to restrict SQL Server access to authorized subnets only.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all active connections originate from private subnet ranges

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Inbound traffic to the CDE is restricted

Name: Linked Servers Should Be Minimal and Reviewed
Description: Checks whether the number of defined linked servers exceeds a conservative threshold. A high linked-server count often indicates that data connections were added opportunistically over time without periodic review. Each linked server represents a potential information-flow path and attack-surface expansion; NIST AC-4 requires that information flows between systems are controlled and authorised. This check uses a threshold of 5 as a flag point for review.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sys.servers WHERE is_linked = 1) > 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run `SELECT name, product, provider, data_source, modify_date FROM sys.servers WHERE is_linked = 1 ORDER BY modify_date DESC` to review all linked servers sorted by last modification. For each server that is unused, undocumented, or no longer required, drop it with `EXEC sp_dropserver @server = 'LinkedServerName', @droplogins = 'droplogins';`. Maintain a register of approved linked servers with business justification and review quarterly.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when 5 or fewer linked servers are defined. Returns FAIL when more than 5 linked servers are defined, triggering a review.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Outbound traffic from the CDE is restricted

ValidationSources.source: Brent Ozar - Linked Servers Are Evil

Name: Direct Table or View Grants to Individual Users Should Be Avoided
Description: Checks whether any individual database user (not a role) has been granted explicit SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or EXECUTE permissions directly on tables or views. Direct object grants to individual users bypass role-based access control, make access reviews harder, and are a SOC 2 CC6.1 red flag. The correct pattern is to grant permissions to roles and assign users to roles, so that access is managed through a documented role hierarchy.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_permissions WHERE class = 1 AND state_desc = 'GRANT' AND grantee_principal_id > 4 AND grantee_principal_id NOT IN (SELECT principal_id FROM sys.database_principals WHERE type = 'R')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run `SELECT grantee.name, obj.name AS object_name, dp.permission_name FROM sys.database_permissions dp JOIN sys.database_principals grantee ON dp.grantee_principal_id = grantee.principal_id JOIN sys.objects obj ON dp.major_id = obj.object_id WHERE dp.class = 1 AND grantee.type IN ('S','U') AND dp.state_desc = 'GRANT'` to list all direct grants. For each: create or identify the appropriate role, grant the permission to the role, add the user to the role, then revoke the direct grant: `REVOKE [permission] ON [object] FROM [user]; EXEC sp_addrolemember 'role_name', 'user_name';`
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no direct table or view permissions are granted to individual users in the current database. Returns FAIL when one or more direct object grants to individual users are found.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Access to system components is restricted to the minimum necessary
Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts

Name: Mixed-Mode Authentication Is Active - SQL Login Attack Surface Warning
Description: FAILs when mixed-mode authentication is active (IsIntegratedSecurityOnly=0), regardless of whether SQL logins are present. Distinct from NEW_038, which PASSes when Windows-only mode is confirmed. This check counts active SQL logins as context but FAILs on mixed-mode presence alone — it is a posture check, not an MFA check. Mixed-mode authentication enables SQL logins which use password-based authentication and are susceptible to brute-force attacks, credential theft, and password spray. Windows Authentication-only mode is the preferred configuration for regulated environments because it delegates authentication to Active Directory, which supports MFA, account lockout, and centralised credential management.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN SERVERPROPERTY('IsIntegratedSecurityOnly') = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Evaluate whether all active SQL logins could be replaced with Windows accounts or AD service accounts. For application SQL logins that cannot be replaced, document the business justification and ensure CHECK_POLICY = ON, CHECK_EXPIRATION = ON on each. To switch to Windows Authentication-only mode once SQL logins are removed: In SSMS, right-click the server > Properties > Security > Server authentication = Windows Authentication mode, then restart the SQL Server service.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when server is in Windows Authentication-only mode. Returns FAIL when server is in mixed-mode authentication, including a count of active SQL logins.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure Windows Authentication Mode is Used
All user IDs are unique and properly managed

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Windows Authentication Mode
Microsoft Learn - Choose an Authentication Mode

Name: Audit Specifications Cover Login, Schema, and Principal Change Events (AU-3)
Description: Checks whether at least one enabled SQL Server Audit specification captures LOGIN_CHANGE_PASSWORD_GROUP, SCHEMA_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP, and SERVER_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP. Without these three action groups, critical identity and schema changes leave no audit trail -- a gap that looks like a serious control failure during compliance reviews. This check looks at sys.server_audit_specification_details joined to enabled audits only.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_audit_specifications WHERE is_state_enabled = 1 AND audit_action_id IN ('LGIF','LGO','LGIS','LGSD','LGIS')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more of the three required AU-3 action groups is absent from all enabled audit specifications. Add LOGIN_CHANGE_PASSWORD_GROUP, SCHEMA_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP, and SERVER_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP to an enabled server audit specification. Verify with a SELECT against sys.server_audit_specification_details filtered by those action names joined to enabled audits.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all three action groups appear in at least one enabled audit specification. Returns FAIL when any are missing.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Audit logs capture all individual user access to cardholder data

ValidationSources.source: Microsoft Learn - SQL Server Audit Action Groups

Name: Audit File Destination Is Configured and Not Writing to System Drive (AU-3)
Description: Checks that all enabled SQL Server Audits writing to a FILE destination have a non-empty file_path and that none of them point to the system drive root (C:\). Audit files stored on the OS partition could be overwritten or crowded out during Windows updates, which looks like an AU-3 integrity gap to auditors. This check queries sys.server_file_audits joined to sys.server_audits.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_file_audits WHERE log_file_path LIKE '%C:%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more enabled FILE audits have a missing or system-drive-root path. Alter each affected audit to write to a dedicated volume. Confirm max_size and max_rollover_files are set deliberately so logs are retained for the required period without crowding the OS drive.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all enabled FILE audits have a non-empty, non-system-root path. Returns FAIL when any have a blank or OS-root path.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure SQL Server Audit is set to capture both failed and successful logins
Retain audit log history for at least 12 months

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Audit File Configuration

Name: At Least One Server Audit Specification Is Enabled and Its Parent Audit Is Active (AU-3)
Description: Checks that at least one server audit specification exists in an enabled state AND that its parent server audit is also enabled. A specification that is defined but disabled -- or whose parent audit is stopped -- silently drops all audit events. This looks like a fully configured AU-3 posture from the outside while producing no records at all.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_audit_specifications WHERE is_state_enabled = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: No enabled audit specification with an active parent audit was found. Enable the parent server audit first (ALTER SERVER AUDIT ... WITH (STATE = ON)), then enable the specification. Verify both is_state_enabled columns are 1 in the respective catalog views.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one specification is enabled and its parent audit is active. Returns FAIL when no such combination exists.

Name: Extended Events Session Exists for Long-Running or Session Tracking (AU-14)
Description: Checks whether at least one non-system Extended Events session is running that could serve session-tracking or long-running query capture purposes. NIST AU-14 (Session Audit) calls for the ability to audit individual user sessions. The default SQL Server trace and XE system sessions alone do not satisfy this -- a deliberate session-tracking XE session should be present and running. This check queries sys.dm_xe_sessions for non-system, non-health sessions.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_xe_sessions WHERE create_time < DATEADD(hh,-1,GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: No non-system Extended Events session is running. Create a session-tracking XE session covering at minimum login, logout, and sql_statement_completed events, configure STARTUP_STATE=ON, and start it. Verify it appears in sys.dm_xe_sessions after the next SQL Server restart.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one non-system XE session is running. Returns FAIL when only system-managed sessions exist.

Name: Query Store Retention Period Is Set to at Least 30 Days on User Databases (AU-14)
Description: Checks that Query Store is enabled on user databases and that the retention period (query_capture_mode not OFF, cleanup_policy_stale_query_threshold_days >= 30) is adequate for session-level forensic reconstruction. A very short Query Store window means historical query patterns and session activity cannot be reviewed more than a few days back, undermining AU-14 session audit capability.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_query_store_options WHERE stale_query_threshold_days < 30 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more user databases have Query Store disabled or configured with retention below 30 days. Enable Query Store and set CLEANUP_POLICY to STALE_QUERY_THRESHOLD_DAYS = 30 (or higher) for all production databases to support AU-14 session-level lookback.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all online user databases have Query Store enabled with >= 30-day retention. Returns FAIL when any are disabled or below threshold.

Name: DBCC CHECKDB Has Run on All User Databases Within the Last 7 Days (PM-14)
Description: Checks the msdb backup history for evidence that DBCC CHECKDB completed successfully on each online user database within the last 7 days. PM-14 (Testing, Training, and Monitoring) calls for scheduled integrity testing. SQL Server stores the last known good CHECKDB completion time in the boot page, but reading it requires DBCC DBINFO which can vary; this check uses the msdb.dbo.suspect_pages table as a proxy -- zero suspect pages is consistent with passing integrity checks. Databases with no entry in suspect_pages AND no page corruption in backupset are treated as PASS when the check cannot directly read the boot-page date.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 AND d.name NOT IN ('tempdb') AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages sp WHERE sp.database_id = d.database_id AND sp.last_update_date > DATEADD(dd, -7, GETDATE()))) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more user databases show suspect pages or have no recent backup, suggesting CHECKDB or integrity monitoring is not running on schedule. Schedule DBCC CHECKDB weekly for all user databases, review and clear suspect_pages entries, and confirm SQL Agent jobs are completing. Consult msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory for job failure details.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no suspect pages exist in the last 30 days and all user databases have a full backup within 7 days. Returns FAIL otherwise.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Environmental protections, software, data back-up processes

Name: All Enabled SQL Agent Jobs Must Have Failure Notification - Zero-Tolerance Check (PM-14)
Description: FAILs if any single enabled job has no failure notification (zero-tolerance). Distinct from NEW_160, which FAILs only when fewer than 50% of jobs have notifications (coverage-rate threshold). Use this check for environments where every job must have a notification wired, regardless of job type. (notify_level_eventlog, notify_level_email, notify_level_netsend, or notify_level_page). Jobs that fail silently -- especially maintenance and backup jobs -- leave no observable signal, which looks like an absence of PM-14 monitoring. This check queries msdb.dbo.sysjobs for jobs where all notify_level_* columns are 0 (no notification on failure).
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs WHERE enabled = 1 AND notify_level_eventlog = 0 AND notify_level_email = 0 AND notify_level_page = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more enabled SQL Agent jobs have no failure notification configured. Set notify_level_eventlog = 2 at minimum for all enabled jobs, and configure operator email notifications where mail is available. Use sp_update_job or SSMS Job Properties to apply changes.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all enabled jobs have at least one failure notification method set. Returns FAIL when any enabled job has all notify_level_* = 0.

Name: Backup Verification (RESTORE VERIFYONLY) Recorded in Restore History
Description: Checks whether RESTORE VERIFYONLY has been executed against recent backups and recorded in msdb restore history. Unverified backups are a critical reliability gap that leaves recovery integrity uncertain.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d
  WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 AND d.name <> 'tempdb'
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.restorehistory rh
      WHERE rh.destination_database_name = d.name
        AND rh.restore_type = 'V'
        AND rh.restore_date > DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())
    )
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Add RESTORE VERIFYONLY to backup maintenance plans. Run weekly for full backups, daily for critical databases.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all user databases have verified backups within 30 days

Name: Server System Time Is Within 5 Minutes of UTC Reference (PCI 10.6.3)
Description: Checks whether the SQL Server system time offset is within a reasonable band of UTC. PCI-DSS 10.6.3 requires time-synchronization controls so that audit log timestamps are accurate and event correlation across systems is possible. This check compares SYSDATETIMEOFFSET() against GETUTCDATE() and flags large discrepancies that could indicate a drifted or misconfigured system clock.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_sys_info WHERE ABS(DATEDIFF(mi, GETUTCDATE(), GETDATE())) > 5) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: The SQL Server clock shows drift beyond the 5-minute tolerance versus UTC. Investigate W32tm or NTP configuration on the host. For VMs, also check hypervisor time synchronization settings. Rerun this check after correcting time sync to confirm the drift is resolved.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when clock drift is within 300 seconds. Returns FAIL when drift exceeds 300 seconds.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Time-synchronization technology is configured and kept current

ValidationSources.source: PCI Security Standards Council - PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.6

Name: AG Replica Last Received Time Shows No Clock Divergence Across Replicas (PCI 10.6.3)
Description: Checks availability group replica last_received_time values from sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states to detect large clock divergence between the primary and secondary replicas. PCI-DSS 10.6.3 requires consistent time across all CDE systems. A large spread in last_received_time across replicas could signal that NTP is not uniformly configured on AG nodes. This check only runs when AG replicas are visible from the current instance.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states WHERE last_received_time IS NOT NULL AND DATEDIFF(mi, last_received_time, GETDATE()) > 5) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: AG replica last_received_time values show a spread exceeding 5 minutes. Investigate NTP on each node and check AG health via sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states. Confirm all nodes sync to the same NTP source and that no replica is in a lagging or disconnected state.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when last_received_time spread across replicas is within 300 seconds. Returns SKIP when no AGs are configured. Returns FAIL when spread exceeds 300 seconds.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Time-synchronization technology is configured and kept current

Name: SERVER_ROLE_MEMBER_CHANGE_GROUP Is Captured by an Enabled Audit Spec (PCI 10.2.1.1)
Description: Checks whether at least one enabled server audit specification captures SERVER_ROLE_MEMBER_CHANGE_GROUP. PCI-DSS 10.2.1.1 requires audit logging of all administrator actions, including changes to privileged role membership such as sysadmin, securityadmin, and serveradmin. Without this event group, adding a login to a privileged role produces no audit trail.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_audit_specifications WHERE audit_action_id = 'MDSL' AND is_state_enabled = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: SERVER_ROLE_MEMBER_CHANGE_GROUP is not in any enabled audit specification. Add it to an existing enabled specification via ALTER SERVER AUDIT SPECIFICATION. Verify the parent audit is also enabled (is_state_enabled = 1 in sys.server_audits).
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when SERVER_ROLE_MEMBER_CHANGE_GROUP appears in at least one enabled audit specification with an active parent audit. Returns FAIL when absent.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Audit logs of all individual user access to cardholder data and all actions by root or admin
Ensure the SQL Server Audit is configured to capture server role changes

ValidationSources.source: PCI Security Standards Council - PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.2.1.1

Name: Login Property Changes Are Captured by an Enabled Audit Spec (PCI 10.2.1.1)
Description: Checks whether at least one enabled server audit specification captures both SUCCESSFUL_LOGIN_GROUP and SERVER_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP -- the two events that together cover login creation, property changes, and successful authentication. PCI-DSS 10.2.1.1 requires administrator action auditing; login property changes (password reset, disable/enable, default database) are administrator actions that must appear in the audit log.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_audit_specifications WHERE audit_action_id = 'LGPM' AND is_state_enabled = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or both of SUCCESSFUL_LOGIN_GROUP and SERVER_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP are missing from enabled audit specifications. Add the missing groups to an existing enabled specification and confirm both appear in sys.server_audit_specification_details joined to active audits.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when both groups are covered. Returns FAIL when one or both are missing from all enabled specifications.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Audit logs of all actions by individuals with root or administrative privileges
Ensure SQL Server Audit is set to capture login events

Name: SQL Server Is Configured for Windows Authentication Only (PCI 8.3.2 / MFA)
Description: PASSes only when IsIntegratedSecurityOnly=1 (Windows-only auth mode). Distinct from NEW_025, which FAILs on mixed-mode presence and counts SQL logins. This check is the positive-confirmation form: it is used for PCI/MFA compliance evidence where the auditor needs a PASS result confirming Windows-only mode, not merely the absence of mixed-mode risk.; Windows Authentication delegates credential management and MFA enforcement" to Active Directory, making it the only supported path. SQL Authentication with static passwords cannot satisfy PCI 8.3.2 on its own. This check reads SERVERPROPERTY('IsIntegratedSecurityOnly').
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE name = 'sa') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: SQL Server is in Mixed Mode. Evaluate whether any SQL Authentication logins are truly required. Where possible, migrate to Windows Authentication and switch the instance to Windows Authentication only mode via SQL Server Configuration Manager (requires service restart). Document any SQL logins retained as compensating controls.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when IsIntegratedSecurityOnly = 1. Returns FAIL when Mixed Mode is enabled.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: MFA is implemented for all access into the CDE
Ensure Authentication Mode Is Set to Windows Authentication Only

ValidationSources.source: CIS Microsoft SQL Server Benchmark - Authentication Mode

Name: DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP Is Captured for Provisioning Audit Trail (SOC 2 CC6.2)
Description: Checks whether at least one enabled server or database audit specification captures DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP. SOC 2 CC6.2 requires that user provisioning and deprovisioning events are logged and reviewable. Without this event group, database user creation, alteration, and deletion produce no audit record -- leaving access provisioning unauditable.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_audit_specifications WHERE audit_action_id IN ('DATG','DLG','DUG','DCHG','SCHEMA_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP') AND is_state_enabled = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP is absent from all enabled audit specifications. Add it to an existing server-level specification. This single addition covers provisioning events for all databases without needing per-database configurations.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP is in at least one enabled server audit specification with an active parent audit. Returns FAIL when absent.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Prior to issuing system credentials, the entity authorizes users
All users are assigned a unique ID before access is granted

ValidationSources.source: Microsoft Learn - SQL Server Audit Action Groups

Name: Enabled SQL Logins Dormant Over 90 Days - Deprovision Candidates (SOC 2 CC6.2)
Description: FAILs when enabled SQL logins have PasswordLastSetTime older than 90 days (dormancy proxy). This is the access-lifecycle / deprovisioning check. Distinct from NEW_014 (which also tests the is_expiration_checked flag) and NEW_054 (365-day threshold). This check frames dormancy as an authorisation-hygiene issue per SOC 2 CC6.2 — these logins are deprovision candidates, not just password-rotation candidates. Dormant but enabled logins represent stale authorizations that should have been deprovisioned -- a SOC 2 CC6.2 finding. This check uses LOGINPROPERTY and sys.sql_logins; it skips Windows logins because their lifecycle is managed in AD.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals WHERE type IN ('S','U') AND is_disabled = 0 AND DATEDIFF(dd, modify_date, GETDATE()) > 90 AND name NOT LIKE '##%' AND name NOT LIKE 'NT %') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more enabled SQL logins have not had their password changed in 90+ days. Review each login with the application owner, disable those no longer needed, and rotate passwords on active ones. Enable CHECK_EXPIRATION to prevent recurrence.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no enabled SQL logins have PasswordLastSetTime older than 90 days. Returns FAIL when any do.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: If passwords are used as authentication factors, passwords for user accounts are changed at least once every 90 days

Name: Audit Log File Retention Period Supports 90-Day Online Retention
Description: Checks whether SQL Server audit file specifications retain logs for at least 90 days. HIPAA requires documented retention of audit records and short retention windows make incident investigation impossible.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_audits sa
  INNER JOIN sys.server_file_audits sfa ON sa.audit_guid = sfa.audit_guid
  WHERE sa.is_state_enabled = 1
    AND (sfa.max_rollover_files < 30 OR sfa.max_files < 30)
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Increase max_rollover_files and max_files in server audit file specifications to support 90-day retention.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when audit files have sufficient retention capacity

Name: SA Login Disabled OR Renamed - Either Control Satisfies Baseline
Description: Checks whether the sa login is either disabled or renamed. Either control satisfies baseline compliance requirements under PCI-DSS and NIST. An enabled default-named sa account is a guaranteed auditor finding.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals
  WHERE sid = 0x01
    AND (is_disabled = 0 OR name = 'sa')
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run ALTER LOGIN sa DISABLE. For mixed-mode instances, also rename sa with ALTER LOGIN sa WITH NAME='sql_sa_disabled'.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when sa is either disabled or renamed

Name: FAILED_LOGIN_GROUP Is Captured by an Enabled Audit Specification (PCI 10.2.1.1 / AU-14)
Description: Checks whether at least one enabled server audit specification captures FAILED_LOGIN_GROUP. Failed login events are a primary indicator of credential attacks and are explicitly required by PCI-DSS 10.2.1.4 and NIST AU-14. Without this event group, brute-force and password-spray attacks against SQL Server leave no audit record.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_audit_specifications WHERE audit_action_id = 'LGIF' AND is_state_enabled = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: FAILED_LOGIN_GROUP is absent from all enabled audit specifications. Add it to an existing specification. After adding, test by attempting a deliberate failed login and confirming the event appears in the audit log.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when FAILED_LOGIN_GROUP is in at least one enabled audit specification with an active parent audit. Returns FAIL when absent.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Audit logs capture invalid logical access attempts
Ensure SQL Server Audit is configured to capture failed logins

ValidationSources.source: PCI Security Standards Council - PCI DSS v4.0 Requirement 10.2.1.4

Name: Maintenance Plan Jobs Have Run Successfully in the Last 7 Days (PM-14)
Description: Checks whether SQL Agent jobs associated with maintenance plans have recorded at least one successful execution in the last 7 days. PM-14 requires that monitoring and maintenance activities are performed and evidenced. Jobs that have not run recently -- or that have only failed executions -- indicate that the maintenance schedule has lapsed, which could leave integrity checks, index maintenance, and backup verification unperformed.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysmaintplan_log WHERE task_name = 'DBCC CHECKDB' AND succeeded = 0 AND start_time > DATEADD(dd, -7, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more maintenance plan jobs have not run successfully in the last 7 days. Check SQL Agent service health, confirm schedules are active, and review sysjobhistory for failure details. Resolve the underlying failure and confirm the next execution completes.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all enabled maintenance plan jobs have a successful execution in the last 7 days. Returns FAIL when any have not. Returns SKIP when no maintenance plan jobs are configured.

Name: sp_configure Non-Default Values Should Be Reviewed and Documented
Description: Detects sys.configurations entries where value_in_use differs from the SQL Server default, indicating a deliberate or accidental configuration change. Undocumented non-default settings could represent a security risk, performance time-bomb, or compliance gap. This check counts settings that deviate from documented defaults so a DBA can confirm each one is intentional.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE value_in_use <> value AND name NOT IN ('max degree of parallelism','cost threshold for parallelism','max server memory (MB)','min server memory (MB)','clr enabled','xp_cmdshell','Ole Automation Procedures','remote admin connections','optimize for ad hoc workloads')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run `SELECT name, value_in_use, default_value, description FROM sys.configurations WHERE CAST(value_in_use AS BIGINT) <> CAST(default_value AS BIGINT) ORDER BY name` to enumerate deviations. For each entry, confirm the change is documented in your change management system. Revert any setting with no business justification using `EXEC sp_configure N'<name>', <default_value>; RECONFIGURE;`.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no unexpected non-default sp_configure values are found. Returns FAIL when one or more settings deviate from defaults (memory and MAXDOP excluded as they are tuned by design).

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process
Ensure Latest SQL Server Updates Are Applied

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Configuration Best Practices

Name: MAXDOP and Cost Threshold for Parallelism Should Be Within Recommended Bounds
Description: Checks whether max degree of parallelism (MAXDOP) is set to 0 (unlimited) and whether cost threshold for parallelism is still at the factory default of 5. A MAXDOP of 0 with a cost threshold of 5 looks like a configuration that was never tuned, which could result in excessive parallelism overhead on OLTP workloads and is flagged by most baseline configuration audits.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'max degree of parallelism' AND value_in_use NOT BETWEEN 1 AND 8) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Set MAXDOP to a value appropriate for your NUMA node topology (SQL Server 2019+ provides automatic recommendations). Set cost threshold for parallelism to at least 25 for OLTP or 50 for mixed workloads. Example: `EXEC sp_configure N'max degree of parallelism', 8; EXEC sp_configure N'cost threshold for parallelism', 50; RECONFIGURE;`. Validate via `SELECT name, value_in_use FROM sys.configurations WHERE name IN (N'max degree of parallelism', N'cost threshold for parallelism')`.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when MAXDOP is not 0, or cost threshold for parallelism is not 5, indicating at least some parallelism tuning has occurred. Returns FAIL when both are at factory defaults simultaneously.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process

ValidationSources.source: Microsoft Learn - Server Configuration - max degree of parallelism

Name: Undocumented or High-Risk Global Trace Flags Should Not Be Running
Description: Detects globally enabled trace flags that are either undocumented, deprecated, or known to change security-relevant engine behaviour. Trace flags active at the global scope affect all connections and sessions; a flag like TF 3625 (masks error messages) or TF 8048/8015 (NUMA reassignment) could indicate a misconfiguration or deliberate obfuscation. This check surfaces all active global flags so DBAs can confirm each one is intentional.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_server_registry WHERE registry_key LIKE '%Parameters%' AND value_name LIKE 'TraceFlags%' AND value_data NOT IN ('','NULL')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run `DBCC TRACESTATUS(-1)` to list all globally active trace flags. For each flag not in your approved baseline, run `DBCC TRACEOFF(<flag>, -1)` to disable it globally. To prevent flags from re-enabling at startup, review the SQL Server service startup parameters in SQL Server Configuration Manager and remove any -T flags that are not in your approved list.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no global trace flags outside the approved list are active. Returns FAIL when one or more unapproved global trace flags are running.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Secure Configuration Process

Name: xp_cmdshell Should Be Disabled to Reduce OS-Level Attack Surface
Description: Checks whether xp_cmdshell is enabled on the instance. When active, xp_cmdshell allows T-SQL callers to execute arbitrary operating system commands in the context of the SQL Server service account. This looks like one of the highest-risk SQL Server features that a NIST CM-7 (least functionality) review would flag, because it creates a direct bridge from the database tier to the OS shell.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'xp_cmdshell' AND value_in_use = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Disable xp_cmdshell immediately: `EXEC sp_configure N'show advanced options', 1; RECONFIGURE; EXEC sp_configure N'xp_cmdshell', 0; RECONFIGURE;`. Then set show advanced options back to 0 if appropriate. Audit all SQL Agent job steps and application code that may have relied on xp_cmdshell and replace with SQLCLR or dedicated agent-based file operations.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when xp_cmdshell value_in_use is 0 (disabled). Returns FAIL when xp_cmdshell is enabled.

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Surface Area Reduction

Name: OLE Automation Procedures, CLR, and External Scripts Should Be Disabled
Description: Checks whether OLE Automation Procedures, CLR integration, and External Scripts (R/Python via Machine Learning Services) are enabled on the instance. Each of these features extends the SQL Server execution surface area beyond T-SQL into external runtimes. Enabling any of them without a documented business need looks like a violation of NIST CM-7 least-functionality principles.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name IN ('Ole Automation Procedures','clr enabled','external scripts enabled') AND value_in_use = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: For each enabled feature, disable via sp_configure: `EXEC sp_configure N'Ole Automation Procedures', 0; EXEC sp_configure N'clr enabled', 0; EXEC sp_configure N'external scripts enabled', 0; RECONFIGURE;`. If CLR is required, consider CLR strict security (SQL 2017+) as a middle-ground: `EXEC sp_configure N'clr strict security', 1; RECONFIGURE;`.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when OLE Automation Procedures, CLR, and External Scripts are all disabled. Returns FAIL when any of the three features is enabled, with the message identifying which.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure CLR Assembly Permission Set is SAFE

Name: Logins Active From More Than 2 Distinct Hosts - Low-Threshold Shared Account Heuristic
Description: FAILs when any login connects simultaneously from more than 2 distinct host_name values. Low-threshold (2) heuristic — generates more findings but catches smaller shared-account patterns. Distinct from NEW_090, which uses a threshold of 5 and is tuned for high-traffic environments where 2-3 hosts per login is expected from load-balanced apps. A single login appearing from many different hosts looks like a shared or generic account that multiple users or services connect with, which prevents individual accountability and is a finding under NIST IA-2 (identification and authentication). This check uses session data as a heuristic only.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions WHERE is_user_process = 1 AND login_name NOT LIKE '##%' AND login_name NOT LIKE 'NT %' GROUP BY login_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT host_name) > 2) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: For each flagged login, run `SELECT login_name, host_name, COUNT(*) AS session_count FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions WHERE login_name = N'<login>' AND is_user_process = 1 GROUP BY login_name, host_name` to understand the spread. If the login is genuinely shared across users, create individual logins and retire the shared one. If the login is a legitimate application service account used across multiple hosts, document and accept the finding.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no SQL login is connecting from more than 2 distinct hosts simultaneously. Returns FAIL when one or more logins appear to be shared across many hosts.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Accounts
All user IDs are uniquely identified

Name: SQL Server Service Accounts Should Not Have Interactive User Sessions
Description: Detects sessions where the login_name matches a pattern consistent with SQL Server service accounts (MSSQLSERVER, SQLSERVERAGENT, NT SERVICE\, or known service SIDs) but the session has is_user_process=1, suggesting an interactive or application connection rather than an internal engine session. Service accounts logging in interactively could indicate credential reuse or misconfigured connection strings.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions WHERE is_user_process = 1 AND login_name LIKE '%SQL%Service%' AND host_name IS NOT NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: For each flagged session, run `SELECT session_id, login_name, host_name, program_name FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions WHERE is_user_process = 1 AND login_name LIKE N'NT SERVICE\%'` to identify the application. Reconfigure the application to use a dedicated, least-privilege SQL login instead of the service account. Apply Windows login auditing to detect reuse.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no user-process sessions are running under SQL Server service account principals. Returns FAIL when one or more interactive sessions are found using service account identities.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Accounts

Name: SQL Login Passwords Older Than 365 Days - Annual Rotation Threshold (NIST IA-5)
Description: FAILs when any enabled SQL login has not had its password changed in over 365 days (uses LOGINPROPERTY PasswordLastSetTime). Uses the annual rotation threshold — distinct from NEW_014 (90-day threshold + expiry flag test) and NEW_041 (90-day dormancy / deprovisioning framing). This check is appropriate for audits targeting the NIST IA-5 annual rotation baseline. Long-lived passwords on SQL logins look like a failure of authenticator lifecycle management under NIST IA-5 and are a common finding in PCI-DSS v4.0 audits for cardholder data environment credentials.
