SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE DATEDIFF(dd, createdate, GETDATE()) > 365 AND is_policy_checked = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Identify stale passwords with: `SELECT name, LOGINPROPERTY(name, 'PasswordLastSetTime') AS last_set, DATEDIFF(DAY, CAST(LOGINPROPERTY(name, 'PasswordLastSetTime') AS DATETIME), GETDATE()) AS days_old FROM sys.sql_logins WHERE is_disabled=0 ORDER BY days_old DESC`. Rotate each password using `ALTER LOGIN [<name>] WITH PASSWORD = '<new_password>'`. For service accounts, update the connection string in the relevant applications before or immediately after the rotation.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all enabled SQL logins have had passwords set within the last 365 days. Returns FAIL when one or more logins have passwords unchanged for more than a year.

Name: SQL Server Certificates Expiring Within 30 Days Should Be Renewed
Description: Checks sys.certificates for any non-system certificates expiring within 30 days or already expired. Expired certificates used for Service Broker authentication, Database Mirroring, or encrypted backup keys could cause service interruptions or leave encrypted data inaccessible. This check surfaces near-expiry certificates so rotation can be planned before the deadline.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.certificates WHERE expiry_date BETWEEN GETDATE() AND DATEADD(dd, 30, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Identify expiring certificates with: `SELECT name, expiry_date, subject, DATEDIFF(DAY, GETDATE(), expiry_date) AS days_remaining FROM sys.certificates WHERE expiry_date < DATEADD(DAY, 30, GETDATE()) AND name NOT LIKE '##%'`. Create a replacement certificate, re-bind any endpoints or keys to it, then drop the old certificate after confirming nothing depends on it. Follow the Microsoft guidance for your specific use case (Service Broker, mirroring, or backup encryption).
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no user certificates are expiring within 30 days or already expired. Returns FAIL when one or more certificates are within the 30-day expiry window.

Name: SA Account Must Be Both Renamed AND Disabled - SID-Based Verification
Description: Tracks the sa account by SID 0x01 (not by name) to verify BOTH conditions: it has been renamed away from 'sa' AND it is disabled. This is the strictest of the three sa checks. Distinct from NEW_012 (tests is_disabled only, by name) and NEW_043 (tests disabled-or-renamed via name lookup). This check uses SID 0x01 so it succeeds even when the account has been renamed to something non-obvious. Leaving the sa account at its default name makes it a" predictable brute-force target; an attacker with network access to port 1433 already knows this login exists. PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 2.2.2 and CIS SQL Server Benchmark require both renaming and disabling the sa account when SQL authentication is needed.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals WHERE sid = 0x01 AND (name = 'sa' OR is_disabled = 0)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: "Rename the sa account: `ALTER LOGIN [sa] WITH NAME = [<obscure_name>]`.\ \ Then disable it: `ALTER LOGIN [<obscure_name>] DISABLE`. Ensure at least one other\ \ sysadmin login exists before disabling to avoid lockout. Verify: `SELECT name,\ \ is_disabled FROM sys.server_principals WHERE sid = 0x01`."
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when the account with SID 0x01 is both renamed (not "sa") and disabled. Returns FAIL when the account is still named "sa" or is enabled.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure the sa Login Account is Renamed and Disabled

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - sa Account

Name: Guest User Should Be Disabled in All User Databases
Description: Detects user databases where the guest database principal has CONNECT permission, which allows any server-level login to access the database even without an explicit database user mapping. The guest account enabled in user databases looks like a misconfiguration that grants database access to any login on the instance, contrary to least-privilege principles in PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 7 and NIST AC-6.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.database_principals WHERE name = 'guest' AND type = 'S' AND (DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_ID('guest')) IS NOT NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: For each affected database, run: `USE [<database_name>]; REVOKE CONNECT FROM guest;`. Generate the script for all affected DBs with: `SELECT 'USE [' + name + ']; REVOKE CONNECT FROM guest;' FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4`. Note: system databases (master, msdb, model, tempdb) intentionally have guest access and should not be modified.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when guest CONNECT permission is revoked in all user databases. Returns FAIL when one or more user databases have the guest account with CONNECT permission.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Access to System Components is Restricted

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Guest User

Name: SQL Server Default Port 1433 May Be in Use
Description: Checks whether SQL Server has a TCP listener active on port 1433. Port 1433 is the well-known default SQL Server port; using it makes the instance trivially discoverable by port scanners and automated attack tools. PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 2.2.1 recommends changing default ports for services in the cardholder data environment. This check flags instances that look like they have never had the port changed from the factory default.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_connections WHERE local_tcp_port = 1433) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Change the TCP port in SQL Server Configuration Manager: SQL Server Network Configuration > Protocols for <instance> > TCP/IP Properties > IP Addresses tab > IPAll > TCP Port. Set to a non-standard value above 1024 that is not in common scanner lists. Update firewall rules and all application connection strings before restarting the SQL Server service.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no online TCP listeners are active on port 1433. Returns FAIL when one or more listeners are on the default port 1433.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure SQL Server is Not Using Default Port

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Default Port

Name: SQL Server Version Should Not Be End-of-Life (2012 / 2014 / 2016)
Description: Detects SQL Server installations running versions that have reached or are approaching Microsoft end-of-life (EOL), specifically SQL Server 2012 (major version 11, EOL July 2022), 2014 (version 12, EOL July 2024), and 2016 (version 13, EOL July 2026). Running an EOL SQL Server means no further security patches are available; any vulnerability discovered after the EOL date is permanently unpatched. PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 6.3.3 requires that all system components be protected from known vulnerabilities.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_os_windows_info WHERE windows_release <> '10') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Plan an upgrade path to SQL Server 2019 or 2022. Use the Database Experimentation Assistant (DEA) and Query Tuning Advisor to assess compatibility. As an interim measure, isolate EOL instances from internet-accessible networks and apply any available Extended Security Updates. Run `SELECT SERVERPROPERTY('ProductVersion'), SERVERPROPERTY('ProductLevel'), SERVERPROPERTY('Edition')` to confirm the current version prior to planning.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when the SQL Server major version is 14 (2017) or later. Returns FAIL when the instance is running SQL Server 2012, 2014, or 2016 (near EOL).

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Perform Automated Operating System Patch Management
All System Components Protected from Known Vulnerabilities

ValidationSources.source: Microsoft SQL Server End of Support

Name: SQL Server Version Should Not Be End-of-Life
Description: Checks whether the SQL Server version has reached end-of-life support. Unsupported versions receive no security patches, making them non-compliant with every regulatory framework and an automatic auditor finding.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN SERVERPROPERTY('ProductMajorVersion') < 13 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Plan an upgrade to a supported SQL Server version. SQL Server 2012 and older are out of extended support.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when SQL Server version is actively supported (2016+)

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Processes and Mechanisms for Security Vulnerabilities

Name: Sysadmin Role Membership Count Exceeds 3 (ISO/NIST Strict Threshold)
Description: FAILs when sysadmin has more than 3 active non-system members. Uses the strict (ISO 27001 / NIST AC-6) threshold of 3. Distinct from NEW_016 (SOC 2 liberal threshold of 5). Use this check for ISO 27001 and NIST-regulated environments where stricter least-privilege controls are expected. More than 3 sysadmin members on a production instance looks like privilege creep and is a finding under ISO 27001:2022 A.5.15 (access control) and NIST AC-6 (least privilege). The sysadmin role bypasses all permission checks; every unnecessary member represents an unrestricted lateral-movement path.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sys.server_principals p INNER JOIN sys.server_role_members rm ON p.principal_id = rm.member_principal_id WHERE rm.role_principal_id = SUSER_ID('sysadmin') AND p.type IN ('S','U') AND p.name NOT LIKE 'NT %' AND p.name NOT LIKE '##%') > 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: List all sysadmin members with: `SELECT sp.name, sp.type_desc FROM sys.server_role_members srm JOIN sys.server_principals sp ON srm.member_principal_id = sp.principal_id JOIN sys.server_principals r ON srm.role_principal_id = r.principal_id WHERE r.name = 'sysadmin' AND sp.is_disabled = 0`. Remove unnecessary principals with `EXEC sp_dropsrvrolemember N'<login>', N'sysadmin'`. Grant targeted permissions (e.g. dbcreator, alter any login) instead of sysadmin where possible.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when 3 or fewer active non-system logins are sysadmin members. Returns FAIL when more than 3 active logins hold the sysadmin role.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure the sysadmin Role Has Minimal Membership

Name: High-Risk Fixed Server Roles Should Have Minimal Membership
Description: FAILs when any active non-system login is a member of high-risk roles: securityadmin, serveradmin, setupadmin, processadmin, diskadmin, bulkadmin. Any membership triggers FAIL (zero-tolerance). Distinct from NEW_115, which groups by role and fails only when any individual role exceeds 5 members total. Use this check to detect any presence in these roles; use NEW_115 for a count-based review." These roles grant significant OS-adjacent or security-modifying capabilities. For example, securityadmin can grant any server permission and is almost equivalent to sysadmin in practice. Unexpected membership in these roles looks like privilege creep and is reviewed under ISO 27001:2022 A.8.2 and NIST AC-6.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals p INNER JOIN sys.server_role_members rm ON p.principal_id = rm.member_principal_id WHERE rm.role_principal_id IN (3,4,5,6,7,8,9,10) AND p.type IN ('S','U')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: "List all members of high-risk roles with: `SELECT r.name AS role, sp.name\ \ AS login, sp.type_desc FROM sys.server_role_members srm JOIN sys.server_principals\ \ sp ON srm.member_principal_id = sp.principal_id JOIN sys.server_principals r ON\ \ srm.role_principal_id = r.principal_id WHERE r.name IN ('securityadmin','serveradmin','setupadmin','processadmin','diskadmin','bulkadmin')\ \ ORDER BY r.name, sp.name`. Remove unnecessary members with `EXEC sp_dropsrvrolemember\ \ N'<login>', N'<role>'`."
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no active non-system logins are members of the six high-risk fixed server roles. Returns FAIL when any such membership is found.

Name: User Databases Should Be Encrypted with TDE
Description: Checks whether any user databases are present that do not have Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) in a fully encrypted state (encryption_state=3). Databases without TDE store data pages in plaintext on disk; if a backup file or data file is accessed by an attacker or falls outside data centre control, the contents could be read without any further authentication. ISO 27001:2022 A.8.24 and PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 3.5 require encryption of stored cardholder or sensitive data at rest.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE is_encrypted = 0 AND database_id > 4 AND state = 0 AND name NOT IN ('master','model','msdb','tempdb')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Enable TDE on each unencrypted database: first create a database master key if one does not exist in master, create a certificate to protect the DEK, then: `USE [<database>]; CREATE DATABASE ENCRYPTION KEY WITH ALGORITHM = AES_256 ENCRYPTION BY SERVER CERTIFICATE <cert_name>; ALTER DATABASE [<database>] SET ENCRYPTION ON;`. Monitor progress with `SELECT database_id, encryption_state, percent_complete FROM sys.dm_database_encryption_keys`.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all online user databases have a TDE encryption key with encryption_state=3 (ENCRYPTED). Returns FAIL when any user database lacks full TDE encryption.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Primary Account Number Protected with Strong Cryptography

Name: Recent Backups Should Use Backup-Level Encryption
Description: Checks msdb backup history to determine whether recent full backups of user databases used backup-level encryption (encryptor_type IS NOT NULL or is_password_protected=1). A backup file that is not encrypted at the backup level could be restored to any SQL Server instance by an attacker who obtains the file, even if the source database uses TDE. Backup encryption is a separate control from TDE and is required by ISO 27001:2022 A.8.24 for comprehensive data-at-rest protection.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE type = 'D' AND is_copy_only = 0 AND encrypted <> 1 AND backup_finish_date > DATEADD(dd, -7, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Add WITH ENCRYPTION to backup commands: `BACKUP DATABASE [<db>] TO DISK = N'<path>' WITH COMPRESSION, ENCRYPTION (ALGORITHM = AES_256, SERVER CERTIFICATE = <cert_name>)`. Ensure the certificate exists in master: `SELECT * FROM sys.certificates WHERE name = N'<cert_name>'`. Update SQL Agent backup job steps to include the ENCRYPTION clause.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all user databases with full backups in the last 30 days used backup-level encryption (encryptor_type IS NOT NULL). Returns FAIL when any recent full backup lacks backup-level encryption. Returns SKIP when msdb is inaccessible.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Primary Account Number Protected with Strong Cryptography

Name: Symmetric Keys Should Be Rotated Within the Last 365 Days
Description: Identifies user-created symmetric keys in any database whose create_date is older than 365 days. Long-lived symmetric keys increase the blast radius if the key material is ever exposed; regular rotation limits the decryptable data window. ISO 27001:2022 A.8.24 requires cryptographic key lifecycle management including rotation schedules. This check uses create_date as a proxy for last rotation since key rotation involves creating a new key.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(+ N'SELECT ''' + name + N''', name, create_date, DATEDIFF(DAY, create_date, GETDATE()) ' + N'FROM sys.symmetric_keys ' + N'WHERE symmetric_key_id > 101  -- exclude ##MS_ServiceMasterKey## (id=101) ' + N'  AND create_date < DATEADD(DAY, -365, GETDATE()); ' FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state = 0; EXEC sp_executesql @sql; + ' symmetric key(s) in user databases were created more than 365 days ago and may be overdue for rotation. ' + 'Query sys.symmetric_keys across user databases to ) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Identify stale keys with: `SELECT name, create_date, DATEDIFF(DAY, create_date, GETDATE()) AS days_old FROM sys.symmetric_keys WHERE symmetric_key_id > 101`. For each key requiring rotation: create a new key, re-encrypt the data using the new key, then drop the old key. Consult the application team to identify all encrypted columns before starting rotation.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no user symmetric keys in any user database are older than 365 days. Returns FAIL when one or more symmetric keys exceed the age threshold.

Name: The Public Role Should Not Have Permissions Beyond Defaults in User Databases
Description: Detects user databases where the public database role has been granted explicit permissions beyond the SQL Server default set. Any permission granted to public is inherited by every user, login, and service account in that database. Granting broad permissions to public looks like a shortcut that undermines least-privilege principles and is flagged by CIS SQL Server Benchmark and PCI-DSS v4.0 Requirement 7 audits.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(+ N'SELECT ''' + name + N''', COUNT(*) ' + N'FROM sys.database_permissions dp ' + N'WHERE dp.grantee_principal_id = DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_ID(''public'') ' + N'  AND dp.permission_name NOT IN (''CONNECT'') ' + N'  AND dp.state IN (''G'', ''W''); ' FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state = 0; EXEC sp_executesql @sql; FROM #pub_check WHERE permission_count > 0; + ' user database(s) have explicit permissions granted to the public role beyond CONNECT. ' + 'Query sys.database_permissions WHERE) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: In each affected database, identify public permissions with: `SELECT permission_name, state_desc, class_desc, OBJECT_NAME(major_id) AS object_name FROM sys.database_permissions WHERE grantee_principal_id = DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_ID('public') AND permission_name <> 'CONNECT'`. Revoke each non-default permission: `REVOKE <permission> FROM public;`. For each permission revoked, check whether specific users or roles that legitimately need it have it granted directly.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no user databases have explicit non-CONNECT permissions granted to the public role. Returns FAIL when one or more databases have extra permissions on public.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure Public Role Permissions Are Limited
Access to System Components is Restricted

ValidationSources.source: CIS SQL Server Benchmark - Public Role

Name: Extended Events Session Capturing Failed Logins Is Running (NIST SI-4)
Description: Checks whether at least one Extended Events session is active that captures failed-login events (audit_login_failed). NIST SI-4 calls for real-time monitoring of authentication failures. Without a running XE session or equivalent, credential attacks against SQL Server logins go undetected until an attacker already has access.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_event_sessions WHERE name LIKE '%login%' OR name LIKE '%failed%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: No active Extended Events session is capturing failed logins. Create and start an XE session that includes the audit_login_failed event. Example skeleton: CREATE EVENT SESSION [FailedLogins] ON SERVER ADD EVENT sqlserver.audit_login_failed ADD TARGET package0.ring_buffer WITH (STARTUP_STATE=ON); ALTER EVENT SESSION [FailedLogins] ON SERVER STATE=START;
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one running XE session captures failed-login events. Returns FAIL when no such session is active.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Failures of Critical Security Controls Are Detected and Reported

Name: Default Trace Is Running
Description: Checks whether the SQL Server default trace is currently running. The default trace captures critical DDL changes, security events, and server configuration changes needed for forensic analysis and audit compliance.
RecommendedAction: Enable the default trace with EXEC sp_configure 'default trace enabled', 1; RECONFIGURE. Restart SQL Server if needed.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when the default trace is running

Name: SQL Server Agent Alert Wired for Severity 16+ Errors (NIST SI-4)
Description: Checks whether at least one enabled SQL Server Agent alert targets severity 16 or higher errors in msdb.dbo.sysalerts. Severity 16+ errors indicate user-correctable problems through to fatal hardware errors. Without an alert, DBAs and security teams rely on polling error logs rather than receiving real-time notification, which could delay response to active failures or attacks.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysalerts WHERE severity BETWEEN 16 AND 25 AND enabled = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: No severity 16+ alerts with active notification exist. In SQL Server Agent, create alerts for severity 16 through 25, assigning each to a notification operator. Verify operators are configured in SQL Server Agent > Operators with valid email addresses. Test with: RAISERROR('Test alert', 16, 1) WITH LOG.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one enabled severity-16+ alert has operator notification or a job response. Returns FAIL otherwise.

Name: PAGE_VERIFY CHECKSUM Enabled on All User Databases (NIST SI-7 / ISO A.8.6)
Description: Checks whether all online user databases have PAGE_VERIFY set to CHECKSUM (page_verify_option_desc = 'CHECKSUM' in sys.databases). CHECKSUM is the only option that detects I/O subsystem corruption on every page read. Databases using TORN_PAGE_DETECTION or NONE miss a large class of silent corruption that only surfaces as data loss or wrong query results. NIST SI-7 requires software and information integrity controls; page verification is the SQL Server primitive for storage-layer integrity.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE page_verify_option_desc <> 'CHECKSUM' AND database_id > 4 AND state = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more user databases are not using PAGE_VERIFY CHECKSUM. For each affected database run: ALTER DATABASE [DatabaseName] SET PAGE_VERIFY CHECKSUM WITH NO_WAIT; then schedule DBCC CHECKDB to establish an integrity baseline. Identify affected databases with: SELECT name, page_verify_option_desc FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND page_verify_option_desc <> 'CHECKSUM'.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all online user databases have page_verify_option_desc = 'CHECKSUM'. Returns FAIL when any online user database uses a weaker setting.

Name: No Suspect Pages Recorded in msdb.dbo.suspect_pages (NIST SI-7)
Description: Checks whether msdb.dbo.suspect_pages contains any rows with event_type in (1, 2, 3) -- these codes indicate 823/824 errors and bad checksums, meaning SQL Server has recorded actual page-level corruption. The table is populated automatically when CHECKSUM errors or torn-page errors occur. Any row here could indicate active data corruption that has not been investigated.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WHERE database_id > 4 AND last_update_date >= DATEADD(d, -30, GETDATE()) AND event_type IN (1,2,3,4)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Suspect page records indicate page corruption events. Run DBCC CHECKDB ([DatabaseName]) WITH NO_INFOMSGS on each affected database. Identify affected databases with: SELECT DISTINCT DB_NAME(database_id) AS db, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WHERE event_type IN (1,2,3) GROUP BY database_id. After investigation, update event_type = 7 for repaired pages or restore from backup.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when suspect_pages has no rows with event_type in (1,2,3). Returns FAIL when any active corruption records exist.

Name: db_owner Role Membership Audit Across All User Databases (PCI-DSS 7.2)
Description: Counts the total number of principals (excluding dbo and system accounts) who are members of the db_owner role across all online user databases. db_owner membership grants unrestricted control over a database including the ability to drop tables, disable audits, and impersonate other users. PCI-DSS 7.2 requires that access to system components is restricted to least privilege; db_owner is rarely needed for normal application or analyst accounts.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE' AND is_read_only = 0; OPEN db_cursor; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cursor INTO @db; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 SELECT DB_NAME() AS db_name, m.name AS principal_name, m.type_desc AS principal_type FROM sys.database_role_members rm JOIN sys.database_principals r ON rm.role_principal_id = r.principal_id JOIN sys.database_principals m ON rm.member_principal_id = m.principal_id WHERE r.name = ''db_owner'' AND m.name NOT IN (''dbo'', ') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more principals hold db_owner across user databases. Identify them with: SELECT DB_NAME() AS db, m.name, m.type_desc FROM sys.database_role_members rm JOIN sys.database_principals r ON rm.role_principal_id=r.principal_id JOIN sys.database_principals m ON rm.member_principal_id=m.principal_id WHERE r.name='db_owner' AND m.name NOT IN ('dbo','sys'). Remove with: ALTER ROLE db_owner DROP MEMBER [principal];
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no non-system principals are db_owner members across all user databases. Returns FAIL when any non-system principals hold db_owner.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Access to System Components and Data Is Appropriately Defined
Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Admin Accounts

Name: CONTROL SERVER Permission Not Granted Outside sysadmin (PCI-DSS 7.2)
Description: Checks whether any server principal outside the sysadmin fixed server role has been explicitly granted CONTROL SERVER permission via sys.server_permissions. CONTROL SERVER is effectively equivalent to sysadmin and allows a principal to perform any server-level action. Explicit CONTROL SERVER grants are unusual, difficult to audit compared to role membership, and could represent a privilege escalation backdoor. PCI-DSS 7.2 requires that such unrestricted access is tightly controlled and documented.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(FROM sys.server_permissions sp JOIN sys.server_principals pr ON sp.grantee_principal_id = pr.principal_id WHERE sp.permission_name = 'CONTROL SERVER' AND sp.state IN ('G', 'W') AND pr.is_disabled = 0 AND pr.name NOT LIKE '##%' AND pr.principal_id NOT IN ( SELECT member_principal_id FROM sys.server_role_members WHERE role_principal_id = SUSER_ID('sysadmin') );) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more principals outside sysadmin hold CONTROL SERVER. Identify them with: SELECT pr.name, sp.state_desc FROM sys.server_permissions sp JOIN sys.server_principals pr ON sp.grantee_principal_id=pr.principal_id WHERE sp.permission_name='CONTROL SERVER' AND sp.state IN ('G','W'). Revoke with: REVOKE CONTROL SERVER FROM [principal];
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no enabled non-sysadmin principals hold explicit CONTROL SERVER grants. Returns FAIL when such grants exist.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Access to System Components and Data Is Appropriately Defined
Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Admin Accounts

Name: Any Enabled Server-Level DDL Trigger Exists - Broad Change-Capture Check (PCI-DSS 11.5)
Description: FAILs when no enabled server-level DDL trigger exists (sys.server_triggers WHERE is_disabled=0). Broad check — any trigger counts. Distinct from NEW_125, which specifically filters to parent_class=100 and excludes MS-shipped triggers, targeting the narrower workforce-authorisation use case. Use this check to confirm change-detection mechanism presence at any level. Server DDL triggers can capture CREATE/ALTER/DROP LOGIN, CREATE/ALTER/DROP PROCEDURE, and other schema change events before they complete. PCI-DSS 11.5 requires change-detection mechanisms that alert personnel to unauthorized modification of critical system files and configuration. A server DDL trigger is a SQL-native mechanism for this at the instance level.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_triggers WHERE is_disabled = 0 AND is_ms_shipped = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: No enabled server-level DDL triggers exist. Create a trigger: CREATE TRIGGER [trg_AuditLoginChanges] ON ALL SERVER FOR DDL_SERVER_SECURITY_EVENTS AS BEGIN INSERT INTO AuditDB.dbo.DDLLog SELECT GETDATE(), ORIGINAL_LOGIN(), EVENTDATA(); END; Review whether existing SQL Audit specifications already cover this gap before adding a trigger.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one enabled server-level DDL trigger exists. Returns FAIL when no such trigger is active.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Network Intrusions and Unexpected File Changes Are Detected and Responded To

Name: Default Trace Captures Schema Change Events
Description: Checks whether the default trace is capturing schema change events (EventClass 164). Without schema change auditing, unauthorized DDL modifications can go undetected for months.
RecommendedAction: Verify default trace is running. For more granular auditing, create a server audit specification capturing SCHEMA_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when the default trace is running and captures schema changes

Name: Blocked Process Threshold Configured for Locking Visibility (ISO A.8.16)
Description: Checks whether the 'blocked process threshold (s)' server configuration is set to a non-zero value (sys.configurations). When this value is 0 (the default), SQL Server never generates blocked process reports, and deadlock-adjacent locking contention goes invisible to monitoring tools. ISO 27001:2022 A.8.16 (Monitoring Activities) requires that performance and availability issues that could signal attacks or misuse are observable. A blocked process threshold of 5-30 seconds is the standard recommendation.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'blocked process threshold (s)' AND value_in_use > 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Blocked process threshold is 0. Enable it: EXEC sp_configure 'show advanced options', 1; RECONFIGURE; EXEC sp_configure 'blocked process threshold (s)', 15; RECONFIGURE; No restart required. Verify: SELECT name, value_in_use FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'blocked process threshold (s)';
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when blocked process threshold is > 0. Returns FAIL when threshold is 0.

Name: system_health Extended Events Session Is Running (ISO A.8.16 / NIST SI-4)
Description: Checks whether the built-in system_health Extended Events session is active (sys.dm_xe_sessions WHERE name = 'system_health'). system_health is SQL Server's always-on diagnostic session; it captures deadlock graphs, blocked process reports, scheduler health, memory broker data, and security errors. If it has been stopped or dropped, a primary source of operational and security telemetry is lost. ISO A.8.16 and NIST SI-4 require that monitoring mechanisms remain continuously active.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_xe_sessions WHERE name = 'system_health') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: system_health is not running. If defined: ALTER EVENT SESSION [system_health] ON SERVER STATE = START; If dropped, restore the session definition from a reference instance or Microsoft documentation for your SQL Server version. This session is recreated at setup and should not normally be absent.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when system_health appears in sys.dm_xe_sessions. Returns FAIL when system_health is stopped or dropped.

Name: SQL Server Audit Is Enabled at the Server Level (HIPAA 164.312(b))
Description: Checks whether at least one SQL Server Audit object exists in sys.server_audits with is_state_enabled = 1. SQL Server Audit is the authoritative, tamper-evident audit mechanism for HIPAA 164.312(b) (Audit Controls), which requires activity recording on systems containing PHI. Without a server-level audit, login events, privilege changes, and data access events are not logged in a format meeting HIPAA audit evidence requirements. The Login Auditing property in SQL Server Configuration Manager is not a substitute.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_audits WHERE is_state_enabled = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: No enabled SQL Server Audit found. Create a server audit: CREATE SERVER AUDIT [HIPAA_Audit] TO FILE (FILEPATH = 'D:\SQLAudit\') WITH (ON_FAILURE = SHUTDOWN); ALTER SERVER AUDIT [HIPAA_Audit] WITH (STATE = ON); Then create audit specifications for the required action groups.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one server audit is enabled. Returns FAIL when no enabled server audit exists.

Name: Required HIPAA Audit Action Groups Covered in Server Audit Specification (HIPAA 164.312(b))
Description: Checks whether an enabled server audit specification covers the four action groups required for HIPAA audit evidence -- FAILED_LOGIN_GROUP, SUCCESSFUL_LOGIN_GROUP, AUDIT_CHANGE_GROUP, and DATABASE_OBJECT_ACCESS_GROUP. A server audit object can exist (see NEW_082) but capture nothing if no specification with the required action groups is linked to it. This check validates coverage, not just presence.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_audit_specifications WHERE audit_action_id IN ('LGIF','LGO','MDSL','LGPM','DATG','DLG','DUG','DCHG','SCHEMA_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP','DATABASE_OBJECT_CHANGE_GROUP','DATABASE_PRINCIPAL_CHANGE_GROUP','SERVER_ROLE_MEMBER_CHANGE_GROUP') AND is_state_enabled = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more required action groups are not covered. Identify missing groups with: SELECT action_name FROM (VALUES ('FAILED_LOGIN_GROUP'),('SUCCESSFUL_LOGIN_GROUP'),('AUDIT_CHANGE_GROUP'),('DATABASE_OBJECT_ACCESS_GROUP')) r(action_name) WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_audit_specification_details d JOIN sys.server_audit_specifications s ON d.server_specification_id=s.server_specification_id WHERE s.is_state_enabled=1 AND d.audit_action_name=r.action_name). Add missing groups to an enabled specification.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all 4 required action groups are covered. Returns FAIL when any are missing. Returns SKIP when no server audit is enabled.

Name: Temporal Tables Present on User Databases for Change History (HIPAA 164.312(c)(1))
Description: Checks whether any user database has at least one system-versioned temporal table (sys.tables WHERE temporal_type = 2). Temporal tables provide an automatic, SQL-standard mechanism for capturing the full history of row changes including who changed what and when. HIPAA 164.312(c)(1) requires integrity controls that protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction; temporal tables are a direct SQL Server mechanism for row-level change integrity. Absence of any temporal table across all user databases could indicate this integrity mechanism is not in use.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE'; OPEN db_cursor; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cursor INTO @db; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 SELECT DB_NAME() AS db_name, COUNT(*) AS temporal_count FROM sys.tables WHERE temporal_type = 2;'; INSERT INTO @result_table EXEC sp_executesql @sql; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cursor INTO @db; CLOSE db_cursor; DEALLOCATE db_cursor; IF ISNULL(@total_temporal, 0) > 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: No temporal tables found. If ePHI tables exist, add system versioning to capture change history. Alternatively verify that CDC (sys.databases.is_cdc_enabled) or application-level audit tables cover PHI integrity requirements. Temporal tables are the lowest-friction option for new tables.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one temporal table exists across user databases. Returns FAIL when no temporal tables exist.

Name: Change Data Capture Enabled Where Required (HIPAA 164.312(c)(1))
Description: Checks whether Change Data Capture (CDC) is enabled on at least one user database (sys.databases.is_cdc_enabled = 1). CDC captures DML changes at the table level and stores them in change tables for downstream consumption. For environments without temporal tables, CDC is a common alternative for satisfying HIPAA 164.312(c)(1) integrity requirements on ePHI tables. If neither CDC nor temporal tables are in use, the instance could lack a row-level change-history mechanism for PHI.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND is_cdc_enabled = 1 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE'; SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE'; OPEN db_cursor; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cursor INTO @db; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 EXEC sp_executesql @sql, N'@cnt INT OUTPUT', @cnt = @t OUTPUT; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cursor INTO @db; CLOSE db_cursor; DEALLOCATE db_cursor; + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @temporal_count) + ' temporal table(s) found. At least one row-level change-history mechanism l) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Neither CDC nor temporal tables are in use. For CDC: EXEC sys.sp_cdc_enable_db in the target database, then enable individual ePHI tables. For temporal tables: alter each PHI table to add system versioning. Choose based on whether you need real-time change feeds (CDC) or point-in-time query (temporal).
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one user database has CDC enabled OR at least one temporal table exists. Returns FAIL when neither mechanism is present.

Name: All Active Connections Use Encryption (HIPAA 164.312(e)(1))
Description: Checks whether any currently active connection in sys.dm_exec_connections is using encrypt_option = 'FALSE'. HIPAA 164.312(e)(1) requires transmission security controls for ePHI; unencrypted SQL Server connections transmit all queries and results in plaintext. This check specifically targets non-sa connections (client applications) since the local Windows admin DAC may appear unencrypted by design. Any application connection that is not encrypted could be exfiltrating PHI in cleartext.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_connections WHERE encrypt_option = 'FALSE') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: "Active unencrypted connections found. Enable Force Encryption in SQL\ \ Server Configuration Manager: SQL Server Network Configuration > Protocols for\ \ [instance] > Properties > Certificate: "select a trusted cert > Force Encryption:\" \ Yes. Restart SQL Server. Review application connection strings to add Encrypt=True."
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no active user connections have encrypt_option = 'FALSE'. Returns FAIL when any unencrypted user connections are active.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Strong Cryptography for Transmission of Cardholder Data

Name: Availability Group Endpoint Uses AES Encryption (HIPAA 164.312(e)(1))
Description: FAILs when AG/mirroring endpoints use RC4, RC4_128, or NONE encryption algorithms (not AES-128/AES-256). Algorithm-strength check — not just encryption-on/off. Distinct from NEW_002, which only checks whether encryption is enabled at all. Use this check as a follow-on to NEW_002 to verify the algorithm quality for HIPAA transmission security compliance. AG endpoints carry log record streams between replicas; if they use RC4, RC4_128, or NONE, the database replication stream could be intercepted on the network. HIPAA 164.312(e)(1) requires that ePHI transmitted over open networks is protected; AG mirroring traffic qualifies if the replicas communicate across routable network segments.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states WHERE role_desc = 'PRIMARY' AND connected_state_desc = 'CONNECTED') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: One or more AG/mirroring endpoints use weak or no encryption. Change each affected endpoint: ALTER ENDPOINT [EndpointName] FOR DATABASE_MIRRORING (ENCRYPTION = REQUIRED ALGORITHM AES); Coordinate with the partner replica. Verify with: SELECT name, encryption_algorithm_desc FROM sys.database_mirroring_endpoints;
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all mirroring/AG endpoints use AES (algorithm 2 or 4). Returns FAIL when any use RC4 or NONE. Returns SKIP when no endpoints exist.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Strong Cryptography for Transmission of Cardholder Data

Name: Logins Created in the Last 30 Days (NIST AC-2 Account Management)
Description: Identifies server-level logins created within the last 30 days via sys.server_principals.create_date. A burst of new logins -- or logins created outside a change-management window -- could indicate unauthorized provisioning or credential stuffing preparation. NIST AC-2 requires organisations to track account creation events and review them periodically.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals WHERE type IN ('S','U') AND DATEDIFF(dd, create_date, GETDATE()) < 30 AND name NOT LIKE '##%' AND name NOT LIKE 'NT %') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Review recent logins with: SELECT name, type_desc, create_date, is_disabled FROM sys.server_principals WHERE type IN ('S','U','G') AND create_date >= DATEADD(DAY,-30,GETDATE()) AND name NOT LIKE '##%' ORDER BY create_date DESC; Disable unexpected logins with ALTER LOGIN [name] DISABLE; and drop after confirmation: DROP LOGIN [name];
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no new logins were created in the last 30 days. Returns FAIL with a count when recent logins are found.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain an Inventory of Accounts
Ensure That the Server Login Control Is Set Correctly

Name: Disabled Logins Still Present on Instance (NIST AC-2 Account Management)
Description: Counts server-level logins that are marked disabled (is_disabled = 1) but have not been removed from the instance. Disabled logins represent dormant attack surface -- they can be re-enabled by any sysadmin, including a compromised one. NIST AC-2 requires timely removal of accounts that are no longer needed, not merely disabling them.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals WHERE type IN ('S','U') AND is_disabled = 1 AND name NOT LIKE '##%' AND name NOT LIKE 'NT %') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: List disabled logins with: SELECT name, type_desc, create_date, modify_date FROM sys.server_principals WHERE type IN ('S','U','G') AND is_disabled = 1 AND name NOT LIKE '##%' AND name <> 'sa'; Remove confirmed ex-accounts: DROP LOGIN [name];
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no non-sa disabled logins remain on the instance. Returns FAIL with a count when disabled logins are found.

Name: Logins Active From More Than 5 Distinct Hosts - High-Threshold Shared Account Signal (NIST AC-2)
Description: FAILs when any login connects simultaneously from more than 5 distinct host_name values. High-threshold (5) heuristic — tuned for environments where load-balanced apps legitimately connect from 2-4 hosts. Distinct from NEW_051, which uses a threshold of 2 and is more sensitive but noisier. A single login used from many machines is a strong indicator of a shared or generic account, which violates non-repudiation requirements under NIST AC-2 and makes individual accountability impossible.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions s GROUP BY s.login_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT s.host_name) > 5) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Identify shared-account logins with: SELECT login_name, COUNT(DISTINCT host_name) AS host_count FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions WHERE is_user_process=1 AND login_name NOT LIKE '##%' GROUP BY login_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT host_name) > 5; Replace shared accounts with individual named logins or properly-scoped service accounts with documented multi-host use.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no login connects from more than 5 distinct hostnames simultaneously. Returns FAIL with a count when shared-account patterns are detected.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: All Users Are Assigned a Unique ID

Name: Non-DBA Logins with CONTROL SERVER or IMPERSONATE ANY LOGIN (NIST AC-6)
Description: Detects enabled server principals that hold CONTROL SERVER or IMPERSONATE ANY LOGIN permission explicitly via sys.server_permissions, excluding members of the sysadmin fixed server role. Both permissions are functionally equivalent to unrestricted server access and should only be held by acknowledged DBAs, not application or service accounts. NIST AC-6 requires that principals receive only the minimum permissions necessary for their function.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT p.name FROM sys.server_principals p WHERE p.type IN ('S','U') AND p.name NOT LIKE '##%' AND p.name NOT LIKE 'NT %' AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_permissions perm WHERE perm.grantee_principal_id = p.principal_id AND perm.permission_name IN ('CONTROL SERVER','IMPERSONATE ANY LOGIN')) AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_role_members rm WHERE rm.member_principal_id = p.principal_id AND rm.role_principal_id = SUSER_ID('sysadmin'))) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Identify and revoke with: REVOKE CONTROL SERVER FROM [principal]; and REVOKE IMPERSONATE ANY LOGIN FROM [principal]; Replace with the least-privilege server role appropriate to the account's function.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no enabled non-sysadmin principal holds CONTROL SERVER or IMPERSONATE ANY LOGIN. Returns FAIL with a count when such grants exist.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Admin Accounts
Access to System Components and Data Is Appropriately Defined

Name: Application Logins Holding db_owner Role Across User Databases (NIST AC-6)
Description: Iterates all user databases and identifies database users mapped to server logins that hold the db_owner fixed database role via sys.database_role_members. Application service accounts holding db_owner can drop tables, truncate data, and execute any stored procedure -- permissions that are rarely required for normal application operation. NIST AC-6 requires restricting access to the minimum necessary to perform the job function.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE' AND is_read_only = 0; OPEN db_cur; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @db; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 INSERT INTO #db_owner_hits SELECT DB_NAME(), dp.name, dp.default_schema_name FROM sys.database_role_members drm JOIN sys.database_principals dp ON drm.member_principal_id = dp.principal_id JOIN sys.database_principals role_dp ON drm.role_principal_id = role_dp.principal_id WHERE role_dp.name = ''db_owner'' AND dp.type IN (''S') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: In each affected database: USE [dbname]; EXEC sp_droprolemember 'db_owner', '[user]'; Then grant minimum required roles such as db_datareader, db_datawriter, or schema-level EXECUTE permissions instead.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no non-system users hold db_owner across user databases. Returns FAIL with a count when such memberships are found.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure the Public Role in the Master Database Is Not Granted Access
Access to System Components and Data Is Appropriately Defined

Name: Separation of Duties - Single Login Holds sysadmin and db_owner (NIST AC-5)
Description: Identifies logins that are members of the sysadmin fixed server role AND also hold db_owner in at least one user database. Combining these two roles in a single account eliminates the separation of duties that prevents a single compromised or malicious account from both controlling the server and owning database objects. NIST AC-5 requires that duties be separated to reduce the risk of error or fraud.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE'; OPEN db_cur; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @db; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 INSERT INTO #sysadmin_dbowner SELECT sp.name, DB_NAME() FROM sys.database_role_members drm JOIN sys.database_principals dp ON drm.member_principal_id = dp.principal_id JOIN sys.database_principals role_dp ON drm.role_principal_id = role_dp.principal_id JOIN sys.server_principals sp ON dp.sid = sp.sid JOIN sys.server_role_members srm ON sp.principa) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Remove explicit db_owner for sysadmin members: USE [dbname]; EXEC sp_droprolemember 'db_owner', '[login]'; Review sysadmin membership and reduce to personnel who genuinely require server-level control. Use separate named accounts for server vs database administration where possible.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no enabled login holds both sysadmin and explicit db_owner in any user database. Returns FAIL with a count when such overlap is found.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Admin Accounts
Access to System Components and Data Is Appropriately Defined

Name: Server Audit ON_FAILURE Action Set to FAIL_OPERATION or SHUTDOWN (NIST AU-9)
Description: Checks sys.server_audits for audits where the on_failure action is set to CONTINUE rather than FAIL_OPERATION or SHUTDOWN. When audit writes fail and the server continues silently, attackers can provoke an audit failure (e.g. by filling the audit log destination) to conduct activity undetected. NIST AU-9 requires that audit information is protected and that audit failures cause a defined, observable response.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_audits WHERE on_failure_desc NOT IN ('FAIL_OPERATION','SHUTDOWN')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Update each non-compliant audit: ALTER SERVER AUDIT [AuditName] WITH (STATE = OFF); ALTER SERVER AUDIT [AuditName] WITH (ON_FAILURE = FAIL_OPERATION); ALTER SERVER AUDIT [AuditName] WITH (STATE = ON); List all audits with: SELECT name, on_failure_desc, is_state_enabled FROM sys.server_audits;
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all enabled server audits have ON_FAILURE set to FAIL_OPERATION or SHUTDOWN. Returns FAIL with a count when any audit uses CONTINUE.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure Audit Log Actions Have Been Set Correctly
Audit Log History Is Retained and Available for Analysis

Name: Always Encrypted Column Coverage in User Databases (PCI-DSS v4.0 3.4)
Description: Counts user databases that contain no Always Encrypted columns (sys.columns WHERE encryption_type IS NOT NULL) despite having TDE enabled. TDE protects data at rest on disk but does not protect column values in memory or in query results -- Always Encrypted provides column-level protection that keeps data encrypted even from DBAs. PCI-DSS v4.0 requirement 3.4 requires PAN data to be rendered unreadable wherever stored.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d JOIN sys.dm_database_encryption_keys ek ON d.database_id = ek.database_id WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state_desc = 'ONLINE' AND ek.encryption_state IN (3, 5); -- 3=encrypted, 5=decryption in progress (was encrypted) OPEN db_cur; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @db; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 INSERT INTO #ae_coverage SELECT DB_NAME(), 1, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sys.columns WHERE encryption_type IS NOT NULL)'; EXEC sp_executesql @sql; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @db; C) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Implement Always Encrypted on sensitive columns: use the SQL Server Management Studio Always Encrypted wizard (right-click table > Encrypt Columns) or the Set-SqlColumnEncryption PowerShell cmdlet. Store column master keys in Azure Key Vault or Windows Certificate Store. Document each encrypted column in your data inventory.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all TDE-enabled user databases contain at least one Always Encrypted column. Returns FAIL with a count when TDE-only databases have no column-level encryption.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Primary Account Numbers Are Rendered Unreadable Anywhere They Are Stored

Name: Database Master Key Present in User Databases (PCI-DSS v4.0 3.5)
Description: Checks whether user databases that store sensitive data have a Database Master Key (DMK) configured via sys.symmetric_keys WHERE name = '##MS_DatabaseMasterKey##'. The DMK is the root of the SQL Server encryption hierarchy for a database; without it, column encryption keys, service keys, and certificate-based encryption cannot be created. PCI-DSS v4.0 requirement 3.5 requires that cryptographic keys protecting PAN data are secured.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE' AND is_read_only = 0; OPEN db_cur; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @db; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 INSERT INTO #dmk_status SELECT DB_NAME(), CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.symmetric_keys WHERE name = ''##MS_DatabaseMasterKey##'') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END'; EXEC sp_executesql @sql; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @db; CLOSE db_cur; DEALLOCATE db_cur; DROP TABLE #dmk_status;) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction:
Create a Database Master Key in each affected database: USE [dbname]; CREATE MASTER KEY ENCRYPTION BY PASSWORD = N'<ComplexPassword>';
 Then back it up immediately: BACKUP MASTER KEY TO FILE = N'<secure-path>' ENCRYPTION BY PASSWORD = N'<BackupPassword>'; Store both passwords and the backup file in separate secure locations.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all online read-write user databases have a Database Master Key. Returns FAIL with a count and ratio when any databases lack one.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Primary Account Numbers Are Secured With Cryptography

ValidationSources.source: Microsoft Learn - CREATE MASTER KEY and sys.symmetric_keys

Name: Expired Backup Records Older Than 2 Years in msdb (PCI-DSS v4.0 9.4.5)
Description: Counts backup history rows in msdb.dbo.backupset where backup_finish_date is older than 2 years. Retaining stale backup records beyond their useful retention period inflates media inventory, creates confusion during restore operations, and may indicate that backup media has not been inventoried or destroyed per policy. PCI-DSS v4.0 requirement 9.4.5 requires that media containing account data is inventoried and that destruction is formally tracked.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE backup_finish_date < DATEADD(yy, -2, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Purge old backup history: EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_delete_backuphistory @oldest_date = DATEADD(YEAR, -2, GETDATE()); Schedule this as a monthly SQL Agent job. Before running on production, verify that no restore operations will need records older than 2 years.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no backup history records older than 2 years exist in msdb. Returns FAIL with a count when stale history is found.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Media Containing Account Data Is Inventoried

Name: Backup Files Written to Unrecognised Drive Paths (PCI-DSS v4.0 9.4.5)
Description: Audits msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily.physical_device_name to identify backup files written to drive letters or UNC paths that do not begin with common expected roots (C: "\, D:\, E:\, F:\, or \\). Backups written to removable media," unusual drive letters, or unexpected network shares may indicate unmanaged or undocumented media destinations that cannot be inventoried or destruction-verified. PCI-DSS v4.0 requirement 9.4.5 requires a formal inventory of all media containing account data.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily WHERE physical_device_name NOT LIKE 'C:%' AND physical_device_name NOT LIKE 'D:%' AND physical_device_name NOT LIKE 'E:%' AND physical_device_name NOT LIKE '\\%') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: "Review anomalous paths: SELECT DISTINCT physical_device_name FROM msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily\ \ WHERE device_type=2 AND physical_device_name NOT LIKE '[A-Z]:\\%' AND physical_device_name\ \ NOT LIKE '\\\\%'; Update SQL Agent backup jobs to write to documented, inventoried\ \ destinations and update your PCI media inventory accordingly."
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all disk backup paths use standard local drive letter or UNC path patterns. Returns FAIL with a count when unexpected path patterns are found.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Media Containing Account Data Is Inventoried

Name: Sleeping Sessions Idle Over 8 Hours Detected (NIST AC-11 / AC-12 Session Termination)
Description: Counts sessions in sleeping state with last_request_end_time older than 8 hours. Long-idle sessions hold open connections, may retain transaction context, and represent unattended authenticated channels that NIST AC-11 (Session Lock) and AC-12 (Session Termination) require to be terminated. A single-row PASS/FAIL/SKIP result is returned; FAIL when the count is above zero.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions WHERE status = 'sleeping' AND is_user_process = 1 AND DATEDIFF(hh, last_request_start_time, GETDATE()) > 8) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Identify idle sessions: SELECT session_id, login_name, program_name, host_name, last_request_end_time FROM sys.dm_exec_sessions WHERE status='sleeping' AND last_request_end_time < DATEADD(HOUR,-8,GETDATE()) AND session_id > 50. Kill individual sessions with KILL <session_id>. Long-term fix: configure application-side connection pool timeouts or Resource Governor SESSION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (SQL 2019+).
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no sleeping sessions idle over 8 hours exist. Returns FAIL with count of offending sessions otherwise.

Name: Resource Governor Workload Groups Without Session Timeout Configured (NIST AC-12)
Description: Checks whether Resource Governor is enabled and whether any non-default workload group lacks a SESSION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS value (SQL 2019+). When Resource Governor is enabled but workload groups carry no session timeout, the server relies entirely on client-side connection pool lifetime for session termination -- violating NIST AC-12. SKIP on SQL < 2019 (SESSION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS not available) and when Resource Governor is disabled.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.resource_governor_workload_groups WHERE session_timeout IS NOT NULL AND session_timeout > 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run: SELECT name, session_timeout_sec FROM sys.resource_governor_workload_groups WHERE group_id NOT IN (1,2). For each group with 0/NULL timeout, set a non-zero SESSION_TIMEOUT_SECONDS value and issue ALTER RESOURCE GOVERNOR RECONFIGURE.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all non-default workload groups have a session timeout. Returns FAIL with count of untimed groups. Returns SKIP on SQL < 2019 or RG disabled.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure Latest SQL Server Service Packs and Hotfixes are Installed

Name: Elevated Server Role Membership Count Exceeds Threshold (PCI-DSS v4.0 12.5)
Description: FAILs when any sensitive server role has more than 5 members. Count-based review check — generates per-role member counts and fails when any role exceeds 5. Broader scope than NEW_062 (adds sysadmin and dbcreator). Distinct from NEW_062, which uses zero-tolerance for any membership in the same non-sysadmin roles. Use this check for PCI-DSS 12.5 periodic access reviews. Any role with more than 5 members is flagged as likely over-provisioned. PCI-DSS v4.0 requirement 12.5 calls for periodic review of personnel holding security responsibilities; this check surfaces the raw counts for that review. FAIL when any sensitive role has more than 5 members.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_principals p INNER JOIN sys.server_role_members rm ON p.principal_id = rm.member_principal_id WHERE rm.role_principal_id > 4 AND p.type IN ('S','U')) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Enumerate members: SELECT r.name, m_p.name AS member, m_p.type_desc FROM sys.server_role_members m JOIN sys.server_principals r ON m.role_principal_id=r.principal_id JOIN sys.server_principals m_p ON m.member_principal_id=m_p.principal_id WHERE r.name IN ('sysadmin','securityadmin','serveradmin','setupadmin','processadmin','diskadmin','dbcreator','bulkadmin'). Remove excess members with ALTER SERVER ROLE [role] DROP MEMBER [login].
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no sensitive server role has more than 5 members. Returns FAIL with count of over-threshold roles and role names.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Security Responsibilities are Defined and Managed
Restrict Administrator Privileges to Dedicated Administrator Accounts
Ensure Principle of Least Privilege is Applied

Name: Compliance Classification Extended Properties Present on User Databases (ISO 27001:2022 A.5.31)
Description: Scans all user databases for extended properties named with a 'compliance' or 'classification' prefix on table objects (class=1). ISO 27001:2022 A.5.31 requires that legal, statutory, and regulatory requirements are identified and documented for information assets. Using extended properties on tables is a lightweight way to attach compliance metadata (e.g., PHI, PAN, GDPR-applicable) directly to the schema. FAIL when no user database contains any such extended property, suggesting compliance tagging has not been started. Returns a SKIP if no user databases exist.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE' ) -- Cursor loop: per-DB query for compliance/classification extended properties SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE'; OPEN db_cur; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @dbname; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 + QUOTENAME(@dbname) + N'.sys.extended_properties WHERE class = 1 AND (name LIKE ''compliance%'' OR name LIKE ''classification%'')'; EXEC sp_executesql @sql, N'@cnt_out INT OUTPUT', @cnt_o) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Start compliance tagging by identifying regulated tables and adding extended properties: EXEC sys.sp_addextendedproperty @name=N'compliance_scope', @value=N'PHI', @level0type=N'SCHEMA', @level0name=N'dbo', @level1type=N'TABLE', @level1name=N'<table_name>'. Apply to every table containing regulated data.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one compliance/classification extended property exists across user databases. Returns FAIL when none are found. Returns SKIP when no online user databases exist.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Legal, Statutory, Regulatory and Contractual Requirements
Establish and Maintain a Data Inventory

Name: SQL Server Sensitivity Classifications Present on User Databases (ISO 27001:2022 A.5.31)
Description: Scans all online user databases for rows in sys.sensitivity_classifications (available SQL 2019+). This catalog view stores structured data classification labels (information_type, label, rank) applied via ADD SENSITIVITY CLASSIFICATION. ISO 27001:2022 A.5.31 requires identification and documentation of legal/regulatory requirements on information assets; absence of any sensitivity classification across all user databases is flagged as a gap. SKIP on SQL < 2019 (view not available).
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE' ) SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE'; OPEN db_cur; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @dbname; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 + QUOTENAME(@dbname) + N'.sys.sensitivity_classifications'; EXEC sp_executesql @sql, N'@cnt_out INT OUTPUT', @cnt_out = @cnt OUTPUT; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @dbname; CLOSE db_cur; DEALLOCATE db_cur;) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Run data discovery from SSMS or apply manually: ADD SENSITIVITY CLASSIFICATION TO <schema>.<table>.<column> WITH (LABEL=N'<label>', INFORMATION_TYPE=N'<type>', RANK=<rank>). Repeat for every column holding regulated data.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one sensitivity classification exists across all user databases. Returns FAIL when none found. Returns SKIP on SQL < 2019 or no online user databases.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Legal, Statutory, Regulatory and Contractual Requirements
Establish and Maintain a Data Inventory

Name: Row-Level Security Policies Present Across User Databases (ISO 27001:2022 A.8.3)
Description: Scans all online user databases for enabled row-level security policies (sys.security_policies) with at least one active security predicate (sys.security_predicates). ISO 27001:2022 A.8.3 (Information Access Restriction) requires access to information to be restricted in accordance with the access control policy. RLS is the primary SQL Server mechanism for row-level enforcement. FAIL when no enabled RLS policy exists in any user database. Returns SKIP when no user databases are online.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE' ) SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE'; OPEN db_cur; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @dbname; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 FROM ' + QUOTENAME(@dbname) + N'.sys.security_policies sp WHERE sp.is_enabled = 1 AND EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM ' + QUOTENAME(@dbname) + N'.sys.security_predicates pred WHERE pred.object_id = sp.object_id AND pred.is_enabled = 1 )'; EXEC sp_executesql @sql, N'@cnt_out I) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: If RLS is appropriate, create security predicates on regulated tables. If RLS is intentionally not used (single-tenant, DB-per-user architecture), document the compensating control and suppress this check.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one enabled RLS policy with an active predicate exists. Returns FAIL when none found. Returns SKIP when no online user databases exist.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Define and Maintain Role-Based Access Control
Ensure the Principle of Least Privilege is Used for Database Access

Name: Dynamic Data Masking Deployed on User Database Columns (ISO 27001:2022 A.8.3)
Description: Scans all online user databases for columns with an active dynamic data masking function (sys.masked_columns). ISO 27001:2022 A.8.3 requires information access to be restricted according to the access control policy; DDM is a SQL Server native control that obscures sensitive column data from unauthorised users at query time without schema changes. FAIL when no masked columns exist in any user database.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE' ) SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND state_desc = 'ONLINE'; OPEN db_cur; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @dbname; WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0 FROM ' + QUOTENAME(@dbname) + N'.sys.masked_columns WHERE is_masked = 1'; EXEC sp_executesql @sql, N'@cnt_out INT OUTPUT', @cnt_out = @cnt OUTPUT; FETCH NEXT FROM db_cur INTO @dbname; CLOSE db_cur; DEALLOCATE db_cur;) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Apply masking to sensitive columns: ALTER TABLE <table> ALTER COLUMN <column> ADD MASKED WITH (FUNCTION='<mask_function>()'); Supported functions: default(), email(), partial(n,'<mask>',n), random(low,high). After applying, verify UNMASK is not broadly granted.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one masked column exists across user databases. Returns FAIL when none found. Returns SKIP when no online user databases exist.

Name: Default Trace Startup Setting Enabled (sys.configurations Configuration Check) (CIS 5.x)
Description: Verifies that the 'default trace enabled' server configuration option (sys.configurations) is set to 1 — the startup/configuration check. Distinct from NEW_072, which checks sys.traces at runtime to see if the trace is actually running. A FAIL here means the feature is disabled at the configuration level and will not restart after a service restart. The default trace captures security-relevant events including login failures, server configuration changes, database creation/deletion, and privilege escalation events in a lightweight rolling trace file. CIS SQL Server Benchmark section 5 (Auditing and Logging) recommends this as a baseline audit mechanism that should remain enabled. FAIL on a single finding -- any instance with default trace disabled loses this coverage.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'default trace enabled' AND value_in_use = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Enable with: EXEC sp_configure 'default trace enabled', 1; RECONFIGURE; No restart required. Verify: SELECT value_in_use FROM sys.configurations WHERE name='default trace enabled'.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when default trace is enabled (value_in_use=1). Returns FAIL when disabled.

Name: TRUSTWORTHY OFF on All User Databases (CIS SQL Server Benchmark 6.x)
Description: Counts user databases (database_id > 4) where is_trustworthy_on = 1. TRUSTWORTHY ON allows modules (stored procedures, functions, CLR assemblies) executing in the database to impersonate server-level identities, including sysadmin. CIS SQL Server Benchmark 6.x and the Microsoft baseline both require TRUSTWORTHY to be OFF on all user databases. A single TRUSTWORTHY-ON database is a FAIL.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND is_trustworthy_on = 1; SELECT TOP 10 @names = @names + name + ' ' FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4 AND is_trustworthy_on = 1 ORDER BY name;) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Disable for each offending database: ALTER DATABASE [<db_name>] SET TRUSTWORTHY OFF; Verify: SELECT name, is_trustworthy_on FROM sys.databases WHERE database_id > 4.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no user database has TRUSTWORTHY ON. Returns FAIL with database names when any found.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure TRUSTWORTHY Database Property is set to OFF

Name: CLR Strict Security Enabled (CIS SQL Server Benchmark 6.x)
Description: Verifies that the 'clr strict security' server configuration option is set to 1 (SQL 2017+). When clr strict security is OFF, UNSAFE or EXTERNAL_ACCESS CLR assemblies are not required to hold a certificate or asymmetric key -- any sysadmin can load arbitrary code. CIS SQL Server Benchmark 6.x requires this setting to be ON. A single-fail FAIL is issued if the value is 0 on a supported instance. SKIP on SQL 2016 (option not available).
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'clr strict security' AND value_in_use = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Enable with: EXEC sp_configure 'clr strict security', 1; RECONFIGURE; No restart required. Pre-flight: enumerate UNSAFE assemblies and ensure they are certificate-signed before enabling.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when clr strict security is 1. Returns FAIL when 0. Returns SKIP on SQL < 2017.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure CLR Strict Security is Enabled

Name: Sample Databases Present on Production Instance (CIS SQL Server Benchmark 7.x)
Description: Detects well-known Microsoft sample databases on the instance (AdventureWorks, AdventureWorksDW, WideWorldImporters, WideWorldImportersDW, Northwind, pubs). Sample databases should not exist on production instances -- they contain demo data, example schemas, and stored procedures that expand attack surface and may confuse monitoring. CIS SQL Server Benchmark 7.x recommends their removal. FAIL on any sample database found.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE name IN ('AdventureWorks','AdventureWorksLT','Northwind','pubs') AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Remove each sample database: USE master; DROP DATABASE [<sample_db_name>]. Confirm beforehand with: SELECT name, create_date FROM sys.databases WHERE name IN ('AdventureWorks', 'Northwind', 'pubs', 'WideWorldImporters').
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no known sample databases are found. Returns FAIL with names when any are detected.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure Sample Databases are not Installed on Production

Name: Non-MS-Shipped Server DDL Trigger for LOGIN Events Exists - Workforce Authorisation Check (HIPAA)
Description: FAILs when no enabled non-MS-shipped server DDL trigger exists (parent_class=100, is_ms_shipped=0). Narrow check targeting custom triggers capturing LOGIN lifecycle events. Distinct from NEW_078, which accepts any server DDL trigger including MS-shipped ones. Use this check for HIPAA workforce authorisation evidence where a customer-defined capture mechanism is required. HIPAA 164.308(a)(3)(ii)(B) workforce authorisation requires that login creation, alteration, and deletion events are trackable. A server DDL trigger on CREATE_LOGIN, ALTER_LOGIN, DROP_LOGIN provides a lightweight capture mechanism independent of the SQL Server Audit subsystem. FAIL when no enabled server-level DDL trigger exists.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.server_triggers WHERE type = 'TR_LOGON' AND is_ms_shipped = 0 AND is_disabled = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Create a server DDL trigger for login events (see recommendations). Verify with: SELECT name, is_disabled FROM sys.server_triggers WHERE parent_class=100.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one enabled server-level DDL trigger exists. Returns FAIL when none found.

Name: All User Databases Have a Full Backup Within the Last 24 Hours (NIST CP-9)
Description: Counts user databases that have no completed full (type='D') backup in msdb.dbo.backupset within the past 24 hours. NIST CP-9 (System Backup) requires that backup frequency meets the organisation's defined RPO. A missing daily full backup looks like an unacceptable data-loss exposure for any recovery scenario. System databases (master, model, msdb, tempdb) are excluded; tempdb is never backed up.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 AND d.name <> 'tempdb' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs WHERE bs.database_name = d.name AND bs.type = 'D' AND bs.backup_finish_date > DATEADD(hh, -24, GETDATE()))) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Identify databases missing backups with: SELECT d.name, d.recovery_model_desc FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state_desc = 'ONLINE' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs WHERE bs.database_name = d.name AND bs.type = 'D' AND bs.backup_finish_date > DATEADD(HOUR,-24,GETDATE())); Then investigate SQL Agent job history for the corresponding backup jobs. Ensure a full backup job runs at least daily for each user database. For databases in FULL recovery model, also schedule log backups (see NEW_126b). Enable backup compression (sp_configure 'backup compression default', 1) to reduce backup window duration.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all online user databases have a full backup recorded in msdb within the last 24 hours. Returns FAIL with a count of unprotected databases.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
Environmental Protections, Software, Data Backup Processes

ValidationSources.source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - CP-9 System Backup

Name: FULL Recovery Model Databases Have Log Backups Within the Last Hour (NIST CP-9)
Description: Counts online user databases in FULL recovery model that have no completed transaction log backup (type='L') in msdb.dbo.backupset within the past 60 minutes. NIST CP-9 requires that backup frequency matches the RPO. For FULL-model databases, point-in-time recovery depends entirely on an unbroken log backup chain; a gap looks like data-loss risk proportional to the missing interval.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.recovery_model_desc = 'FULL' AND d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs WHERE bs.database_name = d.name AND bs.type = 'L' AND bs.backup_finish_date > DATEADD(hh, -1, GETDATE()))) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Identify affected databases: SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.database_id>4 AND d.state_desc='ONLINE' AND d.recovery_model_desc='FULL' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs WHERE bs.database_name=d.name AND bs.type='L' AND bs.backup_finish_date > DATEADD(MINUTE,-60,GETDATE())); Schedule or repair the SQL Agent log backup job. A typical maintenance plan calls BACKUP LOG [dbname] TO DISK = '<path>' WITH COMPRESSION, STATS=10 every 15-60 minutes. For FULL-model databases without any log backup plan, consider whether FULL recovery is intentional; if not, switch to SIMPLE (ALTER DATABASE [name] SET RECOVERY SIMPLE).
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all online FULL-model user databases have a log backup in the last 60 minutes, or SKIP when no FULL-model user databases exist. Returns FAIL with a count of affected databases.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
Environmental Protections, Software, Data Backup Processes

ValidationSources.source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - CP-9 System Backup

Name: Backup Compression Default Is Enabled (NIST CP-9)
Description: Checks whether the server-level configuration option 'backup compression default' (sys.configurations) is set to 1. Backup compression reduces backup size by 50-80%, shortens backup window duration, and lowers I/O pressure on storage during backup operations. An uncompressed default looks like a capacity and RTO risk, particularly for large databases. Enterprise and Standard editions on SQL Server 2016+ support backup compression at no additional licence cost.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'backup compression default' AND value_in_use = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Enable backup compression server-wide: EXEC sp_configure 'backup compression default', 1; RECONFIGURE; Verify: SELECT name, value_in_use FROM sys.configurations WHERE name = 'backup compression default'; No service restart is required. Existing backup jobs will automatically use compression unless they explicitly specify WITH NO_COMPRESSION.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when backup compression default value_in_use = 1. Returns FAIL when value_in_use = 0.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
Environmental Protections, Software, Data Backup Processes

ValidationSources.source: Microsoft Learn - sys.configurations backup compression default

Name: Backup Chain LSN Continuity - No Gaps Between Consecutive Full Backups (NIST CP-10)
Description: Detects databases where consecutive full backup entries in msdb.dbo.backupset show a gap in the LSN chain (database_backup_lsn of a later backup does not match the first_lsn of the prior backup set family). A broken LSN chain looks like a point-in-time restore may fail even if individual backup files appear valid. NIST CP-10 requires successful reconstitution; an undetected LSN gap is a silent restore blocker.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT database_name FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE type = 'D' GROUP BY database_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT first_lsn) < COUNT(*)) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: For each database with an LSN gap, take a new full backup immediately to re-establish the chain baseline: BACKUP DATABASE [dbname] TO DISK = '<path>' WITH COMPRESSION, INIT, STATS=10; Then resume incremental and log backups. For FULL-model databases, take a log backup immediately after the full backup to begin a new log chain: BACKUP LOG [dbname] TO DISK = '<path>' WITH COMPRESSION, INIT;
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no LSN chain gaps are detected across consecutive full backups. Returns SKIP when not enough backup history exists for analysis. Returns FAIL with a count of gap instances.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
Environmental Protections, Software, Data Backup Processes

ValidationSources.source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - CP-10 System Recovery and Reconstitution

Name: Availability Group Has at Least One Secondary Replica Configured (NIST CP-7)
Description: Counts Availability Groups on this instance where the total replica count (primary + secondaries) is fewer than 2.
