NIST CP-7 requires an alternate processing site; for SQL Server, an AG with at least one secondary is the primary mechanism. SKIP when no AG feature is detected (IsHadrEnabled=0 or no AG groups found) -- this check is only meaningful when the organisation has deployed AG for HA/DR.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: "For each AG with only one replica, add a secondary: ALTER AVAILABILITY\ \ GROUP [AGName] ADD REPLICA ON N'SecondaryServerName' WITH (ENDPOINT_URL = N'TCP: "//secondaryserver:5022',\" \ AVAILABILITY_MODE = ASYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT, FAILOVER_MODE = MANUAL, SEEDING_MODE\ \ = AUTOMATIC); Verify replica count with: SELECT ag.name, ar.replica_server_name,\ \ ar.availability_mode_desc FROM sys.availability_groups ag JOIN sys.availability_replicas\ \ ar ON ag.group_id=ar.group_id;"
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all AGs have >= 2 replicas. Returns SKIP when no AG feature or no AGs are present. Returns FAIL with a count of under-replicated AGs.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
Environmental Protections, Software, Data Backup Processes

ValidationSources.source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - CP-7 Alternate Processing Site

Name: Log Shipping Secondary Databases Have Recent Copy Activity (NIST CP-7)
Description: Checks msdb.dbo.log_shipping_secondary_databases for entries where last_copied_date is older than 2 hours or NULL, indicating the log shipping copy job has stalled on the secondary. NIST CP-7 alternate-site readiness depends on the secondary being current. A stalled copy means the secondary is falling behind and may not meet RTO. SKIP when log shipping is not configured on this instance.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.log_shipping_secondary_databases WHERE last_copied_date > DATEADD(hh, -1, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Check SQL Agent job status on the secondary for the log shipping copy job: SELECT * FROM msdb.dbo.log_shipping_secondary_databases; Investigate job history: EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_help_jobhistory @job_name = '<LS_Copy_job_name>'; Verify the backup share on the primary is accessible from the secondary. Restart the copy job if it is in a failed state.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all log shipping secondaries have copy activity within 2 hours. Returns SKIP when log shipping is not configured. Returns FAIL with a count of stale secondaries.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
Environmental Protections, Software, Data Backup Processes

ValidationSources.source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - CP-7 Alternate Processing Site

Name: Suspect Pages Table Has Active Corruption Entries in the Last 30 Days (NIST IR-4)
Description: Counts rows in msdb.dbo.suspect_pages where event_type IN (1, 2, 3) and last_update_date is within the last 30 days. Event types 1 (823/824 I/O error), 2 (bad checksum), and 3 (torn page) represent active storage or I/O integrity incidents that require immediate incident response. NIST IR-4 (Incident Handling) requires that incidents are detected and responded to; suspect page entries look like unhandled corruption incidents when not cleared and investigated.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WHERE last_update_date > DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: For each suspect page entry, identify the database with: SELECT DB_NAME(database_id), file_id, page_id, event_type FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WHERE event_type IN (1,2,3); Run DBCC CHECKDB ([database_name]) WITH NO_INFOMSGS to determine scope. If corruption is confirmed, restore from a clean backup. After resolution, update event_type to 5 (repaired) or delete the row: DELETE FROM msdb.dbo.suspect_pages WHERE database_id = <id> AND file_id = <fid> AND page_id = <pid>;
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no suspect page entries with event_type 1/2/3 exist in the last 30 days. Returns FAIL with a count of active corruption entries.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain an Incident Response Process

ValidationSources.source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - IR-4 Incident Handling

Name: SQL Server Agent Alerts Are Configured for Severity 16-25 Errors (NIST IR-4)
Description: Checks whether at least one enabled SQL Server Agent alert (msdb.dbo.sysalerts) exists for each severity level 16 through 25. These severities cover user errors (16-19), fatal errors (20-24), and hardware errors (25) -- the range that includes DBCC CHECKDB integrity failures and storage corruption. NIST IR-4 requires incident detection and notification; without severity-level alerts, critical SQL Server errors may not trigger any operational response.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysalerts WHERE severity BETWEEN 16 AND 25 AND enabled = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Create severity-based alerts via T-SQL (run for each severity 16-25 or use a range approach): EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_add_alert @name = N'Severity 016 - User Error', @severity = 16, @enabled = 1, @delay_between_responses = 60, @notification_message = N'A Severity 16 error has occurred. Investigate immediately.'; Then assign to an operator: EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_add_notification @alert_name = N'Severity 016 - User Error', @operator_name = N'<OperatorName>', @notification_method = 1;
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one enabled severity-based alert for severity 16-25 exists. Returns FAIL when none found.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain an Incident Response Process
Assessment and Decision on Information Security Events

ValidationSources.source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - IR-4 Incident Handling

Name: At Least One Enabled SQL Agent Operator With Email Address Exists (NIST IR-6)
Description: Verifies that at least one enabled SQL Server Agent operator (msdb.dbo.sysoperators) has a non-empty email address. NIST IR-6 (Incident Reporting) requires that security and operational incidents are reported to designated personnel. Without an enabled operator with an email address, SQL Agent alerts cannot deliver notifications; the incident notification chain looks broken regardless of how many alerts are configured.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysoperators WHERE enabled = 1 AND email_address IS NOT NULL AND email_address <> '' AND LEN(email_address) > 5) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Create an operator with a valid email address: EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_add_operator @name = N'DBATeam', @enabled = 1, @email_address = N'dba-team@company.com'; Then associate the operator with the severity alerts from NEW_134. Verify with: SELECT name, enabled, email_address FROM msdb.dbo.sysoperators WHERE enabled=1;
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one enabled operator with an email address exists. Returns FAIL when none found.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain Contact Information for Reporting

ValidationSources.source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - IR-6 Incident Reporting

Name: Database Mail Profile and Account Are Configured (NIST IR-6)
Description: Checks that at least one Database Mail profile exists (msdb.dbo.sysmail_profile) joined to at least one account (msdb.dbo.sysmail_profileaccount + msdb.dbo.sysmail_account). Without a working Database Mail profile, SQL Agent alert notifications cannot be delivered via email regardless of operator or alert configuration. NIST IR-6 incident reporting depends on a functional notification path. A missing mail profile looks like a silent gap in the entire alerting chain.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysmail_profile WHERE name IS NOT NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Enable Database Mail XPs: EXEC sp_configure 'Database Mail XPs', 1; RECONFIGURE; Create a profile and account: EXEC msdb.dbo.sysmail_add_profile_sp @profile_name = 'DBAAlerts'; EXEC msdb.dbo.sysmail_add_account_sp @account_name = 'SMTPRelay', @email_address = 'sqlserver@company.com', @mailserver_name = 'smtp.company.com'; EXEC msdb.dbo.sysmail_add_profileaccount_sp @profile_name = 'DBAAlerts', @account_name = 'SMTPRelay', @sequence_number = 1; Test: EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_send_dbmail @profile_name='DBAAlerts', @recipients='dba@company.com', @subject='Test', @body='Database Mail test';
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one Database Mail profile has at least one associated account. Returns FAIL when no configured profile/account pair found.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain Contact Information for Reporting

ValidationSources.source: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 - IR-6 Incident Reporting

Name: Availability Group Replicas Use Synchronous Commit for RPO-Critical Coverage (ISO 22301 8.4)
Description: Counts Availability Groups where all replicas are configured as ASYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT with no SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT replica present. ISO 22301 section 8.4 (BC Strategy) requires that RPO-critical systems have a continuity strategy that meets the documented RPO. An AG with only asynchronous replicas cannot guarantee zero data loss on failover, which looks like an RPO breach for any database classified as RPO=0. SKIP when no AG feature or no AGs are configured.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.availability_replicas ar INNER JOIN sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states rs ON ar.replica_id = rs.replica_id WHERE ar.availability_mode <> 3 AND rs.role_desc = 'SECONDARY') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Change an existing replica to synchronous commit: ALTER AVAILABILITY GROUP [AGName] MODIFY REPLICA ON N'SecondaryServerName' WITH (AVAILABILITY_MODE = SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT, FAILOVER_MODE = AUTOMATIC); Verify: SELECT replica_server_name, availability_mode_desc, failover_mode_desc FROM sys.availability_replicas WHERE group_id = (SELECT group_id FROM sys.availability_groups WHERE name = 'AGName');
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all AGs have at least one SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT replica. Returns SKIP when no AG feature or no AGs exist. Returns FAIL with a count of all-asynchronous AGs.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Environmental Protections, Software, Data Backup Processes

Name: Availability Group Has Automatic Failover Configured on at Least One Replica (ISO 22301 8.4)
Description: Checks whether each Availability Group has at least one replica with failover_mode_desc='AUTOMATIC'. ISO 22301 section 8.4 (BC Strategy) requires that the organisation's continuity procedures minimise RTO; manual-only failover means a human must intervene before the AG secondary becomes primary. An AG with all replicas set to MANUAL failover looks like an unmet RTO commitment for any service with an automated recovery expectation. SKIP when no AG or HADR not enabled.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.availability_replicas ar INNER JOIN sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states rs ON ar.replica_id = rs.replica_id WHERE ar.failover_mode = 1 AND rs.role_desc = 'SECONDARY') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Change a synchronous replica to automatic failover: ALTER AVAILABILITY GROUP [AGName] MODIFY REPLICA ON N'SecondaryServerName' WITH (FAILOVER_MODE = AUTOMATIC); Note: AUTOMATIC failover requires SYNCHRONOUS_COMMIT mode first. Verify: SELECT replica_server_name, availability_mode_desc, failover_mode_desc FROM sys.availability_replicas WHERE group_id = (SELECT group_id FROM sys.availability_groups WHERE name = 'AGName');
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all AGs have at least one AUTOMATIC failover replica. Returns SKIP when no AG feature or no AGs exist. Returns FAIL with a count of manual-only AGs.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Environmental Protections, Software, Data Backup Processes

Name: Database Files Use Fixed MB Autogrowth, Not Percentage (ISO 22301 8.4.2)
Description: Counts database data and log files in sys.master_files where is_percent_growth=1 (percentage autogrowth is enabled). Percentage autogrowth causes unpredictably large growth events on large databases, leading to extended I/O stalls that inflate RTO during high-activity periods. ISO 22301 section 8.4.2 (RTO) requires that recovery procedures meet defined time targets; uncontrolled file growth events look like an avoidable RTO risk that should be eliminated through fixed-size growth configuration.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.master_files WHERE is_percent_growth = 1 AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Change affected files to fixed-size autogrowth. For each file identified: ALTER DATABASE [DBName] MODIFY FILE (NAME = N'<LogicalFileName>', FILEGROWTH = 256MB); Repeat for each data file (FILEGROWTH = 512MB is a common default for data files; log files typically use 256MB). Verify with: SELECT DB_NAME(database_id), name, growth, is_percent_growth FROM sys.master_files WHERE is_percent_growth=1;
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no database files (excluding tempdb) use percentage autogrowth. Returns FAIL with a count of percentage-growth files.

Name: Maintenance Plan Execution History Is Present in msdb (ISO 22301 8.4.2)
Description: Checks whether msdb.dbo.sysmaintplan_log contains any rows, indicating that at least one SQL Server Maintenance Plan has executed and recorded results. ISO 22301 section 8.4.2 requires that recovery and maintenance procedures are carried out and their outcomes are documented. An empty maintenance plan log looks like either no maintenance plans are defined or they have never run successfully. SKIP when maintenance plans are intentionally replaced by third-party tooling (Ola Hallengren, etc.) -- this check is thin and primarily useful for shops using native maintenance plans.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysmaintplan_log WHERE succeeded = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Create a maintenance plan via SQL Server Management Studio (Maintenance Plan Wizard) or via SSMS > Management > Maintenance Plans. At minimum, include: (1) DBCC CHECKDB on user databases weekly; (2) index maintenance (rebuild/reorganise) weekly; (3) statistics update weekly. Verify the plan is scheduled and runs successfully by checking: SELECT plan_name, start_time, end_time, succeeded, error_message FROM msdb.dbo.sysmaintplan_log ORDER BY start_time DESC;
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one maintenance plan log row exists in msdb. Returns FAIL when the table is empty. Treat as informational on instances using third-party maintenance tooling.

Name: Production Databases Have Backups Written to More Than One Distinct Destination (ISO A.8.13)
Description: Counts user databases where all backup media families (msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily) for recent full backups point to a single distinct physical_device_name root path. ISO 27001:2022 A.8.13 (Information Backup) requires backup destination diversity to protect against single-point-of-failure media loss. A database backed up exclusively to one network share or one local path looks like it has no redundant backup copy and is exposed to a single storage failure wiping all recovery options.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT database_name FROM msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily bmf INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset bs ON bmf.media_set_id = bs.media_set_id WHERE bs.type = 'D' AND bs.backup_finish_date > DATEADD(dd, -7, GETDATE()) GROUP BY bs.database_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT bmf.physical_device_name) < 2) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Configure a second backup destination for each flagged database. Options: (1) Add a secondary BACKUP ... TO DISK = '<NetworkShare>\<db>.bak' step to the existing backup job; (2) Use Azure Backup or managed backup to Azure Blob as the second copy; (3) Use BACKUP ... TO DISK = '<local>', DISK = '<unc_share>' to stripe across two locations in a single BACKUP statement. Review and update all backup maintenance jobs to write to at least two distinct root locations.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all user databases with recent backups use more than one distinct destination root. Returns FAIL with a count of single-destination databases.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain a Data Recovery Process
Environmental Protections, Software, Data Backup Processes

Name: All Backups Stored on Local or Offsite Storage Per ISO 27001 A.8.13
Description: Verifies that all recent database backups exist on local or designated offsite storage. ISO 27001 A.8.13 requires information backup and documented storage procedures for all critical data assets.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d
  WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 AND d.name <> 'tempdb'
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs
      WHERE bs.database_name = d.name AND bs.type = 'D'
        AND bs.backup_finish_date > DATEADD(dd, -7, GETDATE())
    )
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Schedule full backups for all user databases. Verify backup history weekly via msdb.dbo.backupset.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all databases have recent backups within 7 days

Name: Backup Encryption Present on Offsite/Remote Backup Copies (ISO 27001 A.5.29)
Description: Verifies that recent full backups recorded in msdb.dbo.backupset have an encryptor_type (encryptor_type IS NOT NULL and encryptor_type <> '') indicating native backup encryption was used. ISO 27001:2022 A.5.29 requires that off-site backup copies remain protected during disruption; unencrypted backup media in transit or at rest constitutes a confidentiality risk if physical custody is lost. FAIL when any full backup taken in the last 7 days has NULL or empty encryptor_type. SKIP if no full backups exist in msdb within 7 days.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE encrypted = 1 AND type = 'D' AND backup_finish_date > DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Enable native backup encryption or document the compensating third-party encryption control. Confirm with: SELECT database_name, encryptor_type, backup_finish_date FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE type='D' ORDER BY backup_finish_date DESC.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all recent full backups have encryptor_type populated. Returns SKIP when no recent backups exist. Returns FAIL with count of unencrypted backup sets.

Name: AG Replica Synchronization Health is Healthy For All Replicas
Description: Checks whether all Availability Group replicas are in a healthy synchronization state. Unhealthy replicas mean failover targets are not ready and data loss exposure is increasing with every transaction.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_hadr_availability_replica_states
  WHERE synchronization_health_desc <> 'HEALTHY'
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Resolve synchronization issues on unhealthy replicas. Check network connectivity, disk space, and AG health via the AG dashboard.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all AG replicas are HEALTHY

Name: Database Mail Enabled Accounts Should Be Reviewed
Description: Checks whether Database Mail has any configured accounts that could send email to external destinations. Database Mail with open relay configurations can be exploited for data exfiltration or spam delivery.
RecommendedAction: Review Database Mail accounts and profiles. Disable or restrict accounts that can send to external email domains.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no Database Mail accounts are configured for external delivery

Name: Extended Stored Procedures Should Be Reviewed
Description: Checks for the presence of custom extended stored procedures in master database. XPs run with SQL Server process privileges and represent a high-risk attack surface if not regularly audited.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.all_objects
  WHERE type = 'X' AND is_ms_shipped = 0
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Review custom XPs and migrate to CLR or external scripts if functionality is required. Remove deprecated XPs.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no custom extended stored procedures exist in master

Name: Data Volume Disk Space Headroom At Least 10% (SOC 2 A1.2)
Description: Uses sys.master_files CROSS APPLY sys.dm_os_volume_stats to find every volume hosting a SQL Server database file and checks that available_bytes / total_bytes >= 0.10 (10% free). SOC 2 A1.2 (Capacity Management) requires that the entity monitors and manages capacity to meet availability commitments. A volume below 10% free space risks transaction log growth failures, tempdb exhaustion, checkpoint failures, and potential data corruption if SQL Server cannot complete writes. FAIL when any volume is below 10% free. A single row is returned per the check pattern (overall PASS/FAIL with worst volume detail).
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.master_files mf CROSS APPLY sys.dm_io_virtual_file_stats(DB_ID(), mf.file_id) WHERE mf.size * 8 / 1048576.0 * 0.1 > (mf.size - FILEPROPERTY(DB_NAME(mf.database_id), 'SpaceUsed')) * 8 / 1048576.0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Free disk space on volumes below 10% threshold. Identify large consumers with: SELECT database_id, name, size*8/1024 AS size_mb FROM sys.master_files ORDER BY size DESC.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all volumes have >= 10% free space. Returns FAIL with count of low-space volumes and details of the worst. No SKIP for data volumes.

Name: Restore History Shows DR Rehearsal Within Last 90 Days (SOC 2 A1.3)
Description: Checks msdb.dbo.restorehistory for at least one RESTORE operation in the last 90 days. SOC 2 A1.3 (Recovery Testing) requires that recovery plans are periodically tested to confirm backups are restorable and RTO objectives are achievable. A server with no RESTORE history in 90 days has never demonstrated that its backup strategy works in practice. SKIP if msdb is not accessible. FAIL when no restore record exists within the last 90 days.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.restorehistory WHERE restore_type = 'D' AND restore_date > DATEADD(dd, -90, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Schedule and execute a RESTORE or RESTORE VERIFYONLY within 90 days. Confirm with: SELECT TOP 10 destination_database_name, restore_date, restore_type FROM msdb.dbo.restorehistory ORDER BY restore_date DESC.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one restore exists in the last 90 days. Returns SKIP when msdb is inaccessible. Returns FAIL when no restores found within 90 days.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Establish and Maintain an Isolated Instance of Recovery Data
Review and Test Incident Response Plan

Name: SQL Server Agent Job History Retention Exceeds 30 Days
Description: Checks whether SQL Agent job history retention is set to at least 30 days. Short retention windows mean failed job history is lost before anyone notices, making root cause analysis impossible.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory
  WHERE run_date < CONVERT(VARCHAR(8), DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE()), 112)
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Set SQL Agent history retention via SQL Agent Properties or msdb.dbo.sp_purge_jobhistory with appropriate retention period.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when job history rows exist beyond 30 days

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Ensure SQL Agent job history is retained

Name: At Least 50% of SQL Agent Jobs Have Failure Notifications - Coverage Rate Check (PCI-DSS 12.10)
Description: FAILs when fewer than 50% of enabled non-system jobs have failure notification configured. Coverage-rate threshold — tolerates legacy unconfigured jobs while still flagging systemic gaps. Distinct from NEW_032, which requires 100% coverage (any unconfigured job is a FAIL). Use this check for PCI-DSS 12.10 where the goal is incident response coverage, not absolute completeness. (notify_level_eventlog >= 2 AND/OR notify_email_operator_id IS NOT NULL). PCI-DSS v4.0 12.10 requires an incident response plan to be in place and tested; SQL Agent job failures for backup, maintenance, and monitoring jobs are operational incidents that must surface to on-call staff. FAIL when fewer than 50% of enabled, non-system jobs have any failure notification configured. SKIP if msdb is not accessible or no enabled non-system jobs exist.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs WHERE enabled = 1 AND notify_level_email > 0 AND notify_email_operator_id IS NOT NULL) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Enable notify_level_eventlog=2 or assign notify_email_operator_id for all critical SQL Agent jobs. Verify: SELECT name, notify_level_eventlog, notify_email_operator_id FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobs WHERE enabled=1 ORDER BY name.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when >= 50% of enabled jobs have failure notification configured. Returns SKIP when no jobs or msdb inaccessible. Returns FAIL when < 50% coverage.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Assessment and Decision on Information Security Events
Establish and Maintain an Incident Response Process

Name: Severity-25 Fatal Error Alert Handler Enabled in SQL Agent (PCI-DSS 12.10)
Description: Checks msdb.dbo.sysalerts for a SQL Server Agent alert targeting severity=25 (fatal error in resource, e.g. hardware error, out of memory at server level) with enabled=1. PCI-DSS v4.0 12.10 requires an incident response plan; severity-25 errors represent the most critical SQL Server conditions and must immediately notify operations staff. SKIP if msdb is not accessible. FAIL when no enabled severity-25 alert exists. Also checks severity-19 through 25 alerts collectively as supporting context in the message, but the pass/fail gate is severity=25 specifically.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysalerts WHERE severity = 25 AND enabled = 1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Create enabled severity-25 alert: EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_add_alert @name='Sev 25', @severity=25, @enabled=1; then assign an operator notification via sp_add_notification.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when at least one enabled severity-25 alert exists. Returns SKIP when msdb inaccessible. Returns FAIL when no enabled severity-25 alert found.

FrameworkMappings.control_name: Assessment and Decision on Information Security Events
Establish and Maintain Contact Information for Reporting Security Incidents

Name: SQL Injection Risk — Dynamic SQL with String Concatenation in Stored Procedures
Description: Scans stored procedure bodies for dynamic SQL patterns with string concatenation that are vulnerable to SQL injection. Uses sys.sql_modules to find EXEC or sp_executesql combined with string concatenation operators.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.sql_modules sm
  INNER JOIN sys.procedures p ON sm.object_id = p.object_id
  WHERE (sm.definition LIKE '%sp_executesql%' OR sm.definition LIKE '%EXEC(%' OR sm.definition LIKE '%EXECUTE(%')
    AND (sm.definition LIKE '%+%' OR sm.definition LIKE '%CONCAT%')
    AND p.is_ms_shipped = 0
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Rewrite dynamic SQL using parameterized queries. Use sp_executesql with parameter definitions, not string concatenation.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no stored procedures use dynamic SQL with string concatenation

Name: SQL Injection Risk — xp_cmdshell Referenced in Ad-Hoc Queries
Description: Scans the plan cache for ad-hoc queries referencing xp_cmdshell. This is the #1 post-exploitation pivot technique after SQL injection — attacker runs OS commands through the database engine.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs
  CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_sql_text(qs.sql_handle) st
  WHERE st.text LIKE '%xp_cmdshell%'
    AND qs.creation_time > DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Disable xp_cmdshell. If required, wrap access in a signed stored procedure with EXECUTE AS. Review all queries detected.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no recent queries reference xp_cmdshell

Name: Data Integrity — No DBCC CHECKDB Execution in Last 7 Days
Description: Verifies DBCC CHECKDB has run on all user databases within the last 7 days. DBCC CHECKDB detects corruption from hardware failures, storage driver bugs, and whole-value substitution attacks that modify data pages without detection.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT d.name FROM sys.databases d
  WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 AND d.name <> 'tempdb'
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.sysdbmaintplan_history h
      WHERE h.database_name = d.name
        AND h.activity LIKE '%CHECKDB%'
        AND h.start_time > DATEADD(dd, -7, GETDATE())
    )
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Schedule weekly DBCC CHECKDB via Ola Hallengren's DatabaseIntegrityCheck or a SQL Agent job. Review results for consistency errors.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when all user databases have DBCC CHECKDB run within 7 days

Name: Data Integrity — No Cryptographic Signing of Critical Stored Procedures
Description: Checks whether critical stored procedures are cryptographically signed. Unsigned procedures can be modified by any user with ALTER permission, allowing code substitution attacks that change business logic without detection.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(
  SELECT 1 FROM sys.procedures p
  WHERE p.is_ms_shipped = 0
    AND p.name NOT LIKE 'sp_%'
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM sys.crypt_properties cp
      WHERE cp.major_id = p.object_id
    )
) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Sign critical stored procedures using ADD SIGNATURE with a certificate or asymmetric key. Maintain a list of signed procedures for audit.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when critical stored procedures are cryptographically signed for integrity

Name: High Index Fragmentation
Description: Checks whether any indexes with more than 1,000 pages have fragmentation above 30%. Heavy fragmentation forces SQL Server to do more physical I/O per query - reads slow, CPU climbs, and the problem compounds as data grows. It looks like most environments let this drift for months before anyone notices the query plan cost creeping up.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats(DB_ID(), NULL, NULL, NULL, 'LIMITED') ips
  WHERE ips.avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 30
  AND ips.page_count > 1000
  AND ips.index_id > 0
  ) THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Identify fragmented indexes: SELECT object_name(object_id), index_id, avg_fragmentation_in_percent FROM sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats(DB_ID(), NULL, NULL, NULL, 'LIMITED') WHERE avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 30 AND page_count > 1000. REBUILD indexes above 30%, REORGANIZE between 10-30%. Schedule weekly with Ola Hallengren's maintenance solution.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no indexes have fragmentation above 30% with page count above 1000. Returns INFO when fragmented indexes are found and require scheduled maintenance.

Name: Missing Index Recommendations
Description: Identifies missing index recommendations from SQL Server's query optimizer where estimated impact exceeds 50%. These are indexes the optimizer wanted but couldn't use - queries are doing more work than they need to on every execution. SQL Server has been tracking this since the last restart; it looks like many teams never review it.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM sys.dm_db_missing_index_details mid
  INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_groups mig ON mid.index_handle = mig.index_handle
  INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_group_stats migs ON mig.index_group_handle = migs.group_handle
  WHERE migs.avg_user_impact > 50
  ) THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Pull the full list: SELECT migs.avg_total_user_cost * migs.avg_user_impact AS priority_score, mid.statement, mid.equality_columns, mid.inequality_columns, mid.included_columns FROM sys.dm_db_missing_index_group_stats migs JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_groups mig ON migs.group_handle = mig.index_group_handle JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_details mid ON mig.index_handle = mid.index_handle WHERE migs.avg_user_impact > 50 ORDER BY priority_score DESC. Review and test top suggestions before applying.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no high-impact missing index suggestions exist. Returns INFO when suggestions with avg_user_impact above 50% are found and require review.

Name: Weak Password Policies
Description: Checks if password policy enforcement is disabled for SQL logins
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
  WHEN EXISTS (
  SELECT 1 
  FROM sys.sql_logins 
  WHERE is_policy_checked = 0 
  AND name NOT IN ('sa', '##MS_PolicyEventProcessingLogin##')
  ) THEN 1
  ELSE 0
  END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Weak Password Policies' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Weak Password Policies' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: 3rdParty Backups
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for '3rdParty Backups' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding '3rdParty Backups' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of '3rdParty Backups' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'SQ AgentLogErrors Agent Log Errors' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.

Name: SQ ServerNameCheck Server Name Bios Consistency
Description: This check identifies sq servernamecheck server name bios consistency. This check identifies sq servernamecheck server name bios consistency. From SQLDBA.ORG sp_triage diagnostic procedure.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'SQ ServerNameCheck Server Name Bios Consistency' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'SQ ServerNameCheck Server Name Bios Consistency' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: SQ TCP TCP Listener States
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'SQ TCP TCP Listener States' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'SQ TCP TCP Listener States' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'SQ TCP TCP Listener States' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: TempDB File Count
Description: Checks if TempDB has fewer files than CPU cores (up to 8)
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'TempDB File Count' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'TempDB File Count' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Review Heap Table
Description: Review Heap Table (sp_triage CheckID 1, Priority 100)
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases d LEFT JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset b ON d.name = b.database_name AND b.type = 'D' WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.state = 0 AND (b.backup_finish_date IS NULL OR b.backup_finish_date < DATEADD(DAY, -7, GETDATE()))) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Review Heap Table' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review Heap Table' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Review Log Space
Description: Review Log Space (sp_triage CheckID 2, Priority 100)
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases d WHERE d.database_id > 4 AND d.recovery_model = 1 AND d.state = 0 AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset b WHERE b.database_name = d.name AND b.type = 'L' AND b.backup_finish_date > DATEADD(HOUR, -2, GETDATE()))) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Review Log Space' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review Log Space' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Review Action Statistics
Description: Review Action Statistics (sp_triage CheckID 3, Priority 100)
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE backup_start_date < DATEADD(DAY, -60, GETDATE())) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Review Action Statistics' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review Action Statistics' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Review Missing Index
Description: Review Missing Index (sp_triage CheckID 4, Priority 100)
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_db_missing_index_details mid INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_groups mig ON mid.index_handle = mig.index_handle INNER JOIN sys.dm_db_missing_index_group_stats migs ON mig.index_group_handle = migs.group_handle WHERE (migs.user_seeks + migs.user_scans) * migs.avg_total_user_cost * migs.avg_user_impact > 10000) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Review Missing Index' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review Missing Index' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Review Squeeze Me
Description: Review Squeeze Me (sp_triage CheckID 5, Priority 100)
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats(DB_ID(), NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL) WHERE avg_fragmentation_in_percent > 30 AND page_count > 1000) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Review Squeeze Me' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review Squeeze Me' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Review Compressionstates
Description: Review Compressionstates (sp_triage CheckID 6, Priority 100)
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM sys.databases WHERE page_verify_option_desc != 'CHECKSUM' AND database_id > 4) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Review Compressionstates' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review Compressionstates' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Review Configuration Defaults
Description: Review Configuration Defaults (sp_triage CheckID 7, Priority 100)
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.configurations WHERE value_in_use <> value) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Review Configuration Defaults' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review Configuration Defaults' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Review Db Sps
Description:
Non-compliant configuration for 'Review Db Sps' can lead to unexpected behavior during failover or recovery.
Audit findings will flag this deviation, requiring documented exception or remediation.
Configuration drift increases operational risk and complicates disaster recovery planning.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM sys.procedures WHERE is_ms_shipped = 0) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Review Db Sps' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review Db Sps' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Review Query Stats
Description: Reports the current count of cached query plans from sys.dm_exec_query_stats and flags it for DBA review. A large or rapidly changing plan cache can indicate ad-hoc workloads, plan thrashing, or memory pressure - all worth knowing about. It looks like most teams have never sorted this DMV by CPU cost and are surprised by what's in there.
RecommendedAction: Query sys.dm_exec_query_stats ORDER BY total_worker_time DESC to find the top CPU-consuming queries since last restart. Review execution plans for the top 5. Index or rewrite candidates with high total_worker_time and high execution_count.
ExpectedResult: Always returns INFO with the cached query plan count. DBA reviews the top consumers via follow-up query. No PASS/FAIL - this is a prompt check.

Name: Review SPN Check
Description:
This check validates a key SQL Server configuration or operational state by querying sys.configurations. The setting or condition monitored here directly affects instance behavior, performance, or security posture. Regular validation ensures that drift from approved baselines is detected before it causes incidents.

Without regular validation, configuration drift accumulates silently across SQL Server instances. Changes made for one-time troubleshooting or testing are often never reverted, creating a growing gap between the documented baseline and the actual running state. The check provides an automated detection mechanism that can be integrated into monitoring pipelines, CI/CD deployment validation, and periodic compliance audits. When non-compliant values are detected, the check produces actionable output identifying the specific deviation rather than a generic warning.

The query targets sys.configurations and evaluates the current state against documented best practices. The result column provides a PASS/FAIL/INFO verdict, and the message column identifies which specific items require attention.
RecommendedAction: Investigate the finding 'Review SPN Check' and address the root cause. Consult the recommendations in technical_details for specific remediation steps.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Review SPN Check' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Excessive VLF Count
Description: Checks whether any user database has more than 999 Virtual Log Files in its transaction log. Excessive VLFs slow down database recovery, backup restore, and log scans - the exact moments when speed matters most. It looks like this is caused by repeatedly growing the log in small increments, which most default configurations do without anyone noticing.
SqlQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS(SELECT d.name, COUNT(*) AS VLFs FROM sys.databases d CROSS APPLY sys.dm_db_log_info(DB_ID(d.name)) li WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 AND d.name <> 'tempdb' GROUP BY d.name HAVING COUNT(*) > 999) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
RecommendedAction: Get VLF counts: SELECT d.name, COUNT(*) AS vlf_count FROM sys.databases d CROSS APPLY sys.dm_db_log_info(d.database_id) li WHERE d.state = 0 AND d.database_id > 4 GROUP BY d.name HAVING COUNT(*) > 999 ORDER BY vlf_count DESC. For each database: backup the log, shrink the log file to minimum, then re-grow it in one large increment. Set autogrowth to 512 MB or larger to prevent recurrence.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no user databases have more than 999 VLFs. Returns INFO when high-VLF databases are found and require log file remediation.
